title: Moment topless Bill Belichick caught sneaking from 24-year-old girlfriend's home
meta_description: BILL Belichick was caught sneaking out of a home on a Ring camera last year – and the residence is believed to belong to his current girlfriend. The New England Patriots iconic head coach, 72…
headings: The downsides of having a massive age gap relationship
body_text: BILL Belichick was caught sneaking out of a home on a Ring camera last year – and the residence is believed to belong to his current girlfriend. The New England Patriots iconic head coach, 72, is rumored to be dating 24-year-old former cheerleader Jordon Hudson . It’s been reported by the Daily Mail that Bill Belichick was caught sneaking out of the Massachusetts house of his girlfriend Hudson last November. Ring camera footage spotted the 72-year-old leaving the home without a shirt. Belichick opened and closed the door of the residence before walking down the front porch of the property. TMZ first reported the relationship between him and Hudson last week. Bill Belichick spotted with girlfriend for first time on Boston flight Belichick 'signed girlfriend's homework' during first meetup with love interest Belichick and the ex-cheerleader have been dating since 2022 after the two met on a flight from Boston to Florida in 2021, per the news outlet. It’s believed that the pair spoke about a philosophy project that Hudson had been working on at the time. Belichick and Hudson were pictured in a selfie that was taken during their first experience with each other in February of 2021, TMZ reported. Both smiled in the photo during their first reported encounter on a 2021 flight as Belichick reportedly began a conversation about the book that Hudson was reading . Travis Kelce makes fashion statement at Chiefs ceremony Steelers announce new QB1 with Aaron Rodgers' retirement status still up-in-air College football coach hits back at NFL prospect Ty Simpson over 'bad take' Rob Gronkowski's stunning girlfriend Camille Kostek amazes with Coachella outfit And TMZ also reported the six-time Super Bowl champion signed the then 21-year-old’s Deductive Logic textbook. The message read, “Jordan, thanks for giving me a course on logic! Safe travels! Bill Belichick.” TMZ’s photo of the note showed that Belichick listed the years of his six Super Bowl championships under his signature. Their report revealed that the pair shared contact info after the flight. It’s believed that Belichick and Hudson became a couple after his breakup from Linda Holliday , whom he dated for 16 years . Hudson was a competitive cheerleader at Bridgewater State University in Massachusetts. Belichick was spotted supporting her at a cheerleading competition in March. They also posed for a picture during a work trip to Croatia on a boat. Dr. Jenn Mann, MFT, a licesesed therapist, reveals the key issues experienced by romantic partners with a huge age gap between them. Dr. Mann claimed it might initially feel exciting when one partner is much younger than the other, like Bill Belichick, 72, and Jordon Hudson, 24, who share a whopping 48-year age difference. “When people get older, they might just think it is refreshing that a young woman is looking at them adoringly and is attracted to them and is interested in him,” Dr. Mann told The U.S. Sun. “[But] there is a huge gap in life experience — in just years on this planet, in the way that helps us to evolve, develop, and mature.” “Even the most mature young person, when there is a 40-year age gap, the older people are incredibly immature. “You’re not meeting in the middle regarding development and life experience.” Dr. Mann said one common consideration for older male partners would be the younger female wanting a father figure. “Usually, younger women are drawn to much older men because of unresolved issues with their fathers,” Dr. Mann added. “This could be a lack of attention, love, someone not being there, or even someone being in the household but not being present the way she might wish he was.” Dr. Mann noted that sexual and intimacy issues might begin to be at play, given the older partner may not have as much energy. What’s more, for older men, “it becomes more difficult typically to get and maintain an erection,” she said. “There are problems related to sexual performance, which can create other risks when it comes to taking medication for sexual performance.” Socially, Dr. Mann suggested that issues may arise between a couple when they mix with friends from different generations. “In this case, if they’re hanging with Bill’s friends in their seventies or even sixties and fifties, a 24-year-old woman at that table will likely struggle to find common ground socially,” she said. Last week, Hudson posted a picture of the coach at Tom Brady ‘s Patriots Hall of Fame induction ceremony. “You are the embodiment of virtue, dedication, humility and grace. I am deeply moved by you,” she wrote on an Instagram story post. It’s been rumored that Hudson, who’s also an entrepreneur and philosopher, has helped Belichick with his business endeavors.

title: Can I substitute olive oil for butter/shortening in pie crust?
meta_description: I was making a pie the other day -- a mock apple pie*, to be exact, which is unusual enough. But I came up short on butter and don't keep any shortening stocked in my kitchen. Desperate, I searched
body_text: I was making a pie the other day -- a mock apple pie*, to be exact, which is unusual enough. But I came up short on butter and don't keep any shortening stocked in my kitchen. Desperate, I searched online for any substitutes, including olive oil, and found a small number of hits and recipes . I ended up erring on the safe side and just made a brown sugar crumble topping, but I've been curious about an olive oil crust ever since. Has anyone attempted it? What are some of the differences between olive oil and butter or shortening? What would be the result of using a combination of all the fats? * Mock Apple Pie uses no apples, and is a carboholic's dream. It was for a themed party and I don't intend to make it often. But it's quite a fun surprise to try out on unsuspecting guests at least once! That recipe used to be on the back of every Ritz box. Oil crusts tend to be less flaky than butter or lard crusts. Half butter half oil can be pretty good though. Wayfaring Stranger – Wayfaring Stranger 2018-06-11 22:49:39 +00:00 Commented Jun 11, 2018 at 22:49 The trick to incorporating olive oil into your crust is to freeze it first until it's opaque and congealed, "like the consistency of slightly melted sorbet." From the recipe for an olive oil double crust; it has a "surprisingly neutral taste... [and by freezing it] helps the fat blend into the dough in little pockets, creating the flakiness you crave (Moskowitz, Vegan Pie in the Sky p. 39)" 1 That does sound like a great tip! Thank you. I'm not sure about the neutral taste though...olive oil has a pretty strong flavor IMO, but I usually use grapeseed oil for a neutral flavor anyways. TheSpatulaQueen – TheSpatulaQueen 2012-04-04 02:53:22 +00:00 Commented Apr 4, 2012 at 2:53 1 @TheSpatulaQueen thats what I thought as well, but that's directly quoted from the book, and I have made it with the Boston Cream Pie recipe and it was great. Like she said; ...surprisingly neutral. It probably amounts to a combination of the heat cooking away the taste as it goes from a gel state to 350'F, as well as the flour masking the taste mfg – mfg 2012-04-04 20:27:30 +00:00 Commented Apr 4, 2012 at 20:27 Hot-water or raised-pie doughs, I have had success with olive oil. With your average apple-pie crust (mock or not), the consistency seems to be more fragile in rolling out and shaping: got better results by pressing the dough directly into pan after sprinkling in rather streusel size pieces, if you can picture that. Advantage there is that the dough need not come completely together first and can be played with in the pan until even thickness whereas kneading folding and re-rolling can easily lead to toughness. Recently used up olive oil mayo in a crust. Nice moisture to fat ratio in the mixing and the added tartness was a plus Pat Sommer – Pat Sommer 2018-01-03 16:24:59 +00:00 Commented Jan 3, 2018 at 16:24 I constantly run across statements that it's not possible to make a flaky oil-based crust. However, this is simply not true. My grandma's oil/milk based pie crusts are consistently flakier than any butter/shortening/lard based crust I've ever made or tasted. However, it's important to point out that this bit of wisdom is so common because you cannot simply substitute oil for butter/shortening/lard in a traditional pie crust recipe. A flaky oil based crust requires a different recipe and careful handling of the dough when mixing and rolling. For those interested, give this a try: If you haven't worked with crusts between wax paper before, here's the trick for transferring it to the pie tin: You can further improve the consistency (i.e., flakiness) of the top crust (or bottom crust if you're cooking the pie shell on its own for a pudding or fresh fruit pie) by rubbing a little milk on the surface of the crust and sprinkling a little sugar (or cinnamon/sugar for things like the top crust of an apple pie). I've been using this recipe for 20+ years and friends/guests are always surprised that such a flaky/delicate crust was oil based. Others are surprised at how simple and easy it is to make. It's a great all purpose crust that I use with fruit pies, meat pies, pudding/custard pies, etc. The pie crust itself is great on its own (sprinkle some cinnamon and sugar on it after rubbing it with milk and then cook some "pie crust cookies"). I'm obviously biased because this is the crust that my mom and grandmother always made when I was growing up, but I find the consistency and flavor to be better than typical shortening crusts. (Although, to be fair, my mother-in-law makes an amazing lard/vodka crust and, frankly, some recipes call for a more shortbread-like crust). There is absolutely no problem in making pie crusts with oil instead of butter/shortening. The important thing to remember is that it's not a 1:1 substitution. In my favourite pie crust recipe, which is my own veganization of the Cook's Illustrated vodka pie crust, the substitution is 65 grams of oil for 100 grams of butter, and about a tablespoon of water. Normally, I would use "yellow" oil, one of the canola/soybean/rapeseed neutral oils for this sort of thing. Olive oil has a special flavour which I'm not sure I would want in an apple pie. The ratios would be the same, but olive oil has the flavour, and is more expensive, so I tend to avoid it except for savoury pies. I don't think any sort of oil would work at all for a flaky pâte brisée type of flaky pastry crust. Those kinds of pastry rely on having layers of solid fat separated by dough. As the pastry cooks, the fat melts into the dough but leaves the distinct flakes. Oil might be useful in other types of crust, such as some sort of crumb crust. 1 I don't see why the melting would be a problem. The only thing you have to do is to ensure that some dough builds. After all, you can make a good crust with melted butter. But what I would find problematic is the softness of the dough, it is very hard to work with. rumtscho – rumtscho ♦ 2012-04-02 08:26:31 +00:00 Commented Apr 2, 2012 at 8:26 2 I agree with @caleb, most pastries get their flakiness and fluff from the shortening layers in the pastry melting as it bakes, not before. Some chefs will use multiple types of shortening, each with its own different melting point, in one crust. This adds an especially delicate texture. Often the dry ingredients are chilled beforehand also, to promote the layering process. Derrick – Derrick 2012-04-08 04:51:37 +00:00 Commented Apr 8, 2012 at 4:51 A much better vegan substitute for pie crust is coconut oil, for one thing, it tastes like coconut. Honestly I think I like it better than butter for fruit pies. It is also very nice for curried meat pies. For me, olive oil is more heavy and greasy and not how I want a fruit pie to taste. I would also try almond oil, which for me goes well with both sweet and savory. I do think coconut oil is easier to work with in a crust dough. As others have pointed out, it is not a 1 to 1 sub, since butter is a semi-solid emulsion of air, water, and fat and oil is just liquid fat. 1 Tasting like coconut is a bad thing in my book. It's one thing in a coconut or banana cream pie or something else somewhat tropical but I don't really want my winter pie crust tasting of coconut. Catija – Catija 2017-11-25 20:46:28 +00:00 Commented Nov 25, 2017 at 20:46 Maybe grapeseed oil? It is a very neutral taste. I have never used it because I rather like coconut or almond( or butter since I am not vegan. ) Becks – Becks 2017-11-25 20:54:47 +00:00 Commented Nov 25, 2017 at 20:54 1 I do not use milk in pie crust ever. I use water only and my oil crusts turn out fine. Just a thought, avocado flesh is very buttery and has an otherwise mild flavor. Has anyone tried using avocado as a butter sub in pastry? Since there is water in avocado as well as fat, it might be closer to a 1 to 1 sub. Becks – Becks 2017-11-25 21:37:40 +00:00 Commented Nov 25, 2017 at 21:37 That sounds like a question for the site. Consider asking it. Catija – Catija 2017-11-25 21:38:57 +00:00 Commented Nov 25, 2017 at 21:38 If you make the avocado pie crust please send us a photo, I am very curious what color it might turn out. Lorel C. – Lorel C. 2017-11-26 00:07:19 +00:00 Commented Nov 26, 2017 at 0:07 I come from a olive oil producing country. Every single person that knows how to cook in my family has the following rule: Olive oil for savory recipes. Corn/sunflower oil or butter/margarine for sweet ones. It wasn't until I disobeyed this rule, that I found out why it came to exist in the first place. Let me tell you it wasn't pleasant throwing those doughnuts in the garbage, after all the ingredients and effort I put on them. Olive oil has a distinctive and strong taste that you don't want to overthrow the other components of your sweets. It can get a lot worse if your olive oil is of poor quality, which in my experience is very possible if you don't live in southern Europe (some of my relatives struggled to find olive oil that tasted 'home-like' when they lived abroad). So, when you are making sweet pies opt for unsalted butter /other oils with mild flavor . I agree that oil crust are very flaky, but as others have said, they are delicate. I just tried making a lattice top for the first time, for a mock apple pie (using green tomatoes), and the strips kept tearing. But oil crust work well for double crust pies. You can also refrigerate the dough for the top crust for 10-20 minutes and grate it on a box grater, to make crumble. I have made pie crust cookies using different oils and done side-by-side taste tests. I like peanut oil best, which has a rich flavour, though avocado oil (a bit fruity) is acceptable for fruit pies. Canola oil tastes fishy; I have stopped using it. I have used melted butter and water and compared it to peanut oil and water, and peanut oil and 2% milk. As you can imagine the peanut oil and water had the least flavour; it was also the most crumbly. I find the flavour of peanut oil and 2% milk competes well with butter and water. If you use milk as the liquid in an oil crust you still get the non-fat milk solids (NFMS) that make butter taste good. I did the math and in order to get the same amount of NFMS you should dilute the milk by 1/4 to 1/3, but I usually use straight milk...In my experience oil crusts made with 2% milk are sturdier than those made with whole milk. I have not tried using 1% or skim milk. Note that extra virgin olive oil contains natural mono- and diglycerides, which are emulsifiers. I recently tired using EVOO as the oil in a crust for a quiche; the crust was very soggy. I think the emulsifiers in the EVOO bonded with the liquid in the filling. I won't use EVOO for pies with wet fillings again. Find the answer to your question by asking.

title: @WebMvcTest not running due to missing dependency
meta_description: I want to test my controller by using @WebMvcTest. I @MockBean the dependencies of the controller but when running the test, it fails to start. The application starts correctly when running the main
body_text: I want to test my controller by using @WebMvcTest . I @MockBean the dependencies of the controller but when running the test, it fails to start. The application starts correctly when running the main class. The SoccerService has a dependency on SoccerAnalysisRepository which is a MongoRepository . I don't understand why the SoccerService is created by the test since @Service are not scanned by @WebMvcTest . The application is a Maven multi modules, so I has to explicitly configure the component scanning and repository. 1 Pay attention to documentation, please: docs.spring.io/spring-boot/docs/2.0.3.RELEASE/reference/… Alexander Polozov – Alexander Polozov 2018-07-21 08:47:37 +00:00 Commented Jul 21, 2018 at 8:47 I created a configuration class where I put the ComponentScan and EnableMongoRepositories , so the main class is only annotated with @SpringBootApplication . The MongoTemplate is not required anymore. Create an answer and I'll accept it. Sydney – Sydney 2018-07-21 09:12:08 +00:00 Commented Jul 21, 2018 at 9:12 Move your SpsWebApplication to the com.sps package (as adviced by the Spring Boot developers) and remove all the annotations but the @SpringBootApplication . Spring Boot will then automatically detect any framework needed and your sliced test will also work. M. Deinum – M. Deinum 2018-07-23 06:20:05 +00:00 Commented Jul 23, 2018 at 6:20 You must move all area-specific configuration, like @ComponentScan and @EnableMongoRepositories , to a separate @Configuration file. It's important not to litter the application’s main class with configuration settings that are specific to a particular area of its functionality. More information: https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/docs/2.0.3.RELEASE/reference/htmlsingle/#boot-features-testing-spring-boot-applications-testing-user-configuration Find the answer to your question by asking.

title: Recette gâteau léger aux pépites de chocolat | Supertoinette
meta_description: Gâteau léger aux pépites de chocolat : Découvrez cette recette de gâteau léger aux pépites de chocolat, moelleux, simple à réaliser en 40 minutes. Astuces, variantes sans gluten, et conseils anti-raté inclus !
headings: Gâteau léger aux pépites de chocolat
body_text: Découvrez cette recette de gâteau léger aux pépites de chocolat, moelleux, simple à réaliser en 40 minutes. Astuces, variantes sans gluten, et conseils anti-raté inclus ! Tags: Cuisson au four | Desserts | Pour débutants | Cuisine diététique | Recettes rapides | Sel | Beurre | Chocolat | Farine | Levure chimique | Oeuf | Sucre | Sucre vanillé | Gâteau au chocolat Ce gâteau léger aux pépites de chocolat est parfait pour se faire plaisir sans culpabiliser. Avec une texture moelleuse, un goût chocolaté subtil et une préparation rapide, il convient aussi bien aux goûters qu’aux desserts après le repas. Cette version a été pensée pour être simple, économique, tout en gardant une part gourmande. Battre les oeufs avec le sucre, le sucre vanillé et la pincée de sel jusqu'à ce que le mélange blanchisse. Ajouter le beurre mou et battre pendant 1 minute. Incorporer la farine, la levure , et mélanger à nouveau. Ajouter les pépites de chocolat et mélanger. Verser la préparation dans le moule et enfourner 30 minutes. Laisser refroidir et démouler . Utilisez un mélange farine de riz + fécule de maïs. Réduisez le sucre à 35 g et ajoutez un peu de compote pour conserver le moelleux. Ajoutez des noix, des amandes torréfiées ou des pépites de chocolat blanc. - Remplacez les 4 œufs par 150 g de compote de pomme sans sucre ajouté , 50 ml de yaourt végétal ou crème végétale , et mélangez avec 1 c. à café de vinaigre de cidre + ½ c. à café de bicarbonate pour faire lever la pâte. - Remplacez le beurre par de la margarine ou de l’huile neutre. - Résultat : un gâteau tout aussi moelleux et 100 % végétal. Roulez les pépites dans un peu de farine avant de les ajouter pour éviter qu’elles ne tombent au fond. Oui, c’est parfait pour une version sans lactose ou vegan. Oui, utilisez 50 g de banane bien mûre ou 50 g de tofu soyeux mixé pour un goût plus neutre. 2 à 3 jours à température ambiante dans une boîte hermétique. Quelques astuces utiles avant de commencer une recette avec des oeufs : découvrez quel est le poids d'un oeuf de poule ? Et sachez comment reconnaître la fraîcheur d'un oeuf . Retrouvez également nos différents recettes d'oeufs en fonction des cuissons : Et pour varier les plaisirs sans faire d'écarts, découvrez nos recettes light : Le gâteau au chocolat, vous le préférez moelleux, fondant, mi-cuit, coulant ? On vous propose de tester nos meilleures recettes de gâteaux au chocolat pour trouver votre préféré : Un vin rouge sucré puissant évolué, plutôt simple et boisé, au nez ouvert et à la bouche opulente > Bon accord : Rappu - VDL/VDN rouge (tuilé) > Très bon accord : Yecla - VDL/VDN rouge (tuilé) > Excellent accord : Macvin du jura - VDL/VDN rouge (tuilé) > Accord quasi parfait : Banyuls - VDL/VDN rouge (tuilé) > Accord parfait : Rivesaltes - VDL/VDN rouge (tuilé) *L'alcool est dangereux pour la santé, à consommer avec modération Perdez jusqu'à 7 kg en 1 mois Votre bilan minceur gratuit Piqûres de moustiques : vite une banane ! Les moustiques vous dévorent ! Frottez votre peau avec l'intérieur d'une peau de banane.... Tous droits réservés Supertoinette.com, 2001-2026. title: Carbon Cycle, Photosythesis, and Cell respiration Flashcards
meta_description: Study with Quizlet and memorize flashcards containing terms like Sinks in carbon cycle, Sources in carbon cycle, What enters a leaf and more.
headings: Carbon Cycle, Photosythesis, and Cell respiration
body_text: Carbon Cycle, Photosythesis, and Cell respiration Save Groups Get a hint Sinks in carbon cycle absorbing CO2, so photosynthesis, oceans, forests, and soil 1 / 33 1 / 33 Created by quizlette20529134 Created 1 month ago Terms in this set (33) Sinks in carbon cycle absorbing CO2, so photosynthesis, oceans, forests, and soil Sources in carbon cycle release CO2, so cell respiration and combustion of fossil fuels What enters a leaf water (liquid), Sunlight (energy), and Carbon dioxide What exits a leaf Oxygen, Water (gas), and sugar through the central vein General equation for photosynthesis Carbon dioxide+water (energy)→sugars + oxygen Where do things enter and exit in a leaf Sunlight enter through the top and everything else enters/leaves through the bottom Upper epidermis the top, typically single-layered, transparent surface of a plant leaf that protects against water loss, pathogens, and UV damage Chloroplast specialized, double-membrane organelles in plant and algal cells that function as the primary site of photosynthesis, converting sunlight into chemical energy (glucose) using chlorophyll. Air Space gaps between leaf cells, that facilitate internal gas exchange and water vapor movement Lower epidermis the bottommost, single-layered, protective skin of a leaf, covered by a thin cuticle to prevent water loss Guard cell specialized, paired epidermal cells on plant leaves that regulate gas exchange and transpiration by controlling the opening and closing of stomatal pores Cuticle a protective, hydrophobic, waxy layer covering the epidermis of aerial plant parts. It acts as a barrier against water loss, UV radiation, pathogens, and mechanical injury Palisade mesophyll the primary photosynthetic tissue in plant leaves, located just beneath the upper epidermis. They are tightly packed and contain the most chloroplasts Vein transport water, nutrients, and photosynthetic products, while acting as a structural skeleton Spongy mesophyll loosely shaped, oddly shaped, and facilitate gas exchange via large intercellular air spaces and to conduct photosynthesis. They contain few chloroplasts.

title: Gratis: la Epic Games Store sorprende a los jugadores de PC y regala más de $100 USD en contenido en marzo de 2026
meta_description: Los usuarios ya pueden reclamar los juegos gratuitos en la Epic Games Store, pero los regalos sólo estarán disponibles por tiempo limitado
headings: Gratis: la Epic Games Store sorprende a los jugadores de PC y regala más de $100 USD en contenido en marzo de 2026
La Epic Games Store ofrece estos regalos en marzo de 2026
La Epic Games Store obsequiará Isonzo y Cozy Grove
body_text: Los usuarios ya pueden reclamar los juegos gratuitos en la Epic Games Store, pero los regalos sólo estarán disponibles por tiempo limitado Por Ulises Contreras el 05 de marzo de 2026 La Epic Games Store abrió sus puertas en 2018 y, en su intento de irrumpir en un mercado dominado por Steam , puso en marcha su iniciativa de regalos semanales . Este programa es un éxito y mes a mes logra atraer a nuevos usuarios. Los jugadores estarán felices de saber que, en esta ocasión, pueden conseguir totalmente gratis más de $100 USD en contenido. Cada jueves, la tienda de PC sorprende a la fanaticada con 2 o más obsequios. Para conseguir estas recompensas, basta con tener una cuenta activa y reclamar los artículos durante el periodo correspondiente. ¿La mejor parte? Los juegos permanecen para siempre en la biblioteca , sin la necesidad de contratar un servicio o cumplir con otros requisitos. La plataforma acaba de actualizar su lista de regalos, y ahora los usuarios tienen la oportunidad de obtener sin costo alguno un divertido roguelike de aspecto pixelart y un contenido descargable valuado en más de $100 USD para un popular RPG. Aunque a veces consiente a la comunidad con grandes juegos AAA , generalmente la Epic Games Store obsequia títulos independientes ; esto no es algo malo, pues la promoción representa la oportunidad ideal para conocer propuestas que, por el contrario, permanecerían por debajo del radar de muchos jugadores. Del 5 al 12 de marzo de 2026 , los usuarios con una cuenta activa en la tienda de PC podrán conseguir gratis un total de 2 obsequios: el videojuego indie Turnip Boy Robs a Bank y un paquete DLC para Idle Champions of the Forgotten Realms . El regalo más llamativo de esta semana es el indie Turnip Boy Robs a Bank , un divertido roguelike de acción con vista aérea que recuerda a exponentes del género como Enter The Gungeon . En esta aventura single-player, el criminal conocido como Nabolino se une a una pandilla con un objetivo en mente: saquear un banco para conseguir un gran botín. Este proyecto de Snoozy Kazoo y Graffiti Games tiene reseñas extremadamente positivas en Steam, así que en definitiva los jugadores de PC deberían reclamarlo gratis en la Epic Games Store mientras aún sea posible. El segundo obsequio de esta semana es el paquete Dragonlance para Idle Champions of the Forgotten Realms , el popular RPG que reúne a personajes icónicos del universo ficticio de Dungeons & Dragons . Según la descripción oficial, DLC tiene un valor de más de $100 USD y da acceso a recompensas especiales. La oferta permite a los jugadores conseguir gratis a los campeones Raistlin , Gale , Spurt, Briv y Ellywick , junto con 32 cofres de platino , 2 pociones de tiempo limitado y más objetos. Si bien la tienda de PC ofreció una lista bastante sólida para iniciar marzo, es posible que los obsequios no sean del agrado de todos los jugadores. La buena noticia es que, como es tradición, ya conocemos la identidad de los títulos que se ofrecerán totalmente gratis en la segunda semana del mes. Del 12 al 19 de marzo, los usuarios de la Epic Games Store podrán reclamar un total de 2 videojuegos : Cozy Grove , un hermoso simulador de vida en el que los jugadores acampan en una isla encantada repleta de espíritus animales; e Isonzo , un trepidante FPS multijugador ambientado en la Primera Guerra Mundial. Estos son los próximos juegos gratuitos de la Epic Games Store: Turnip Boy Robs a Bank ― Del 5 al 12 de marzo DLC Dragonlance para Idle Champions of the Forgotten Realms ― Del 5 al 12 de marzo Pero cuéntanos, ¿te gustaron los regalos? Comparte tu opinión en los comentarios. Encontrarás más información sobre juegos gratuitos de la Epic Games Store si visitas esta página . Video relacionado: Ser gamer es más caro que nunca

title: Marvel Rivals: Best Dive Characters
meta_description: Dive characters in Marvel Rivals are versatile and mobile. These are the best picks in the game.
headings: Disrupts Strategists And Holds Objective Like No Other
Swiftly Takes Out Enemies Without Being Seen
body_text: Dive heroes like Venom excel at surprise attacks on squishy enemies with high mobility. Captain America disrupts strategists and excels at holding objectives with his reflective shield. Black Panther swiftly takes out enemies with speed and evasion, resetting cooldowns consistently. There are many characters on Marvel Rivals' growing roster, from the blazing Human Torch to the mighty God of Thunder, Thor. One of the most popular types of characters to play are 'dive' heroes. Dive heroes are unique in that they typically have high mobility to move around the map, yet rely on close-range combat to achieve any eliminations. These characters are a great pick against 'poke' enemies, yet have a harder time against 'rush' heroes. Dive characters like Psylocke or Spider-Man work best at flanking the enemy team and taking out enemy Strategists or other lower-HP foes in the back line. Everyone in Marvel Rivals brings something to the table, but these characters tend to fall under the radar. Venom is one of Marvel Rivals' most fearsome Vanguards, as he constantly aggravates the enemy's back line and its supports. Venom, like Spider-Man, can swing around the map, as well as crawl and run on the walls to navigate terrain with ease. With his dive bomb ability, Venom excels in surprise attacks on groups of 'squishy' enemies. After diving onto a team, launching his tendril ability allows him to dish out good amounts of damage to multiple enemies while focusing his primary fire onto the most prominent target. Venom is also the best hero when it comes to solo survivability, as his regeneration ability grants him more shield the lower his health is. Captain America can utilize his unlimited sprint and shield dive abilities to quickly bombard enemy Strategists and poke heroes. His survivability and reflective shield allow him to continue fighting without healing for a while as well. Captain America can throw his shield, which will ricochet off a surface and track enemy targets nearby for a small amount of damage. Combining this with his dive, primary melee attacks, and primary ranged attack, Cap can either achieve an easy elimination or disrupt Strategists from their healing. Captain America is also the best at holding objectives, as his mobility and ultimate ability allow him to stay alive long enough for his team to take back point. Though infamously known as the "tickle monster" in Marvel Rivals , Black Panther can be excruciatingly disruptive to an entire enemy team when played correctly and not countered. Black Panther's playstyle revolved around his attacks leaving vibranium marks on an enemy, and then using his dash on the same enemy to 'spend' the mark and regain a dash. These characters didn't make it onto the roster of fighters, but they're still mentioned in the game's lore or dialogue. Without missing, Black Panther constantly resets cooldowns and moves at such a speed that it becomes almost impossible to nail him down until he misses a vibranium mark or dash. Using his ultimate, which deals a fair amount of damage to those in front of him and leaves vibranium marks on enemies, and combining his abilities, Black Panther can wipe out a team by himself. Though his damage was nerfed in Season 1, Iron Fist gained a buff in survivability that allowed him to stay alive longer when diving the enemy team. His primary melee attack, Jeet Kune Do, locks onto an enemy and pulls Iron Fist toward them while attacking. When he uses Dragon Defense to counter enemy attacks, Iron Fist will use Yat Jee Chung Kuen as his primary attack, which unleashes a flurry of punches onto the enemy and melts health bars fairly quickly. Iron Fist can also heal 150hp for himself with Harmony Recovery, allowing him to stay in the fight longer and attack Strategists or melt Vanguards' health down. Psylocke is devastating when it comes to eliminating an enemy by surprise. She can use her Psychic Stealth ability to turn invisible and move much faster for 3 seconds, which is enough time to flank her target or get away from an enemy. When she's ready to take out a target, she can utilize her abilities to erase a Strategist's or Duelist's health bar entirely. When she's not flanking, her abilities are on cooldown, or can't locate a viable target, Psylocke uses her Psionic Crossbow to essentially poke at the team when needed and can use her ultimate ability to take out an entire group of enemies. As the most difficult Duelist to master in Marvel Rivals , Spider-Man's skill ceiling is exceptionally high. Spider-Man has the best mobility in the game, as he can swing multiple times around the map, crawl and run on walls, and use specific techniques to combo his abilities and move around even faster. If an opponent Spider-Man is giving your team trouble in Marvel Rivals, bring out one of these characters to stand up to the webslinger. Spider-Man is a menace when diving onto a single enemy. Shooting a target with a web will place a Spider-Tracer onto them, allowing him to trigger the tracer with an uppercut or normal melee attack to deal bonus damage. This allows him to combo an enemy to elimination in seconds and is done in the blink of an eye when he's teamed up with Venom . Magik is often regarded as one of the best Duelists in Marvel Rivals , and for good reason. Magik's sword and her various abilities do massive amounts of damage to a target. When she deals damage to an enemy, Magik generates shield for herself, which allows her to dive the enemy team and stay alive for longer periods of time. Like Spider-Man, she can combo her abilities, such as using Magik Slash from afar, running in with Umbral Incursion, and then finishing the combo off with a primary sword slash and quick melee attack .

title: 明石家さんま、変装なしでコンビニに現れた姿がファンを驚かす 「違和感すごい」「腰抜かすレベルです」（1/2） | エンタメ ねとらぼ
meta_description: null
headings: 明石家さんま、変装なしでコンビニに現れた姿がファンを驚かす 「違和感すごい」「腰抜かすレベルです」
body_text: お笑いコンビ「次長課長」の井上聡さんが9月7日、明石家さんまさんが座長を務める公演「 笑輪の笑い〜Born ready達〜全国ツアー 」のオフショットをInstagramで公開。さんまさんがナチュラルにコンビニにいる姿に反響が寄せられています。 同ツアーには、さんまさんの他、松尾伴内さん、ジミー大西さん、「中川家」「次長課長」「イワイガワ」の岩井ジョニ男さん、「アキナ」「アインシュタイン」、金原早苗さんといった総勢13人の出演者が集結。2022年元旦から行われており、これまで福島県、山口県、宮崎県、7月8日からは富山公演が行われていました。 井上さんはこの日、「さんまさんの舞台ツアーで静岡に行ってきました」と9月2日、3日に開催された静岡公演の様子をシェア。「今回もいつも通り、あたたかいお客さんのおかげでとても楽しい公演となりました」と来場者へ感謝をつづっています。 投稿では、楽屋や打ち上げでのオフショットを公開。中には、さんまさんを初めとする出演陣がコンビニに訪れている姿も公開されており、コメント欄では「さんまさんがコンビニいるの違和感すごい、変な感じw」「コンビニにいきなりこのメンバーがいたら、腰抜かすレベルです」「さんまさんの『大量に氷を買い込む姿』は特にレアで大好きなシリーズです」「こんなおじさん達がたくさん買い物に来たら、レジで1人パニクってるはず」と驚く声が多く寄せられました。 7月には富山公演でも訪れたコンビニでのショットを公開。その際にも「このメンバーでコンビニは強すぎます」といった声があがっていました。

Surely the Inquisitor cannot be a part of such things. It seems a base prank to intentionally disrupt such an affair. A memo from Josephine , written on a letter from Sera: This guy is a special prick. Swats his people around, Thinks his sovereigns pay for it. Embarrass him at his own party And you please a dozen people. People with keys and little else. Sera A date and time are scribbled below. Also an Orlesian mask, with other masks for eyes. It is trivial to ensure we are represented, but should we encourage such base behavior? Lights out. Not murder-just let them stumble in darkness. A small training exercise outside will disrupt the event. The report is scandalous -- A dowager threw a pie! Red Jenny is somehow credited, but we have received a number of keys to caches of smuggled goods. None of the nobility are objecting, lest they be forced to claim it as theirs. It appears our detracting Lord found shame in the dark, thanks to a scandalous laugh. Also, blind-fetes are now the rage. Can Red Jenny claim that? Orlais remains Orlais. How do I best describe it? Our noble target was angered by the distraction of our soldiers and, in an attempt to regain attention, accidently stabbed a revered mother in the hat. Somehow Red Jenny is the assumed benefactor, but we have received several propositions from witnesses to the events. Unlocks war table operation Red Jenny and the Bad Goods once the Inquisition reaches Influence Level 5 and has captured at least one keep.

title: Dominik Szoboszlai has David Beckham target after latest Liverpool heroics
meta_description: Liverpool midfielder Dominik Szoboszlai scored another free-kick this weekend and the Hungarian is within touching distance of a Premier League record set by David Beckham
headings: Dominik Szoboszlai has David Beckham target after latest Liverpool heroics
body_text: Liverpool midfielder Dominik Szoboszlai celebrates scoring against Tottenham (Image: Liverpool FC via Getty Images) Dominik Szoboszlai has scored four direct free-kick goals in the Premier League this season, which is the most ever by a Liverpool player in a single campaign in the competition. In fact, only David Beckham (in 2000/01 for Manchester United ) and Laurent Robert (in 2001/02 for Newcastle ) have netted more in a single season (they both got five). James Ward-Prowse has reached four in two previous seasons — 2020/21 and 2021/22 — so Szoboszlai is level with him, and needs just one more to match the Beckham and Robert record. He has eight more games remaining to score via that route again. Though Szoboszlai got on the scoresheet against Spurs, Liverpool was unable to further extend its lead in the game and was made to pay for that when Richarlison equalized. "I don’t know what happened, I have nothing to say," Szoboszlai said live on Sky Sports post-match . "In the last minute, again [we conceded], I don’t know how many times this season already. We have to wake up. "We have to wake up because there’s eight, nine games to go, and if we keep going like this, next season we should be happy with the Conference League. Liverpool head coach Arne Slot suffered another blow on Sunday (Image: Gaspafotos/MB Media/Getty Images) "I don’t know [why it keeps happening]. If I would have the answer then I probably tell you guys and we will change it. But, honestly, I don’t know." It is now eight times that Liverpool has conceded in stoppage time in the Premier League this season. Those points could prove costly in the race for the Champions League places. "Of course it's damaging," Arne Slot admitted. "But it's how many times this has been damaging for us. We don't help ourselves at all. "It's so many times this season that we've created much more xG or chances than the amount of goals we score. Liverpool's home and away strips for the 2025/26 season are the first in their new partnership with adidas. Fans can order jerseys in the US at Fanatics.com or via the LFC Online Store in both the US and across the world. "Usually in the Premier League you see a lot of teams outperforming their xG, but that definitely is [not] what we are doing throughout the whole season. "Then a game still stays tight until the end and we struggle to keep clean sheets. We haven't had as many clean sheets as you would want if you want to go higher up in the table. That's a bad combination for picking up the amount of points we want to pick up." Liverpool.com says: Szoboszlai has been the Reds' best player this season, but his influence is lessened by being at right-back. He scored against Spurs, but wasn't as involved as he would have been in the center.

title: Award-winning holiday parks get multi-million pound makeovers
meta_description: There's plenty to get excited about with new Bear Grylls Escape Rooms, high-ropes, off-road vehicles and more
headings: Award-winning holiday park hailed as 'a special place' after multi-million makeover
There's plenty to get excited about with new Bear Grylls Escape Rooms, high-ropes, off-road vehicles and more
body_text: This March, British seaside breaks are set to get a major refresh as Parkdean Resorts reopens two of its most popular destinations – Vauxhall Holiday Park in Norfolk and Highfield Grange in Essex . Following £10 million transformations, both parks promise a "Bigger, Better, Bolder" experience just in time for the Easter holidays. The multi-million-pound makeovers combine high-tech adventure with reimagined family comforts, ensuring there’s something for everyone – from toddlers to grandparents. Both parks has been upgraded to create a fresh, fun-filled holiday experience. Proving that family fun doesn’t have to break the bank, getaways start from just £112 in April. School holiday deals also offer excellent value, with a four-night stay at Vauxhall Holiday Park from £149 – £37 per person. The park improvements include a wide range of activities to keep the fun going, whatever the Great British weather throws at us. Thrill-seekers can tackle new high and low ropes courses, while the Bear Grylls Escape Rooms offer a survival-themed challenge for families and friends to enjoy together. Younger explorers can enjoy the "Off-Road Rangers" track and the new "Tots Safe Zones," designed just for little adventurers. Meanwhile, the Hat Tricks Sports Bar provides a social hub for adults and teens with interactive darts, shuffleboard, indoor golf, and stylish outdoor dining terraces for al fresco meals. Vauxhall Holiday Park in Norfolk and Highfield Grange in Essex have both undergone an extensive £10 million transformation. This £10 million transformation isn’t just about new activities; it completely reimagines the park experience. Upgraded facilities and fresh dining options, including Thunderbird Fried Chicken and Scoops Ice Cream parlours, ensure the quality of the stay matches the excitement of the entertainment. With the grand re-openings in March, the parks are ready to welcome families planning last-minute Easter breaks or summer getaways. Both are conveniently close to local beaches, guaranteeing plenty of seaside fun. Highfield Grange lies just a 10-minute drive from Clacton Beach with its Pier and traditional amusements, whilst the neighbouring town of Frinton-on-Sea provides a charming retreat with Victorian character and independent boutiques. Meanwhile, Vauxhall Holiday Park sits just minutes from Great Yarmouth's bustling promenade. For those wishing to venture slightly further afield, the nearby Norfolk Broads are within easy reach by car. An additional enhancement planned for the 2026 season includes the launch of an all-inclusive package at 42 of its UK parks for selected dates throughout the year. Rates start from £25 per adult per day and £10 per child, with four-night midweek all-inclusive packages proving particularly attractive for both couples and families. The all-inclusive package encompasses three complete meals each day - breakfast, lunch and dinner. Guests can enjoy unlimited Costa Coffee and soft drinks throughout their stay, enabling them to unwind without worrying about additional costs. For those unable to find a suitable break with Parkdean Resorts, Haven has also launched its 2026 offers, with basic three-night caravan holidays available from just £49. Elsewhere at Forest Holidays, nature lovers can save up to £60 on log cabin getaways across 13 UK sites, or up to £150 on week-long breaks. Guests at both holiday parks have taken to sharing their feedback online, with numerous five-star reviews appearing. One satisfied guest at Highfields Grange commented: "We've had a lovely week away. The entertainment staff have been amazing. Great shows, brilliant interaction with the kids. Becky, Ricky and all the entertainers... can't fault them." Another guest remarked: "Amazing holiday kids has so much much perfect location. The night entertainment was amazing." At Vauxhall Holiday Park, one guest described it as a "special place" saying: "It was our first holiday of this kind, but we felt wonderful. The caravan was clean, spacious and warm, the beds comfortable. We went with our five-year old grandson, who enjoyed a lot of activities, many interactive, in which we, the grandparents, also found a place. " However, not every visitor shared the same enthusiasm. One remarked that "the caravan was OK but not clean" whilst another observed: "Caravan was 100%. Perfect. Modern, clean, comfortable, but the whole pool area let everything down. Dirty, unattractive, changing rooms door did not lock." Despite this, the majority of holidaymakers appear to appreciate a classic seaside caravan getaway at both locations, even prior to any recent upgrades. One repeat guest commented: "Went on holiday here in August, loved every minute and worth every penny. Definitely will be going again, great facilities from the arcade to the bar, from the pool to the restaurant and excellent staff all around." For further information or to make a reservation, visit Vauxhall Holiday Park or Highfield Grange via the Parkdean website.

title: Casamento na infância ou adolescência não é conto de fadas
body_text: Casamento na infância ou na adolescência não é história de conto de fadas como alguns acreditam. Sim, tem gente que acredita que uma menina em vulnerabilidade social pode ter uma vida melhor se casando com um homem mais velho que vai prover alguns "confortos" a ela. É o casamento infantil travestido de proteção, de uma melhor condição de vida. Aliás, tá ai duas palavras que jamais deveriam dividir a mesma linha: casamento e infância ou casamento e adolescência. E mesmo após a mudança na legislação em 2019, que proibiu o casamento antes dos 16 anos em qualquer circunstância, os números seguem desafiando a letra da lei. Em 2024, foram registrados 193 casamentos civis envolvendo menores de 16 anos no país, sendo 183 casos em que a noiva era menor e o noivo tinha 16 anos ou mais. Como explicar registros oficiais incompatíveis com a própria legislação? O Instituto Liberta tem repetido há anos que a violência sexual contra crianças no Brasil é epidêmica. São cerca de 66 mil estupros por ano, sendo a maioria contra meninas. No caso do estupro de vulnerável, mais de 60% das vítimas têm até 13 anos. E, em 64% dos casos, o agressor é alguém do convívio familiar. A violência não está na esquina escura da cidade. Está dentro de casa. O casamento infantil , nesse contexto, não é um evento isolado. Ele é muitas vezes o desfecho de uma sequência de violências : abuso, gravidez precoce, evasão escolar, trabalho doméstico intensificado. É a tentativa de transformar crime em compromisso, violência em contrato, estupro em "união estável". E há algo ainda mais perverso: o discurso de que essas meninas "assumem responsabilidade" , "constroem família", "amadurecem". O relatório da Plan é categórico ao mostrar que o poder de decisão dessas meninas é mínimo, seja sobre educação, mobilidade, finanças ou planejamento reprodutivo. Muitas relatam intimidação ao desafiar a autoridade do marido. Que conto de fadas é esse em que a princesa não pode decidir nem sobre o próprio corpo? "O casamento infantil raramente é uma escolha livre", fala Lucas Ramos Lopes, secretário executivo da Coalizão Brasileira pelo Fim da Violência contra Crianças e Adolescentes . Segundo ele, na maior parte das vezes, surge como resposta socialmente aceita a contextos de pobreza, gravidez precoce, violência sexual ou ausência de proteção estatal. O casamento infantil no Brasil não é apenas fruto da pobreza - embora ela seja determinante. É também fruto de uma moral social que ainda considera a sexualidade da menina um problema a ser resolvido, e não um direito a ser protegido. É fruto de normas de gênero que continuam a valorizar a fertilidade e a obediência como destino feminino. É fruto de uma sociedade que ainda hesita em chamar violência pelo nome.

title: Can You Outgrow ADHD?
meta_description: Do kids with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) outgrow it? Here’s what the evidence shows.
headings: What Happens to ADHD and Its Symptoms With Age?
What Happens to the Brain With Age When You Have ADHD?
What to Expect as You Grow Up With ADHD
body_text: If your child has trouble staying focused or acts impulsively due to attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), you might hope that it’s something they’ll grow out of. After all, some symptoms of ADHD , such as daydreaming, forgetfulness , or fidgeting , can be pretty normal for kids. But while it can happen sometimes, most kids with ADHD don’t just outgrow it. People can find out they have ADHD at any age, but it’s most often diagnosed in kids around age 6. Experts – and many parents – used to think that ADHD lasts through the teenage years and into adulthood only about half the time. But more recent studies suggest that ADHD and its symptoms usually continue even after kids grow up. Sometimes, ADHD symptoms might go away and come back or change over time. Earlier studies suggested that ADHD symptoms often did go away. For example, one 2016 study looked at 579 kids diagnosed with ADHD between ages 7 and 10. Close to half of them were evaluated again as adults, at an average age of about 25. The study was based on reports from parents and from the adults who’d had ADHD. The study found that 60% of kids with ADHD still had symptoms as adults. More than 40% of them fully met the criteria for ADHD based on their symptoms and the degree to which those symptoms interfered with their life. Studies such as this one led to the general view that ADHD continues into adulthood about half the time. But the precise numbers have varied from one study to the next. For instance, another study from 2013 looked at more than 5,700 adults who’d been diagnosed with ADHD as children. It found that ADHD persisted in about 30% of them. It was clear from these earlier studies that many kids with ADHD don’t outgrow it when they become adults. But they still suggested that perhaps a lot of kids do “outgrow” ADHD. This view was challenged by a more recent study that looked at people with ADHD several times instead of only once or twice. The researchers noted that most efforts to look at what happens in ADHD over time didn’t account for whether people still had any symptoms of ADHD. They also didn’t consider whether adults who’d had ADHD as kids were still taking medicine for it. To get more information in this new study, researchers enrolled more than 550 kids with ADHD. They followed up with them eight times, starting from 2 years after the first interview up to 16 years, when the subjects were around age 25. They looked for ADHD and ADHD symptoms each time by asking parents, teachers, and the people with ADHD themselves. They also considered whether they were being treated for ADHD. What they found suggests that ADHD isn’t something that you just have or don’t have. And the path it can take over time also can vary. Consistent with earlier studies, about 30% of those in the study did seem to fully recover from ADHD and its symptoms at some point. But most who seemingly recovered – 60% – had a recurrence. So their ADHD seemed to go away for a time and then come back. The study also found that most adults who didn’t fully meet the criteria for ADHD still had symptoms sometimes or still were taking ADHD medicine . Only about 9% of the kids got over or seemed to permanently “outgrow” their ADHD. The condition appeared to remain stable in less than 11% of people in the study. Most with ADHD showed changes over time. Based on reports by those in the study, ADHD symptoms most often seemed to go up and down. They could get better for a while and then worse again. This was a small study, so we need more research to draw firm conclusions. But these findings show that ADHD and its symptoms can change for reasons that aren’t clear. It’s not entirely understood what happens in the brain of kids with ADHD or how it changes over time. Lots of studies have been done to look at the brain. But one reason it’s hard to say is that different studies have seen different things. There could be many reasons for those differences, including variations among people with ADHD and how those studies were done. Another recent small study looked at brain images of 31 young adults with ADHD compared to adults without ADHD. It also considered how many symptoms the adults had when they were kids. The study did see some widespread changes, including changes in density and shape in different parts of the brain. Those changes were most strongly linked to symptoms of ADHD people had when they were kids. It suggested to the researchers that changes in the brains of kids with ADHD symptoms are still there when those kids get older. So this adds to the idea that childhood ADHD doesn’t go away with age. But there are still lots of questions. More study is needed to really understand what happens in the brain in ADHD and how it changes over time. It also may depend on what type of ADHD a person has. Even if your child doesn’t outgrow ADHD, it may look different as they get older. ADHD symptoms in kids often are easier to spot. Adults who’ve had ADHD for years might have found ways to manage or hide their symptoms. Kids with ADHD might squirm, fidget, or run around a lot. They may have trouble staying on task at school or at home. They might also be impulsive, cut into lines, or talk out of turn. As adults, they may often continue to struggle with attention while some of the other symptoms go away or are turned inward. For example, instead of being hyperactive, they may feel restless or easily bored. Many different things can affect how ADHD symptoms might change over time. Stress can play a role. So can the amount of support a person receives and how well they can find ways to manage these symptoms. It’s also possible that some kids who seem to outgrow ADHD may not have had it to begin with. Some experts think this is one reason that some kids appear to outgrow it. The number of kids who are diagnosed with ADHD has gone up in recent years. It’s not clear if that’s because the condition is more common or just that more kids are being diagnosed. The way doctors diagnose ADHD also has changed over time, which may explain some of the differences. If your child lives with ADHD now, you can expect symptoms to get better with treatment and as you learn ways to help them manage it. As they grow up, it’s likely there will be times when the symptoms won’t bother them as much or may even go away. But the latest evidence suggests that most likely – nine times out of 10 – they won’t completely outgrow it.

title: Supervolcán en Italia enciende las alarmas: expertos temen una erupción con impacto global
meta_description: Geólogos advierten sobre la actividad del supervolcán Campos Flégreos en Italia, cuya erupción podría alterar el clima y afectar al mundo entero.
body_text: La Tierra guarda bajo su superficie una fuerza difícil de imaginar: los supervolcanes. Estos gigantes dormidos han provocado en el pasado catástrofes capaces de cambiar la historia del planeta, y ahora, uno de ellos vuelve a ser noticia. Los expertos mantienen la mirada fija en Italia , donde el supervolcán de los Campos Flégreos muestra señales de inquietante actividad. Un informe reciente ha encendido la alerta científica . Aunque no hay certeza sobre una erupción inminente, los movimientos detectados en la región hacen pensar que el riesgo es real. La magnitud de una explosión de este tipo podría provocar alteraciones climáticas a nivel global, daños en los ecosistemas y graves consecuencias para la población. Al Extremo | Programa 22 de agosto del 2025 Los Campos Flégreos se encuentran cerca de Nápoles , en la región de Campania. Esta zona alberga varias calderas, incluida la famosa Solfatara , conocida por sus fumarolas y emisiones de gases sulfurosos. Se trata de un “laboratorio natural” donde los geólogos pueden estudiar la actividad volcánica en tiempo real . Su importancia no solo radica en lo científico : históricamente, esta región ha sido testigo de grandes erupciones y guarda restos arqueológicos de civilizaciones como Pompeya y Herculano . Para los investigadores, cualquier variación en la actividad subterránea es motivo de atención, pues incluso un cambio mínimo podría anunciar un evento mayor . A diferencia de un volcán común, un supervolcán puede liberar miles de veces más material al entrar en erupción . Esto generaría una nube de cenizas que bloquearía la luz solar durante semanas o meses, afectando la agricultura, la temperatura del planeta y la vida humana . Aunque los escenarios más extremos parecen lejanos, la comunidad científica insiste en no bajar la guardia y reforzar los sistemas de monitoreo. El mensaje de los geólogos es claro: aún no sabemos cuándo podría despertar este coloso, pero estar preparados es la mejor defensa ante una de las mayores amenazas naturales que enfrenta la humanidad .

How to Use Disposable Email Address?

It is also known by names like : tempmail, 10minutemail, 10minmail, throwaway email, fake-mail , fake email generator, burner mail or trash-mail. The Tech behind Disposable Email Addresses Everyone owns an email address each and every hour, for everything from connecting at work, with business prospects, reaching out to friends and colleagues using the email address as an online passport. Nearly 99% of all apps and services we sign-up today required an email address, likewise to most shoppers loyalty cards, contest and offer entries, and more. We all enjoy having an email address, but getting tons of spam emails each day doesn’t feel comfortable. Furthermore, it’s entirely common for stores to have their databases hacked, leaving your business email address at risk and more likely to end up on spam lists. Still, nothing done online is 100% private. Thus you need to protect your email contact identity and best done using disposable emails address. So, What Is A Disposable Email Address? Recently, I found a bounce rate complex than usual on my latest email blast! I later realized the surge of users (or bots) signing up for my services hiding their real identity using disposable mail addresses. Disposable email address (DEA) technically means an approach where a user’s with a unique email address gets a temporary email address for your current contact. The DEA allow the creation of an email address that passes validity need to sign-up for services and website without having to show your true identity. Disposable emails address if compromised or used in connection with email abuse online, the owner can’t be tied to the abuse and quickly cancel its application without affecting other contacts. With temporary mail, you can you receive your emails from the fake emails in your genuine emails address for a specified time set. The fake email address is simply a through-away email, temporary email set and self-destructs email. Why would you need a fake email address? You must have noted services such as Amazon Prime, Hulu and Netflix allow limited-time test runs(trials), however, if still determined to use the services all you need is a disposable email address. Technically, you can extend your trial usage using a different email address linked to your original (genuine) after the trial period expires. An offline or online retailer tend to demand an email address to take advantage of their offers, however, this result in an unwanted deluge of spam promotional emails that you could avoid. Temporary email address makes it easy to cut out those irritating messages you are still receiving. Technically, the idea of a temporary email address conjures up with black hat hackers and underworld internet, but there are convincing reason to us fake email services. headings: Simone Biles Stuns Fans with Heartfelt Message After Husband Joins Colts
body_text: Simone Biles is set to bring her electrifying presence to Indiana as her husband, Jonathan Owens, joins the Indianapolis Colts on a one-year deal. The couple, who spent the last two years in Chicago, are ready to embark on this new chapter in their lives. Owens, at 30, becomes the latest addition to the Colts' roster, joining the team alongside fellow newcomers Arden Key and Michael Clemons. As they prepare to settle in Circle City, Biles shared her excitement on Instagram, expressing pride in Owens' journey: “Excited to call Indy home this upcoming football season 💙 Beyond proud of you @jowens! Keep defeating the odds. I love you.” The Indianapolis community has warmly embraced the seven-time Olympic gold medalist and her husband. Fans flooded social media with welcoming messages, reflecting the city's enthusiasm for their arrival. “Welcome, Simone! We’re so happy to have you here in Indy,” one fan shared, while another added, “Welcome to Indianapolis, Owens family. You will enjoy our city, and we will love and support both of you 💗💚.” Biles, known for her unwavering support, was frequently seen cheering for Owens during his time with the Chicago Bears. Now, with Owens donning the Colts' blue and white, fans can expect her presence to add even more star power to Lucas Oil Stadium. Starting anew in a different city can be challenging, but with the outpouring of support from fans, Biles and Owens seem poised to make a seamless transition. Indianapolis is ready to welcome this dynamic duo with open arms. title: Inside Benicio del Toro's Private World as Dad to Daughter Delilah
meta_description: Oscar winner Benicio del Toro lets his acting do the talking when it comes to his Hollywood persona, but off-screen the charismatic star is just Dad to daughter Delilah, 14, with Kimberly Stewart.
body_text: It's not as if Benicio del Toro went anywhere, but now he's noticeably everywhere. Of course, that has something to do with it being awards season and the 2026 Oscar nominee has been attending one show after another—including the Actor Awards with his 14-year-old daughter Delilah —following his hoot of a supporting turn as a stealth-activist karate instructor in One Battle After Another . One wonders, in fact, just how far outside his comfort zone Del Toro had to reach to play cool-as-a-cucumber sensei Sergio St. Carlos, who gives Leonardo DiCaprio 's frantic dad Bob Ferguson a major assist in finding his missing daughter Willa, played by Chase Infiniti . And not only because Paul Thomas Anderson wrote the part for Del Toro, after which the Puerto Rican star made some key changes that ended up setting the tone for the rest of the movie. "My character was introduced by killing someone in my dojo," Del Toro said in December on the Los Angeles Times ' The Envelope Oscar Actors Roundtable . "So I asked [PTA], 'OK, if I kill this guy in the dojo, I'm not going to drive Leo anywhere! We have to get rid of the body and we're going to have to clean the dojo, or set it on fire." Ultimately, "Why am I doing that?" he explained. "So, from there, it evolved into, 'We're not killing anybody.'" But that's just the kind of confidence Del Toro—who also starred in Wes Anderson 's The Phoenician Scheme last year—brings with him to any set now, that swagger well-earned after nearly 40 years in Hollywood, which includes a Best Supporting Actor Oscar win for 2000's Traffic. Oprah Winfrey Explains Walking Like She Was "90 Years Old" at Paris Fashion Week Oscars 2026 Red Carpet Fashion: See Every Celebrity Look (Live Updates) Prince William Shares Never-Before-Seen Photo of Princess Diana in Mother’s Day Tribute And yet despite having the sort of presence that instantly elevates every scene he's in, Del Toro saves his drama for the screen, leading a very private life when he's not compelled to speak about himself while promoting films. “Sometimes you read it or see it and it’s like, well, that’s not really what I meant at all,” he told The Hollywood Reporter recently about his aversion to interviews. “It shakes you a bit. It becomes … permanent.” But while he called the amount of attention he's been getting lately "bizarre," it was also "an honor" to be recognized for OBAA , which is nominated for 13 Oscars heading into the March 15 ceremony at Hollywood's Dolby Theatre. "It’s huge," Del Toro said. "But it’s very surprising. There’s something about it that makes me want to not believe it. And I’m trying to enjoy this wave. What I’ve learned about it is, let it rip. It’s beyond my control. There’s nothing I can do." The wave did give him the opportunity to bring his only child to an award show, a rare peek at his life as a girl dad. Delilah's mom is model Kimberly Stewart , 46, who was not in a relationship with Del Toro, per se, when they became parents. Confirming Stewart's pregnancy in April 2011, they said in a statement that Usual Suspects star was "very supportive" and, "although they are not a couple, they are looking forward to the arrival of the baby." "I had her at the right time," Stewart, who shares Delilah's Aug. 21 birthday, told HELLO! in 2015 . "I got all the partying out of my system and I just wanted to be with her all the time. I've been a stay-at-home mother since she was born and I've loved every second." Her then-4-year-old, she continued, was "sassy and has great manners—I really instilled that from a young age—so she's always saying 'please' and 'thank you.'" At the time, Stewart—the second of Rod Stewart 's eight kids—said she was "dating here and there, but no one who I would want to introduce Delilah to." Delilah also happened to be Rod's first grandchild, the "Forever Young" singer telling E! when she was born that his joy was "indescribable." "We had everyone at the hospital to help Kimberly," Rod said. "I won't say we always agree, but we get along." Everyone included Del Toro, who has remained close to Stewart—and her family—as a co-parent. "I try to stay under the radar now," he told the New Zealand Herald in 2016. "I'm a father and that changes everything...I'm thinking about the future of another human being who I'm in love with." And though he has primarily kept that part of his life out of the public eye, with showbiz coursing through Delilah's genes—"I might pull the Oscar out to show to my daughter," Del Toro quipped to Port in 2015."Grandpa has a lot of stuff, but he ain't got one of those"—he's been preparing, just in case she takes after him. While he predicted to E! in 2015 that she was going to be a teacher because "she likes telling me what to do," more recently he's come around to the idea of Delilah following in his footsteps. "I don't come from a family of theater people or movie people, so they were very nervous with me," Del Toro said on a June 2025 episode of the Happy Sad Confused podcast. "I come from a family of professionals, not in the movie world or the theater world, so they were very concerned." But, he added, "My daughter, if she wants to be a lawyer, great. The next day, she wants to be a designer, great. But I do know that if she wants to be an actress, you just have to let her be." So, perhaps consider their father-daughter date at the Actor Awards a trial run for the teen. The Full House alum appears with her look-alike eldest daughter (left) and daughter Beatrix during a trip to Jamaica in 2023. A total touchdown moment. The NBC Nightly News anchor and his son celebrate the Miami Hurricanes' latest win in an adorable selfie. Like mother like daughter. After becoming a household name on The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills , the former supermodel Yolanda Hadid 's daughter Gigi Hadid broke into the fashion industry as a teen and has been rocking runways ever since. Clint Eastwood 's fifth child, son Scott Eastwood , has followed in his movie star footsteps by starring in films like Suicide Squad, Snowden, The Fate of the Furious and Pacific Rim. The Bon Jovi singer and his third child is his mini-me. Pictured: The two appear on either sides of with the musician's wife, Dorothea Bongiovi , and their youngest son Romeo Bongiovi at the Jefferson Awards Foundation' 2017 DC National Ceremony at the Capital Hilton in Washington, DC. in 2017. The look-alike duo is all smiles while attending a 2016 performance of Finding Neverland in the Big Apple. Woah, the Marsden men's resemblance was uncanny at the 2017 SAG Awards . The Alfie star’s daughter, whom she shares with ex Tom Sturridge , joined her mom for a rare public outing in London in April 2025. Double take! The Real Housewives of Atlanta alum's daughter looks like her younger sister! The soccer star has the cutest plus-one ever at the London premiere of Ronaldo . Christie Brinkley & Sailor Brinkley-Cook Sailor is taking after her mom in the modeling industry. And Kate's daughter is also following in her mom's famous footsteps. Here posing for an Instagram snap before the Hot Pursuit premiere in Los Angeles, mom and her daughter have never looked more alike! Like mother, like daughter. The two attend the Vanity Fair Oscars party in 2015. With their matching jumpsuits, sleek hair and glasses, the two look more like twins than mother and daughter! "That moment when you realize you actually are becoming your mother #twinning #imnotmad," Willis joked on Instagram. Pictured here in 2010, the pop icon's daughter is a fashionista just like her mama! You'll get a kick out of how much the soccer star and his son look alike. The spunky morning show host celebrated her daughter's 14th birthday with this adorable pic. Look at that resemblance! Once upon a time in Hollywood...the Kill Bill actress twinned with her daughter on the red carpet. Little Blue stole the show at the 2017 Grammys, and she's totally #twinning with her pops! The Days of Our Lives star and her daughter get in the holiday spirit at Disney On Ice Presents Frozen at the Staples Center. The mother-daughter pair looked like virtual twinsies (especially with those matching bobs) at the Chanel fall-winter Haute Couture show in Paris. The Oscar-winner and her daughter both rock the red hair. The nearly identical stunning stars even dress the same! Watch the Oscars 2026 Sunday, March 15, at 7 p.m. ET/4 p.m. PT on ABC and streaming on Hulu.

title: Pill Container by Isopoda
meta_description: I am a stickler for using a little filament as possible in a print, so I designed my own streamlined travel pill box. The one shown in the images is printed at 80%, I recommend not going any smaller as the walls may become too thin. I used one that was 100% and it fit about twice as much as this one does, possibly more. I simply resized in cura and the model fits really well. The end is tapered so that it fits snugly to hold it shut, but the rest should slide smoothly once open. I modified the handle to be able to pull on it, the one in the picture was a bit too narrow.

body_text: Die Reihenfolge der Suchergebnisse richtet sich nach den Parametern Datum der Anzeige, Preis, Verkäufer-Status (gewerblich / privat) und Entfernung. In der Standard-Sortierung "Neueste", ohne Eingabe eines Ortes, werden neueste Anzeigen oben gezeigt (alternativ änderbar auf "Niedrigster Preis" und "Höchster Preis"). Die Anzeigen werden mit Eingabe eines Ortes auf den Ort bzw. den Radius eingegrenzt. Falls ein Ort sowie ein Radius ausgewählt wurde, ist auch die Sortierung nach "Geringste Entfernung" möglich. Ganz oben oberhalb der Suchergebnisse werden zudem als solche gekennzeichnete Werbeanzeigen eingeblendet, die zu der Suche passen.

title: Term 1 BABS1201 Flashcards
meta_description: Study with Quizlet and memorize flashcards containing terms like to prevent refraction in 100x objective lens,, how to calculate total magnification of an image through microscope, how to calculate true diameter of field of view (microscope) and more.
body_text: Term 1 BABS1201 Save Groups Get a hint to prevent refraction in 100x objective lens, immersion oil is used - oil has same refractive index as glass - so light passes from slide through oil on objective lens without additional refraction 1 / 329 1 / 329 Created by evamai_ Created 2 months ago Terms in this set (329) to prevent refraction in 100x objective lens, immersion oil is used - oil has same refractive index as glass - so light passes from slide through oil on objective lens without additional refraction how to calculate total magnification of an image through microscope power of the objective lens (4x, 10x, 40x) x power of eyepiece how to calculate true diameter of field of view (microscope) diameter = field number / objective magnification how to accurately measure an object under microscope - using ruler, count the no. of eyepiece scale divisions (EPUs) - times that by these values: 25µm for 4x objective, 10µm for 10x objective, 2.5µm for 40x, 1µm for 100x how to calculate a scale for a biological drawing 1. measure no. of EPU's of a specific structure (eg length, width, diam) 2. convert EPU's to micrometers using conversion factor 3. measure the same bit (eg length, width, diam) on the paper drawing 4. work out: how many mm on my drawing = 1µm 5. times both sides of equation by no. (eg 10) that gives an appropriate length scale bar, then draw it tips for biological drawings - always draw what u see, not what u think u should see - large and clear using space provided - sharp HB pencil (NO PENS OR COLOUR, lines continuous - keep labels horizontal and to side of drawing - title for each drawing most abundant elements in universe H, He, O, C major elements of life C, H, N, Phosphorous, O, Sulfur present in quite uniform relative amounts across organisms overview: properties of water that make it essential for life 1. most biological molecules assume their shape and function in response to water's physical/chemical properties 2. water is the medium for most biochemical reactions (aqueous) 3. most reactions that support life involve water 4. living organisms are found where water is water adhesion the attraction of water molecules to polar surfaces eg glass water cohesion the attraction of water molecules to each other water forces: important for plant life cohesion: water cohesion helps form a continuous column in the xylem from roots to leaves adhesion: water attracted to walls of xylem vessels, helping column move upward against gravity water's surface tension - important for life - strong H bonding in water strengthens surface - some insects can "walk" on water's surface due to this water as universal solvent - important for life - water dissolves more substances than any other liquid, due to its polarity - essential role in transport, chemical reactions, waste removal, cell function density of water vs ice - important for life - unlike most liquids, water's density decreases as it freezes - ice has a spacious structure with fewer molecules per V than liquid water (each molecule H-bonded to 4 others in 3D) - floating ice is a barrier that insulates liquid water below from cooler air above → habitat for krill See more

title: AQA GCSE Psychology Paper 1 (2022) Flashcards
meta_description: adjusted for the advanced information Learn with flashcards, games, and more — for free.
body_text: Save Groups AQA GCSE Psychology Paper 1 (2022) 5.0 (1 review) Get a hint Other types of encoding Tactile Memory - what things feel like; olfactory memory - memory for smells 1 / 187 1 / 187 Created by Viren_7 Teacher Created 4 years ago adjusted for the advanced information Terms in this set (187) Other types of encoding Tactile Memory - what things feel like; olfactory memory - memory for smells "Murdock's Aim" To see if memory of words is affected by location in list "Murdock's Method" Participants listened to 20 word lists, with 10-40 words on them, and recalled words after each list. "Murdock's Results" Recall related to the position of words. Higher recall for the first words (primacy effect) and last words (recency effect) than in middle. "Murdock's Conclusion" Shows the serial position effect and supports the MSM model "Murdock's Evaluation 1" Controlled lab study - There was a high level of control so it could be concluded position of words determined recall. "Murdock's Evaluation 2" Artificial task - Word lists were used which is only one type of memory, so the study lacks validity "Murdock's Evaluation 3" "Supporting research, some amnesiacs can't store LTM and also do not show primacy effect, which shows the primacy effect is related to LTM (Carlesimo et al.)" Multi-store model Three memory stores: different coding, capacity and duration. Information moves through attention and verbal rehearsal. STM duration less than 30 seconds unless rehearsed Sensory memory coding appropriate to the sense sensory memory capacity very high capacity sensory memory duration only very briefly (half a second) unless paid attention too STM coding tends to be accoustic STM capacity 5-9 chunks of information See more

Sa superficie est de 158 km2 et elle est la troisième plus grande île des Antilles françaises, juste après la Guadeloupe et la Martinique. L'île de Marie-Galante est surnommée « l'île aux cent moulins » ou « la grande galette », à cause de sa forme arrondie de 15 km de diamètre. Marie-Galante bénéficie d’un climat tropical tempéré grâce aux influences maritimes aux vents des alizés et on distingue deux saisons à Marie-Galante : (i) une saison sèche appelée « carême » qui va de janvier à juin et (ii) une saison humide dite « hivernage », qui va de juillet à décembre. La moyenne des températures est de 27°C, avec une amplitude de 25 °C à 32 °C pour les mois les plus chauds et de 23 °C à 29 °C pour les mois les plus froids.
