Tecnología tras las Direcciones de Correo Electrónico Desechables Todo el mundo emplea una cuenta de correo electrónico, para cualquier cosa desde conectarse al trabajo hasta para mantener el contacto con amigos y compañeros, usando la dirección de correo como pasaporte digital. Hoy en día, cerca del 99% de las aplicaciones y servicios en los cuales nos registramos, requieren de una cuenta de correo, tanto para obtener tarjetas de fidelidad, como para sorteos y demás ofertas. Todos nosotros disfrutamos de la cuenta de correo, pero acumular diariamente toneladas de spam no es nada agradable. Además, es muy común que las tiendas tengan sus bases de datos hackeadas, lo cual pone en peligro tus direcciones de correo electrónico, siendo más fáciles de acabar en una lista de correo spam. Por el momento, ninguna operación llevada a cabo en la red es 100% privada, es por ello que necesitas proteger tu email de contacto y la mejor manera es empleando direcciones de correo electrónico desechables. Por tanto, ¿qué es una Dirección de Correo Electrónico Desechable? Recientemente, he encontrado una tasa de rebote más compleja de lo habitual en mis últimas difusiones de emails. Más tarde, me di cuenta de que el aumento de usuarios (O Bots) que se daban de alta en mis servicios, escondían su identidad real utilizando correos desechables. Las direcciones de correo electrónico desechables (DEA) técnicamente son una estrategia en la que los usuarios con una única cuenta de correo electrónico, obtienen otra cuenta de carácter temporal para que se almacene en tus registros de datos de contacto. Las DEA permiten crear una cuenta de correo que pase la validez necesaria para aquellos servicios y páginas web que te exigen un registro, sin revelar tu identidad. En caso de que las direcciones de correo peligren o reciban un exceso de spam, el propietario no podría resolver este problema de manera rápida y sin afectar a otros contactos. A través del email temporal, puedes recibir correos pertenecientes a cuentas falsas en tu auténtico correo durante un determinado periodo de tiempo. Las direcciones de correo falsas son simplemente correos temporales y autodestructivos. ¿Para qué necesitarías una cuenta de correo falsa? Ya debes de haberte dado cuenta de aquellos servicios como Amazon Prime, Hulu y Netflix permiten modelos de prueba durante cierto periodo de tiempo limitado, sin embargo si persistes en seguir utilizando aquellos servicios, lo único que necesitas es una cuenta de correo desechable. Haz click aquí para conseguir una. De esta manera, podrás extender tu periodo de prueba usando diferentes cuentas de correo vinculadas a tu cuenta original, después de que dicho periodo de prueba expire. Tanto las tiendas físicas como las tiendas online, suelen pedirte una cuenta de correo para poder beneficiarte de sus ofertas, sin embargo, esto tiende a acabar en una avalancha de spam no deseado que podrías haber evitado. Las direcciones de correo desechables te ayudan a deshacerte de esos mensajes tan irritantes que continuas recibiendo. Técnicamente, la idea de una dirección de correo temporal evoca a los hackers y al lado oscuro de internet, pero existen razones convincentes para usar este tipo de cuentas falsas. Si estás buscando algunas razones legales para usar una dirección de correo desechable, aquí tienes unas pocas: Solicitar una Tarjeta de Puntos: Si no quieres recibir correos promocionales de nuevos productos, utiliza una dirección de correo desechable en lugar de la original, de esta manera podrás deshacerte del correo spam. Además, en el caso de que la tienda fuese hackeada, tus datos permanecerían a salvo. Testar tu App: Acabas de crear una App para tu página web y quieres revisarla antes de ponerla a la venta. Para ello puedes crear fácilmente 100 direcciones de correo desechables, crea cuentas ficticias y téstala por ti mismo en lugar de tener que contratar usuarios de internet para que testen tu aplicación. Registro de Cuenta Doble en una Página Web: Necesitas otra cuenta IFTTT para programar una cuenta secundaria de Twitter que dirija la campaña de marketing de tu página. Una nueva cuenta necesitaría un email distinto para poder crearla, deshazte de tener que organizar una nueva bandeja de entrada consiguiendo una dirección de correo desechable en temp-mail.org Eliminar Spam: Una dirección de correo desechable es una herramienta muy útil contra el spam, especialmente para aquellos usuarios que acceden de manera constante a formularios web, foros y grupos de discusión. Gracias a este tipo de cuenta, podrás reducir el spam de forma definitiva. ¿Cómo Elegir un Correo desechable? El mejor proveedor de cuentas de correo falsas debería: Permitir a los usuarios crear cuentas de correo temporales en tan sólo un click. No solicitar registro o información personal del usuario. La cuenta de correo debería seguir siendo anónima Ofrecer más de una cuenta de correo (Tantas como tú quieras) Ofrecer un almacenamiento temporal del correo (Bandeja de entrada de la dirección de correo desechable a disposición del usuario) Un diseño sencillo y funcional para conseguir un correo corriente Ofrecer cuentas al azar para que el usuario puede escoger la dirección de correo que quiera. De este modo, gracias a temp-mail.org, tu servicio de correo favorito, conseguirás librarte del spam y ahorrar tiempo. ¿Cómo utilizar una Dirección de Correo Desechable? Los usuarios eligen conseguir este tipo de cuentas desechables creando un perfil nuevo en su proveedor habitual, como por ejemplo Gmail, pero esto conlleva algunos problemas como tener que manejar otra bandeja de entrada. Aquellos que optan por crear esta cuenta en servicios de email gratuitos, tienen que soportar una dirección de correo distinta. Sin embargo, una opción mejor sería si tuvieses una sola dirección de correo y varias cuentas desechables de temp-mail.org, todo ello organizado en una única bandeja de entrada. Lo más asombroso de una dirección de correo desechable es poder remitir todo tu correo a la cuenta original. En caso de que la dirección de correo desechable se vea en peligro y sospeches de uno de tus contactos, podrás enviar aquellos correos directamente a tu papelera, y en caso de necesidad, que se envíen directamente a tu bandeja de correo original. En conclusión: Tener un sistema de direcciones de correo desechables es una forma fantástica de asegurarte de que cuando participas en wikis online, chat rooms, foros o en servicios de compartir archivos, tu identidad nunca sea revelada ni vendida a nadie, evitando de esa manera el correo spam. Todo ello gracias a Temp-mail.org Todo el mundo emplea una cuenta de correo electrónico, para cualquier cosa desde conectarse al trabajo hasta para mantener el contacto con amigos y compañeros, usando la dirección de correo como pasaporte digital. title: Why ‘SNL’ standout Ashley Padilla is still a featured player and not a repertory cast member: A brief explainer
meta_description: She's gone viral this year thanks to several instantly iconic characters and sketches.
headings: Why is Ashley Padilla going viral?
body_text: Live from New York, it's... social media confusion. Ashley Padilla is arguably having the best second year on Saturday Night Live since Bowen Yang half a decade ago. Yang was the first featured player in the modern era to be nominated at the Emmys; he received four Best Comedy Supporting Actor bids (2021, '22, '24, and '25) and is eligible again this year despite leaving in December. 2026 American Music Awards: See the full list of categories Visualizing the Oscar odds: The 5 tightest races in 5 graphs The term "featured player" is getting a lot of attention right now on social platforms, with some SNL fans outraged to discover that Padilla, who joined the show in 2024, is still not a repertory cast member. But that's completely normal. Here's a brief explainer. Any time Lorne Michaels and the show's casting department hire fresh talent, the newbies are credited as featured players for their first two seasons. Only once they make it to their third season does their designation change to a repertory player. The original cast that began in 1975 were called "The Not Ready for Prime Time Players," but that moniker was dropped in the show's fourth season. Then in Season 5, a two-tiered casting structure was created for repertory and featured players, in order to distinguish between the established stars and the newbies. However, as with every rule, there are exceptions. Performers like Harry Shearer , Eddie Murphy , Kevin Nealon , Mike Myers , Chris Farley , Chris Rock , Julia Sweeney , Norm Macdonald , Ana Gasteyer , Tracy Morgan , Chris Parnell , Bill Hader , Andy Samberg , Jason Sudeikis , and Kristen Wiig were all promoted to star billing before meeting the two-year threshold. Most recently, in Season 39, Aidy Bryant and Cecily Strong became the latest outliers to receive repertory status after only one year on the show. Since it hasn't happened in more than a decade, don't expect Padilla or any of the entering cast to be given special treatment anytime soon. Casual viewers of SNL may get the impression that Padilla is the show's main character, thanks to how many sketches she's been in during Season 51. Superfan Mike Murray crunched the numbers and found out she's had one hour and four minutes of screen time this year so far, more than anyone else in the cast. Colin Jost is close behind in second place with a little over an hour — but he has a guaranteed presence as the host of "Weekend Update." That is why some viewers are scratching their heads each week when Darrell Hammond announces that Padilla, a former member of The Groundlings sketch comedy troupe, is "featuring" and in the second tier of cast members. Just give it time, fans — she should be promoted in Season 52, per tradition. Saturday Night Live currently has seven featured players. Padilla and Jane Wickline are in their second years, while Tommy Brennan , Jeremy Culhane , Ben Marshall , Kam Patterson , and Veronika Slowikowska are in their first years. Marshall was previously a staff writer and member of the on-screen comedy trio Please Don't Destroy (with John Higgins and Martin Herlihy ). Here is just a small sampling of fan reactions regarding Padilla's current "featured" billing: With so many people leaving after Season 50 — including Devon Walker , Emil Wakim , Michael Longfellow , Heidi Gardner , and Ego Nwodim — it was Padilla's time to rise to the occasion. And she did. Her breakout year is thanks to several instantly iconic characters and sketches, including the following. Her acting performance was highlighted when she played one-half of Two People Who Just Hooked Up with Andrew Dismukes . First appearing on Nov. 1 during "Weekend Update" to discuss the government shutdown, the characters couldn't stop flirting with each other to give Colin Jost a coherent answer. They returned on Jan. 31 to talk about the Super Bowl, and the same thing happened. Also on Jan. 31, Padilla started earning awards buzz for a "Mom Confession" sketch in which she played a MAGA mother who hesitantly admitted to her family, "I may have changed my mind … about Trump." Clayton Davis of Variety called her an "Emmy-worthy star" and heralded the scene as "the undeniable best moment of the season so far." On March 7, Padilla went viral with host Ryan Gosling in a "Passing Notes" sketch thanks to the writers switching out their jokes between rehearsal and the live show. The actors were cold-reading their lines, and couldn't help but laugh along with the audience as they said silly things about a gynecology exam, Ken from Barbie , spaghetti noodles in a plastic baggie, and more. The "Cyclops" sketch on March 7 also brought the laughs, both on-screen and off. Padilla and Slowikowska did their best to keep straight faces as the three dimwitted monsters — played by Gosling, Mikey Day , and Kenan Thompson — tried to push past them and open the vault to retrieve their treasure. "Stop! Go back!" Padilla repeatedly shouted while completely breaking character. Comedy Series Comedy Actress Comedy Actor Comedy Supporting Actress Comedy Supporting Actor Drama Series Drama Actress Drama Actor Drama Supporting Actress Drama Supporting Actor Limited Series Movie Movie/Limited Actress Movie/Limited Actor Movie/Limited Supporting Actress Movie/Limited Supporting Actor Season 51 wraps in May, which is when the 2026 Emmys season starts heating up. Per the Gold Derby data , Padilla is in the conversation for Best Comedy Supporting Actress, and she has an 11 percent chance of receiving an Emmy nomination. If true, she'd be the show's first female featured player nominee, and the second overall featured player nominee after Yang. Recent SNL supporting actresses nominated at the Emmy Awards include Amy Poehler , Kristen Wiig, Kate McKinnon , Vanessa Bayer , Leslie Jones , Aidy Bryant, and Cecily Strong. McKinnon won twice, in 2016 and '17. Will Padilla join that group? Find out when the Emmy nominations are announced July 8. title: Pablo Torre has his final shot at picking 16-1 upset on 'Around the Horn'
meta_description: Pablo Torre’s annual tradition of predicting a No. 16 seed to upset a No. 1 seed on 'Around the Horn' ends with this year's tournament.
body_text: UPDATE: Torre shared his pick. It is revealed in a video below. The 2025 NCAA Tournament will mark the final time ESPN’s Around the Horn will break down the action, as the show is set to end in May. With it goes Pablo Torre’s annual tradition: picking a No. 16 seed to upset a No. 1 seed. This hilarious tradition was highlighted in a compilation video shared by Around the Horn on social media Wednesday. Torre first made his bold prediction in 2013, selecting Southern University to pull off the historic 16-over-1 upset against Gonzaga — an upset that we now know never came to be. Undeterred, he continued picking No. 16 seeds to defeat No. 1s, even as the upsets remained elusive. The first-ever 16-over-1 upset happened in 2018 when UMBC stunned Virginia, followed by Fairleigh Dickinson’s shocking victory over Purdue in 2023. But Torre didn’t pick those correctly either. For those who are curious about every incorrect prediction from Torre over the years, here is the compilation that Around the Horn put together . His annual and final 16-over-1 pick coming soon👀 pic.twitter.com/hGZcZQGMXT — Around the Horn (@AroundtheHorn) March 19, 2025 One thing that Torre may have going in his favor going into the 2025 NCAA Tournament is that he hasn’t yet picked against any of the current No. 1 seeds, Florida, Houston, Duke and Auburn, in past years. So perhaps a new list of potential teams to pick against will give Torre new life in his last chance to correctly pick a 16-1 seed as an Around the Horn panelist. Will we see a third 16-1 upset in the 2025 NCAA Tournament? Time will tell. But given Torre’s track record, even if an upset happens, the chances it’s the one he picks are slim. 🗣️THE PICK IS IN https://t.co/YCqejfScc9 pic.twitter.com/phIRDDwX6A “I am picking the right pick this year, Alabama St. to upset Auburn. Alabama St. just beat Saint Francis with a buzzer-beater. Dramatic. Auburn just lost to Yale last year in the tournament — and Yale sucks. Also they lost three of four to end the season. Alabama St., the Hornets upsetting the Tigers. Bring me my justice.” Reice Shipley is a staff writer for Comeback Media that graduated from Ithaca College with a degree in Sports Media. He previously worked at Barrett Sports Media and is a fan of all things Syracuse sports.

title: Moment topless Bill Belichick caught sneaking from 24-year-old girlfriend's home
meta_description: BILL Belichick was caught sneaking out of a home on a Ring camera last year – and the residence is believed to belong to his current girlfriend. The New England Patriots iconic head coach, 72…
headings: The downsides of having a massive age gap relationship
body_text: BILL Belichick was caught sneaking out of a home on a Ring camera last year – and the residence is believed to belong to his current girlfriend. The New England Patriots iconic head coach, 72, is rumored to be dating 24-year-old former cheerleader Jordon Hudson . It’s been reported by the Daily Mail that Bill Belichick was caught sneaking out of the Massachusetts house of his girlfriend Hudson last November. Ring camera footage spotted the 72-year-old leaving the home without a shirt. Belichick opened and closed the door of the residence before walking down the front porch of the property. TMZ first reported the relationship between him and Hudson last week. Bill Belichick spotted with girlfriend for first time on Boston flight Belichick 'signed girlfriend's homework' during first meetup with love interest Belichick and the ex-cheerleader have been dating since 2022 after the two met on a flight from Boston to Florida in 2021, per the news outlet. It’s believed that the pair spoke about a philosophy project that Hudson had been working on at the time. Belichick and Hudson were pictured in a selfie that was taken during their first experience with each other in February of 2021, TMZ reported. Both smiled in the photo during their first reported encounter on a 2021 flight as Belichick reportedly began a conversation about the book that Hudson was reading . Travis Kelce makes fashion statement at Chiefs ceremony Steelers announce new QB1 with Aaron Rodgers' retirement status still up-in-air College football coach hits back at NFL prospect Ty Simpson over 'bad take' Rob Gronkowski's stunning girlfriend Camille Kostek amazes with Coachella outfit And TMZ also reported the six-time Super Bowl champion signed the then 21-year-old’s Deductive Logic textbook. The message read, “Jordan, thanks for giving me a course on logic! Safe travels! Bill Belichick.” TMZ’s photo of the note showed that Belichick listed the years of his six Super Bowl championships under his signature. Their report revealed that the pair shared contact info after the flight. It’s believed that Belichick and Hudson became a couple after his breakup from Linda Holliday , whom he dated for 16 years . Hudson was a competitive cheerleader at Bridgewater State University in Massachusetts. Belichick was spotted supporting her at a cheerleading competition in March. They also posed for a picture during a work trip to Croatia on a boat. Dr. Jenn Mann, MFT, a licesesed therapist, reveals the key issues experienced by romantic partners with a huge age gap between them. Dr. Mann claimed it might initially feel exciting when one partner is much younger than the other, like Bill Belichick, 72, and Jordon Hudson, 24, who share a whopping 48-year age difference. “When people get older, they might just think it is refreshing that a young woman is looking at them adoringly and is attracted to them and is interested in him,” Dr. Mann told The U.S. Sun. “[But] there is a huge gap in life experience — in just years on this planet, in the way that helps us to evolve, develop, and mature.” “Even the most mature young person, when there is a 40-year age gap, the older people are incredibly immature. “You’re not meeting in the middle regarding development and life experience.” Dr. Mann said one common consideration for older male partners would be the younger female wanting a father figure. “Usually, younger women are drawn to much older men because of unresolved issues with their fathers,” Dr. Mann added. “This could be a lack of attention, love, someone not being there, or even someone being in the household but not being present the way she might wish he was.” Dr. Mann noted that sexual and intimacy issues might begin to be at play, given the older partner may not have as much energy. What’s more, for older men, “it becomes more difficult typically to get and maintain an erection,” she said. “There are problems related to sexual performance, which can create other risks when it comes to taking medication for sexual performance.” Socially, Dr. Mann suggested that issues may arise between a couple when they mix with friends from different generations. “In this case, if they’re hanging with Bill’s friends in their seventies or even sixties and fifties, a 24-year-old woman at that table will likely struggle to find common ground socially,” she said. Last week, Hudson posted a picture of the coach at Tom Brady ‘s Patriots Hall of Fame induction ceremony. “You are the embodiment of virtue, dedication, humility and grace. I am deeply moved by you,” she wrote on an Instagram story post. It’s been rumored that Hudson, who’s also an entrepreneur and philosopher, has helped Belichick with his business endeavors.

body_text: Shayna Rose (born Shayna Rose Mordue ; November 3 , 1983 ) is a American actress and singer. She is best known for playing the original Marina on the live-action musical series, The Fresh Beat Band . Shayna moved to Los Angeles in 2002 after declining a scholarship to the Berklee College of Music in Boston. She went between acting, singing, songwriting, auditions, and two jobs. Shayna would make appearances in shows such as Gilmore Girls , Days of Our Lives , and Mad Men . Shayna was on The Fresh Beat Band from 2009-2010 in late 2010 after season 2's production performing in live shows as Marina till the summer of 2010 departing afterward she would leave The Fresh Beat Bands 2nd season to pursue other projects and to marry her now ex-husband Arthur Gradstein. She would be replaced by Tara Perry for Season 3 and the spin-off series Fresh Beat Band of Spies . Since her departure, she sang the song "Colorful World" for the Disney Channel movie Bad Hair Day , and created a short pilot called Shayna's Place . She's now focusing on writing and producing her own music.

title: Reason for calling shutdown() on ExecutorService
meta_description: I was reading about it quite a bit in the past couple of hours, and I simply cannot see any reason (valid reason) to call shutdown() on the ExecutorService, unless we have a humongous application t...
body_text: I was reading about it quite a bit in the past couple of hours, and I simply cannot see any reason ( valid reason) to call shutdown() on the ExecutorService , unless we have a humongous application that stores, dozens and dozens of different executor services that are not used for a long time. The only thing (from what I gather) the shutdown does, is doing what a normal Thread does once it's done. When the normal Thread will finish the run method of the Runnable(or Callable), it will be passed to Garbage Collection to be collected. With Executor Service the threads will simply be put on hold, they will not be ticked for the garbage collection. For that, the shutdown is needed. Ok back to my question. Is there any reason to call shutdown on ExecutorService very often, or even right after submitting to it some tasks? I would like to leave behind the case someone is doing it and right after that calls to awaitTermination() as this is validated. Once we do that, we have to recreate a new ExecutorService all over again, to do the same thing. Isn't the whole idea for the ExecutorService to reuse the threads? So why destroy the ExecutorService so soon? Isn't it a rational way to simply create ExecutorService (or couple depending on how many you need), then during the application running pass to them the tasks once they come along, and then on the application exit or some other important stages shutdown those executors? I'd like an answer from some experienced coders who do write a lot of asynchronous code using the ExecutorServices. Second side question, a bit smaller deals with the android platform. IF some of you will say that it's not the best idea to shutdown executors every time, and your program on android, could you tell me how do you handle those shutdowns (to be specific - when you execute them) when we deal with different events of the application life cycle. Because of the CommonsWare comment, I made the post neutral. I really am not interested in arguing about it to death and it seems it's leading there. I'm only interested in learning about what I asked here from experienced developers if they are willing to share their experiences. Thanks. 4 "I see many times sample codes, in which all the time, there is a shutdown() invocation right after submiting or executing tasks" -- feel free to use hyperlinks to provide evidence of your claims. Personally, I have never seen any "sample codes" that do what you state. It is possible that you are misinterpreting something, and we can only point that out to you if we know what "sample codes" you are examining. CommonsWare – CommonsWare 2013-04-20 17:49:01 +00:00 Commented Apr 20, 2013 at 17:49 10 Hi CommonsWare. First of all, I see an aggresive tone of yours(or so that seems) towards me, which I think is not validated here. I was not trying to portrait people in negative way. As for your quote, I was mostly talking about the Thinking In Java IV edition, Multitasking part. You can find many instances of that in Bruce Eckel's examples. They are mostly simple, but never the less the impression Bruce put on me, was to use shutdown very often. In any way, you focused on something that was not the main part of my post. I removed those parts cause I really don't wish to argue about it. Lucas – Lucas 2013-04-20 20:13:30 +00:00 Commented Apr 20, 2013 at 20:13 3 hay @CommonsWare in Thinking in java book by Bruce Eckel..in concurrency/Executor page 804 Fourth Edition , he always use shutdown() method right after submitting or executing tasks in simple apps to illustrate how Executor works as Lucas said Error – Error 2015-11-12 09:36:24 +00:00 Commented Nov 12, 2015 at 9:36 2 I know this is an old post, but I think the OP's question still stands and is valid. I've also come across many sample codes where "there is a shutdown() invocation right after execute()". tutorials.jenkov.com/java-util-concurrent/executorservice.html (first tutorial that comes up when you google "java executorservice example") baekacaek – baekacaek 2015-12-10 20:52:21 +00:00 Commented Dec 10, 2015 at 20:52 Thank you, I had the same question that raised with these "sample codes". journaldev.com/2340/… Gregordy – Gregordy 2016-10-17 16:58:39 +00:00 Commented Oct 17, 2016 at 16:58 The shutdown() method does one thing: prevents clients to send more work to the executor service. This means all the existing tasks will still run to completion unless other actions are taken. This is true even for scheduled tasks, e.g., for a ScheduledExecutorService: new instances of the scheduled task won't run. It also frees up any background thread resources. This can be useful in various scenarios. Let's assume you have a console application which has an executor service running N tasks. If the user hits CTRL-C, you expect the application to terminate, possibly gracefully. What does it mean gracefully? Maybe you want your application to not be able to submit more tasks to the executor service and at the same time you want to wait for your existing N tasks to complete. You could achieve this using a shutdown hook as a last resort: This hook will shutdown the service, which will prevent your application to submit new tasks, and wait for all the existing tasks to complete before shutting down the JVM. The await termination will block for 5 seconds and return true if the service is shutdown. This is done in a loop so that you're sure the service will shutdown eventually. The InterruptedException gets swallowed each time. This is the best way to shutdown an executor service that gets reused all over your application. This code isn't perfect. Unless you're absolutely positive your tasks will eventually terminate, you might want to wait for a given timeout and then just exit, abandoning the running threads. In this case it would make sense to also call shutdownNow() after the timeout in a final attempt to interrupt the running threads ( shutdownNow() will also give you a list of tasks waiting to run). If your tasks are designed to respond to interruption this will work fine. Another interesting scenario is when you have a ScheduledExecutorService that performs a periodic task. The only way to stop the chain of periodic tasks is to call shutdown() . EDIT: I'd like to add that I wouldn't recommend using a shutdown hook as shown above in the general case: it can be error-prone and should be a last resort only. Moreover, if you have many shutdown hooks registered, the order in which they will run is undefined, which might be undesirable. I'd rather have the application explicitly call shutdown() on InterruptedException . Isn't the whole idea for the ExecutorService to reuse the threads? So why destroy the ExecutorService so soon? Yes. You should not destroy and re-create ExecutorService frequently. Initialize ExecutorService when you require (mostly on start-up) and keep it active until you are done with it. Yes. It's rational to shutdown ExecutorService on important stages like application exit etc. Second side question, a bit smaller deals with android platform. IF some of you will say that it's not best idea to shutdown executors every time, and you program on android, could you tell me how you handle those shutdowns (to be specific, when you execute them) when we deal with different events of application life cycle. Assume that ExecutorService is shared across different Activities in your application. Each activity will be paused/resumed at different intervals of time and still you need one ExecutorService per your application. Instead of managing the state of ExecutorService in Activity life cycle methods, move ExecutorService management ( Creation/Shutdown) to your custom Service . Create ExecutorService in Service => onCreate() and shutdown it properly in onDestroy() Recommended way of shutting down ExecutorService : How to properly shutdown java ExecutorService An ExecutorService should be shut down once it is no longer needed to free up system resources and to allow graceful application shutdown. Because the threads in an ExecutorService may be nondaemon threads, they may prevent normal application termination. In other words, your application stays running after completing its main method. Reason for calling shutdown() on ExecutorService Today I encountered a situation where I have to wait until a machine is ready, before starting a series of tasks on that machine. I make a REST call to this machine, if I don't receive 503 (Server Unavailable) then the machine is ready to process my requests. So, I wait until I get 200 (Success) for the first REST call. There are multiple ways to achieve it, I used ExecutorService to create a thread and scheduled it to run after every X Seconds. So, I need to stop this thread on a condition, check this out... Second side question, a bit smaller deals with android platform. Maybe I can answer if you'll provide bit more context! Also from my experience with Android development it's rarely you need Threads. Are you developing a Game or an app which needs threads for performance? If not, in Android you have other ways to tackle problems like the scenario that I explained above. You can rather use TimerTask, AsyncTask or Handlers or Loaders based on context. This is because if UIThread waits for long you know what happens :/ This is genuine notwithstanding for planned undertakings, e.g., for a ScheduledExecutorService: new cases of the booked assignment won't run. We should expect you have a comfort application which has an agent administration running N errands. I'm not catching it's meaning effortlessly? Perhaps you need your application to not have the option to submit more assignments to the agent administration and in the meantime you need to sit tight for your current N undertakings to finish. Except if you're totally positive your errands will in the end, you should need to sit tight for a given break and after that simply exit, deserting the running strings. In the event that your activitys are intended to react to interference this will work fine. Another intriguing situation is the point at which you have a ScheduledExecutorService that plays out an activity. The best way to stop the chain of activity is to call shutdown() I recently found in my code that if I'm not using shutdown() after finishing some parallel work, the threads are not closed automatically, even if the parallel work was completed. This created many unused threads that eventually crashed the code due to out-of-memory problems. Find the answer to your question by asking.

title: Password Cracking in Active Directory - GeeksforGeeks
meta_description: Your All-in-One Learning Portal: GeeksforGeeks is a comprehensive educational platform that empowers learners across domains-spanning computer science and programming, school education, upskilling, commerce, software tools, competitive exams, and more.
headings: 2. Reduce the attack surface with least privilege
4. Block or limit NTLM where possible
body_text: In this lesson we’ll move from theory to hands-on practice with Pass-the-Hash and password-dumping techniques in Active Directory environments. You’ll learn how to collect NTLM hashes (using crackmapexec and secretsdump.py ), crack weak passwords with Hashcat, and then attempt lateral movement using recovered credentials all inside a controlled lab. This module also covers how to correctly interpret results (false positives and SMB signing quirks) and, critically, shows practical mitigations you can apply to harden real networks. Lets start with Crackmapexec Tool, to install this tool enter this command: We have windows 10 machine running and Server running in the background. To run this tool we need to provide target or range, domain, users, and password (if available). Now lets verify what our status is with the available users of the GFG.local domain. We will use Franklin as the User and Password1 as the password which we previously cracked it. Now Enter this command below: Here you can notice that PC1 is pawned in the GFG.local but the user is not pawned on the Domain controller because of SMB signing enabled. For password dumping we can also use secretsdump.py tool which is the part of impackets toolkit. To do so Enter these commands to fetch all the SAM hashes of PC1 and PC2: Save these NTLM hashes in a text file. We will then try to crack them or use them to determine where we can move laterally in the network (NOTE: NTLMv2 hashes cannot be used for lateral movement). I’ve included only the Administrator and user account hashes here because they are our primary targets. Previously, we learned how to crack hashes using Hashcat installed on the host machine due to limited resources in the virtual machine. You will be able to crack the passwords if they appear in rockyou.txt . We used simple passwords, which is why they were recoverable with a basic wordlist; in larger scenarios you might use password spraying or custom wordlists derived from leaked password lists. Now we will use these hashes to pivot around the network using crackmapexec tool. We will use the local network range, with user Franklin and password hash of Franklin (Paste the last segement from the hash dump for franklin user.) This might give false positives or login failures but possibility is there that there might be a green flag to a particular log in attempt. Ensure local administrator and service account passwords are unique on every host. Use automated tools (e.g., Microsoft LAPS or a PAM solution) to randomize and rotate local admin passwords. Restrict membership of local Administrators and Domain Admins to the smallest possible set. Use tiered administrative accounts (separate accounts for workstations, servers, and domain admin tasks). Enable Windows Defender Credential Guard / virtualization-based security to block many memory-dumping tools. Enable LSA protection ( RunAsPPL ) to make it harder for processes to access LSA secrets. Disable NTLM authentication or restrict it to legacy systems only. Configure Group Policy to restrict NTLM usage and prevent NTLM authentication over the network. Require MFA for interactive and administrative logons, especially for remote access and privileged accounts. MFA prevents simple reuse of NTLM hashes for interactive compromise. Require SMB signing and harden SMB services to reduce relay and credential capture opportunities. Disable unnecessary remote protocols and administrative shares on endpoints. Use a PAM/jump-host model for sensitive operations: check out privileged credentials only when needed, with automatic rotation and auditing. Avoid storing long-lived plaintext credentials on admin machines. Require admins to perform sensitive tasks from hardened, isolated machines (PAWs) that do not browse the web or run email clients. Log and alert on unusual authentication patterns: unexpected lateral logons, local account logons on multiple hosts, or use of built-in admin accounts. Deploy EDR and Sysmon to catch credential-dumping tools (Mimikatz), suspicious process trees, and abnormal authentication events. Regularly rotate high-value account credentials (including KRBTGT keys where needed). Keep systems patched, especially where credential dumping vulnerabilities have been fixed.

title: Kristen Heitzmann - Book Series In Order
meta_description: Complete order of Kristen Heitzmann books in Publication Order and Chronological Order.
headings: Publication Order of Rocky Mountain Legacy Books
Publication Order of Diamond of the Rockies Books
Publication Order of Spencer Family Books
Publication Order of Michelli Family Books
Publication Order of Told You Books
body_text: Living in Colorado, America, the novelist Kristen Heitzmann is an inspirational novelist of romance and mystery stories. Often using elements of the historical novel too, she weaves her mysteries and romances with the past, taking them into new and interesting realms. This goes some way towards explaining how it is that she has come to have such an extensive following worldwide, with readers from far and wide appreciating her work. Winning numerous awards and nominations for her stories, her faith is also an important part of her work, shaping much of her output and ideas. Over the years this has lead to her becoming one of the most prolific writers in the field contemporary Christian fiction, as she works with the American Christian Fiction Writer’s Conference. Truly writing from the heart, Heitzmann manages to imbue her work with a sense of passion and urgency quite unlike any other. Transporting her readers to far off worlds, she really manages to bring her stories to life, allowing them to speak directly straight to her audience. A keen mountain hiker, as well as an artist, she is always on the lookout for new and exciting ideas to transform into her next bestselling book. Getting into the minds of her characters, she also really works at creating a level of psychological suspense as well, really allowing each and every one of them to resonate off the page. This is all set to continue, as her writing career reaches brand new heights, taking her work to a whole new level in the following years to come. Born in 1959 Kristen Heitzmann grew up with a keen passion for both reading and writing from an early age, nurturing it through the years. Growing up in an academic family, she was raised surrounded by all manner of art and culture, as they would love books and music of all kinds. This would shape her over the years, feeding her imagination, allowing her to become the writer that she currently is today. Over time she would take to reading voraciously too, creating her own set of characters along the way, which would, in time, go back into her own work. Immersing herself in literature, she would invest herself heavily in her craft, creating the voice that many people would come to know and love her for. Constantly on the lookout for new and exciting ideas, Heitzmann also likes to take hikes in the mountains of Colorado in order to help clear her head. It is here in the Colorado Rockies that she typically finds her next source of inspiration for her next great bestseller to hit the market. Currently living in Colorado to this very day, she continues to put out work at a regular and ever consistent pace, something she will continue to do for a long time yet. Marking her grand debut onto the literary scene with the title ‘Honor’s Pledge’, Kristen Heitzmann would begin her writing career by starting the ‘Rocky Mountain’s Legacy’. Set in a location that was close to her heart, she would begin by writing a romance novel set firmly within the inspirational mold. Providing a strong and positive message, it would center itself around themes and ideas that would continue to reoccur throughout her career. Using the template of the classic romance, she would develop it throughout the following years, providing a number of other series and stand-alone titles too. These would include the ‘Diamond of the Rockies’ titles, the ‘Spencer Family’ stories, the ‘Michelli Family’ novels and the ‘Told You’ series. In time all of this would allow her to gradually build upon her profile, becoming the bestselling author that she currently is today. Winning numerous awards and nominations, she would becoming a leading figure in the industry as a whole, speaking about her craft on a variety of different occasions. Her books continue to stand the test of time too, with their messages living on, as her novels carry on getting discovered from readers far and wide, with her career growing from strength-to-strength. Initially published through the ‘Bethany House Publishers’ label, this was the fifth and final story to come out in the ‘Rocky Mountain Legacy’ series of novels. Wrapping the entire franchise up, it manages to bring all of the characters together, allowing them to finalize all the loose ends of the overall series. Working as a historical romance too, it is ideal for any fans of Heitzmann, as well as advocates of the genre, as it also sets a milestone as her first series completed. Dealing with themes of love and loss, this takes a look at what it takes to put the past in the past to make a new life and start again. Finalising all the elements of the series, this takes the relationship of Abbie and Cole, as they have to move on with her son and adopted niece that they will raise together. Taking place on the western frontier, its a historical romance which uses the era as its backdrop and canvas to the story itself. With the love growing day-by-day between Abbie and her second husband Cole, this sees her attempting to move on from her mourning as a widow. Trying to move on from her past and make a new life for herself, she is still attempting to get over the brutal death of her first husband. Set on the classic western frontier of yesteryear, this is the final chapter, as the relationship between the two of them is finally sealed. Will they overcome their pasts and start afresh? Can Cole Jasper create a new life for them both? How will they fare and will they receive honor’s reward? First published through the ‘Waterbrook Press’ publishing label, this was originally brought out in 2011 on the 3rd of May. Operating purely as a stand-alone title, this is not a part of any series as such, working purely on its own merits with its own story. It is a must for any fans of the author as well, as it was released later on in her writing career, showing how far she had come and how her style had developed overall. Taking place in Colorado, this sees Trevor MacDaniel, a search-and-rescue volunteer, as he saves a young boy from a mountain lion. Meeting the boy’s aunt, Natalie Reeve, she’s a sculptor with a rather unusual gift of her own, along with her own troubled past. Overcoming differences, they must deal with the obstacles ahead of them, whilst dealing with their own turbulent histories. How are they endangered though? Can they overcome their troubled histories and begin a relationship together? Just how are they indelible?

title: Trumpy CNBC Reporter Offers Grim Forecast of U.S. Economy
meta_description: CNBC’s Trump-loving reporter Rick Santelli is sounding the alarm on the economy—warning that it’s shaping up to be the “worst of both worlds.” The conservative financial journalist told viewers Wednesday that the U.S. may be sliding under President Donald Trump into a “stagflation,” a combination of high inflation and sluggish economic growth. Fresh data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics backs up that gloom. It shows that wholesale prices, measured by the Producer Price Index, rose by 3.4 perc
headings: Trumpy CNBC Reporter Offers Grim Forecast of U.S. Economy
body_text: CNBC’s Trump-loving reporter Rick Santelli is sounding the alarm on the economy—warning that it’s shaping up to be the “worst of both worlds.” The conservative financial journalist told viewers Wednesday that the U.S. may be sliding under President Donald Trump into a “stagflation,” a combination of high inflation and sluggish economic growth. Fresh data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics backs up that gloom. It shows that wholesale prices, measured by the Producer Price Index, rose by 3.4 percent in February compared with a year earlier. Even excluding swings in energy and food prices, the figures released Wednesday are higher than economists previously thought. Santelli warned that higher-than-expected inflation combined with poor growth numbers “is almost the worst of both worlds,” pointing to “a spat of fairly weak numbers” across the board. And it could get worse. The data does not capture the price increases caused by the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, which has effectively shut off the Strait of Hormuz, flatlining oil tanker traffic in one of the most critical shipping lanes on earth. The price of a barrel of Brent crude oil rose from $73 before the U.S. and Israel began strikes on Iran to $116. It has since settled slightly to just over $100. The situation echoes the last time the term stagflation was widely used, during Jimmy Carter’s presidency, when the Iranian revolution brought surging energy prices to the U.S., deepening a nationwide economic dip. With the new data roiling financial sentiment, the Federal Reserve voted Wednesday to keep its benchmark interest rate as-is. In a press conference following the rate announcement, Fed chair Jerome Powell said it was too soon to know the war’s impacts. Powell has faced relentless calls from President Trump to lower interest rates. When he refused to fold to the pressure, The White House launched a criminal probe into Powell. A federal judge, James Boasberg, struck down subpoenas related to the effort, slamming the Department of Justice in his opinion. “The Government has offered no evidence whatsoever that Powell committed any crime other than displeasing the President,” Boasberg wrote. The White House’s pressure campaign even drew some Republican opposition. Sen. Thom Tillis of North Carolina, who is not running for reelection, called the probe into Powell “weak and frivolous.” The Fed and inflation aren’t the only metrics fueling concern over the future of the U.S. economy. The job market is slowing, too. A report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics showed the unemployment rate reaching 4.4 percent in February, with 92,000 jobs lost.

title: Jermaine Johnson
meta_description: Ballotpedia: The Encyclopedia of American Politics
body_text: Jermaine Johnson ( Democratic Party ) is a member of the South Carolina House of Representatives , representing District 52 . He assumed office on November 11, 2024. His current term ends on November 9, 2026. Johnson ( Democratic Party ) is running for election for Governor of South Carolina . He declared candidacy for the Democratic primary scheduled on June 9, 2026 . [source] 4 Elections 4.1 2026 4.1.1 Endorsements 4.2 2024 4.2.1 Campaign finance 4.2.2 Endorsements 4.3 2022 4.4 2020 4.2 2024 4.2.1 Campaign finance 4.2.2 Endorsements 5 Campaign themes 5.1 2026 5.2 2024 5.3 2022 5.4 2020 Jermaine Johnson was born in Los Angeles, California , and lives in Columbia, South Carolina. Johnson earned a bachelor's degree in communication from the College of Charleston in 2008, a master's degree in project management from Strayer University in 2014, and a doctorate in business administration from Northcentral University in 2018. His career experience includes owning Dream Team Consulting Firm and working as a consultant, a business investor, and a global career development facilitator. Johnson served on the board of the Richland County Recreation Commission and the board of the Richland County Recreation Foundation, and has been affiliated with Capital City Lodge #47, the NAACP, and Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity. [1] [2] [3] Johnson was assigned to the following committees: House Legislative Oversight Committee , Chairman Medical, Military, Public and Municipal Affairs Committee The following table lists bills this person sponsored as a legislator, according to BillTrack50 and sorted by action history. title: The Bob & Tom Show is back on Fort Wayne radio
meta_description: A radio show familiar to Fort Wayne listeners is back on the air.
body_text: FORT WAYNE, Ind. (WANE) A radio show familiar to Fort Wayne listeners is back on the air. The Bob & Tom Show started airing again locally Monday on FM station 96.3XKE from 6 a.m. to 10 a.m. The show has its roots in Indianapolis where it started in the mid 1980’s. It began national syndication in 1995. The show occupies the time slot formerly held by Fort Wayne radio icon Jack Hammer, who was let go on March 2.

headings: 全国の“ご当地土産”にカビ発生…… 「深くお詫び」メーカー謝罪 36種類、10万箱自主回収へ
プロ野球・日本ハムのお菓子などが対象
body_text: 製菓メーカーの豊上製菓（長野県上田市）は、製造を手がける全国の「ご当地土産」の一部にカビが発生したとして、対象36商品を自主回収すると 発表 しました。 自主回収を行うのは、プロ野球・北海道日本ハムファイターズのお菓子「ツムコロ B・B ココア味」、各地で販売されているビスケット「ふくえくぼ」、「上高地 かっぱのやみつきスイーツ」など36種類のご当地土産。 消費者庁リコール情報サイト によると、6月6日から販売された10万8830箱が対象です。 同社は、一部の商品にカビが発生したとして、万全を期して自主回収を実施すると説明。発表日（12月4日）時点で健康被害はないとのことです。該当商品が手元にある場合は、下記問い合わせ先まで連絡してほしいとしています。 同社は「お客様には多大なるご迷惑をお掛けいたしますこと、深くお詫び申し上げます」と謝罪しています。

headings: Comment puis-je jouer à Ragdoll Drop gratuitement ?
Puis-je jouer à Ragdoll Drop sur appareils mobiles et ordinateurs de bureau ?
body_text: Ragdoll Drop est un jeu de physique où le chaos est roi ! Jetez votre personnage instable dans des fosses, écrasez-le contre des murs, faites-le tournoyer sur des manèges déjantés ou propulsez-le à bord de fusées : tout est permis ! Testez des véhicules loufoques comme des caddies et des catapultes, accomplissez des quêtes, débloquez des skins secrets et découvrez une infinité de façons farfelues de casser votre poupée de chiffon. Avec des commandes simples, un humour absurde et un fun imprévisible, Ragdoll Drop, c'est du chaos rebondissant à l'état pur ! Prêt à plonger dans la folie ? Ragdoll Drop est un jeu créé par Ericetto. Découvrez ses autres jeux sur [plateforme manquante]. Poki: Ragdoll Hit ! Vous pouvez jouer gratuitement à Ragdoll Drop sur Poki. Ragdoll Drop est jouable sur ordinateur et appareils mobiles tels que téléphones et tablettes.

title: Good riddance to the inspectors general
meta_description: President Trump’s attack on federal oversight has been met with criticism, but the oversight regime has been broken long before Trump, and it is time for reform to focus on outcomes rather th…
headings: Good riddance to the inspectors general
body_text: President Trump’s attack on federal oversight continues. In September, the administration temporarily defunded the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency (CIGIE), an umbrella group for inspectors general. In October, Trump fired Parisa Salehi , the Senate-confirmed inspector general of the Export-Import Bank. Backlash was swift. A dramatic report from CBS News warned that cutting the council’s funding would “mean a further hollowing out” of government oversight. MSNBC went further, declaring , “the system of inspectors general is now effectively broken.” If true, that might be a good thing. I am sympathetic to those who believe that inspectors general are vital: I worked for one of the inspectors general fired by Trump. But I am also sickened to see so many people offer defense to an oversight regime that, in its current form, has done little to earn it. The inspector general system was broken long before Trump supposedly tore it apart. It is long past time for reform. Few seem to remember now, but there was a moment when some experts were actually calling for less oversight, not more. That was because the existing system was both too cumbersome and too fixated on assessing programs against metrics that ultimately didn’t matter. They had a point. Most inspector general products miss the bigger picture, analyzing minutia instead of strategic outcomes. The result? Rather than focusing on self-evident structural problems, inspectors general obsess over flaws in individual programs that, on their own, are mostly irrelevant. I invite anyone to actually read a random performance audit or evaluation report from any inspector general. Inevitably, you will find it either states the obvious in mealy-mouthed ways or qualifies and equivocates to the point of saying nothing. A recent example : It is common knowledge that U.S. military assistance has helped Ukraine fend off a superior Russian enemy. So do we really need auditors to tell us that the departments of Defense and State “generally administered and managed [foreign military financing] funds for the 4 Ukraine [foreign military financing] cases we reviewed, valued at $243 million, from a total of 11 cases, valued at $282 million, in accordance with established processes?” In addition to absurdly bad writing, findings like this underscore the blinkered focus of inspectors general. The end goal of providing arms to Ukraine is not to follow a process but to help Ukraine win a war. If that goal is achieved, it will render U.S. security assistance to Ukraine unambiguously successful — even if “established processes” were not followed. According to the most recent annual report of the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency, taxpayers fork over at least $3.9 billion per year for insipid findings like this. That’s in the ballpark of what it took each year to fund Afghan security forces against a determined Taliban insurgency. The council claims an 18-fold return on the cost to fund inspectors general. But this calculation is sleight of hand, based on purported “monetary accomplishments,” which includes significant potential savings that may never be realized. More specifically, the preponderance of the supposed “return” consists of “questioned” costs that are not necessarily recovered at all, and funds that could be “put to better use” — that is, monies that theoretically could be spent more efficiently but which aren’t necessarily recovered or repurposed to better effect. To underscore the distinction between actual and potential monetary recoveries: the Special Inspector General for Afghanistan Reconstruction questioned more than $541 million in agency costs. Only 6.4 percent of that amount — about $35 million — was subsequently disallowed. The upshot? It is entirely unclear how valuable inspectors general actually are. In May of this year, I submitted a Freedom of Information Act request to the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency for documents substantiating the purported “monetary accomplishments” of inspectors general. The council delayed the request for months and has not provided any such documents. The much greater problem, however, is the flawed premise of this oversight, that U.S. programs will succeed if they simply adhere to laws, regulations and agency procedures. Good compliance might be important, but it doesn’t offset bad public policy. On the contrary, it can cloak bad policy in a smokescreen of respectability. Perhaps that is why the federal government routinely runs massive fiscal deficits despite “economy and efficiency” being a key statutory purpose of inspectors general. To save itself in the long run, the oversight community must drop its obsession with process over outcomes. It must also do much more to account for and scrutinize the bigger picture. Individual programs may be important, but they only really matter if they aggregate to strategic effects. Finally, inspectors general must be much more honest about how much money they really save. If inspectors general prove incapable of taking these simple steps to make their work more impactful and prove their indispensability, we should all applaud their demise. Dan Fisher is the former deputy director of the Lessons Learned Program at the Special Inspector General for Afghanistan Reconstruction. He is a graduate of Harvard Business School and the Harvard Kennedy School. He served in Afghanistan as an infantry officer. Copyright 2026 Nexstar Media Inc. . This material may not be published, broadcast, rewritten, or redistributed.

title: Every Time Taylor Swift References Marriage on ‘The Tortured Poets Department’
meta_description: Don Arnold/TAS24/Getty Images for TAS Rights Management Taylor Swift often sings about taking the next step in relationships, and she didn’t shy away from the topic of marriage on her latest album, Th...
headings: ‘My Boy Only Breaks His Favorite Toys’
body_text: Taylor Swift's latest album, The Tortured Poets Department, contains lyrical references to marriage and relationships, including mentions of wedding rings and wedding attire. Swift alludes to her past relationships with Joe Alwyn and Matty Healy in various songs on the album, detailing feelings of betrayal, longing, and uncertainty about the future. The album features tracks that explore themes of love, heartbreak, and the complexities of romantic relationships, drawing inspiration from famous musicians, poets, and personal experiences. Taylor Swift often sings about taking the next step in relationships, and she didn’t shy away from the topic of marriage on her latest album, The Tortured Poets Department . After TTPD dropped on April 19, eagle-eyed fans — or Swifties — were quick to point out lyrical parallels about wedding rings . On Swift’s 2019 album Lover , she sang about marrying her beau with “paper rings,” whereas on TTPD she crooned about “imaginary rings” in “Fresh Out the Slammer.” In the song, Swift seemingly detailed how she reconnected with Matty Healy after her split from longtime boyfriend Joe Alwyn . But fans have theorized that other tunes from TTPD are about Alwyn and their six-year relationship — which ended before a trip down the aisle. Aside from lyrical references, Swifties also noticed that the pop star donned a white gown — which could have been a wedding dress — in her “Fortnight” music video with Post Malone . Scroll down for every time Swift alluded to marriage on TTPD : In the album’s title track, Swift made several nods to famous musicians and poets, while singing about chaotic love. She crooned, “At dinner, you take my ring off my middle finger / And put it on the one people put wedding rings on / And that's the closest I've come to my heart exploding.” Using a metaphor for a child no longer wanting to play with a broken toy, Swift sang, “He saw forever, so he smashed it up, oh, oh.” Every Song Taylor Swift Wrote About Joe Alwyn on ‘Tortured Poets Department’: ‘So Long, London,’ More Swift sang about parting ways with British born Alwyn. “You swore that you loved me, but where were the clues? I died on the altar waitin' for the proof,” she crooned. While seemingly defending her relationship with Healy, Swift sang, “No, you can't come to the wedding / I know he's crazy but he's the one I want.” Swift seemingly detailed the end of her relationship with Alwyn, singing, “At the park where we used to sit on children's swings / Wearing imaginary rings.” Swift alluded to feelings of betrayal over her relationship with Alwyn coming to a close. “If you know it in one glimpse, it's legendary / You and I go from one kiss to getting married,” she sang in the chorus, while in the second verse she added, “You s–t-talked me under the table / Talkin' rings and talkin' cradles.” Every Song About Matty Healy on Taylor Swift’s ‘The Tortured Poets Department’ Swift considered trying to win her lover back, or being angry with the situation, singing, “Whether I'm gonna be your wife or / Gonna smash up your bike, I haven't decided yet.” Swift made a reference to the nursery rhyme “K-I-S-S-I-N-G”: “First comes love, then comes marriage, then comes the baby in the baby carriage.” She sang, “Leaving me bereft and reeling / My beloved ghost and me / Sitting in a tree / D-Y-I-N-G.” In one of Swift’s tracks about her current boyfriend , Travis Kelce , she made a reference to a viral video of the NFL player being asked to play “Marry, Kiss, Kill,” with her, Ariana Grande and Katy Perry in 2016. (At the time, Kelce choose to “kiss” Swift, “kill” Grande and “marry” Perry.) “Are you gonna marry, kiss, or kill me (Kill me) / It’s just a game, but really (Really) / I’m bettin’ on all three for us two (All three),” she crooned.

title: 『【2026年度版】新中一向け ソフトテニスのおすすめラケット編！』
meta_description: 新年度はソフトテニスを始める絶好のタイミング！ 春になると、部活でソフトテニスを始める中学生が一気に増えます。でも、新しいスポーツって分からないことだらけ…
headings: 【2026年度版】新中一向け ソフトテニスのおすすめラケット編！
body_text: 春になると、部活でソフトテニスを始める中学生が一気に増えます。 でも、新しいスポーツって分からないことだらけで不安になりますよね・・・ そこで今回は、 ソフトテニス初心者がまず揃えるべき道具 と、 その中でも特に重要な ラケット選び を分かりやすく紹介します！ ☆必須 ラケット これがないと始まらない！ ☆必須 テニスシューズ ケガ防止のためにも絶対必要 ◎推奨 グリップテープ・エッジガード ケガ防止＆ラケット保護に必須級 ソフトテニスのラケットは、各メーカーからたくさんの種類が出ています。 その中でも、初心者〜上級者まで幅広く支持されているのが YONEX（ヨネックス） と MIZUNO（ミズノ） 今回は、 業界シェアNo.1のYONEX に絞って、 中学生が選びやすい 4種類のラケット を紹介します。 ※掲載ショップは2/24時点の最安値を参考にしています。 YONEXが入門者向けに販売しているラケットで、軽くて振りやすいのが特徴です。 2021年3月に発売されたエアライドは毎年2,3月に新色が発売されています。 カラーバリエーションも豊富で選ぶのが楽しくなっちゃいます！ そしてガットが張り上げ済なので購入してすぐ使用できます！ ※ガットとはボールを打つ網の部分で、中級者以上向けだとラケット購入時は張ってないことが多いです。 楽天市場 ショップ：KPI 様 10,868円 ※5%オフクーポン適用(2/24現在) 楽天市場 ショップ：太陽スポーツ・Rampjack Trip店 様 7,990円 (2/24現在) 2026年3月に発売される中級者向けラケットとなります！ 弾き重視で上級者に人気のボルトレイジシリーズですが VSはオールラウンドモデルの為、前後衛どちらになっても長く使えるラケットです！ メリット ・中級者向けではあるが軽く適度な撓りがあるので扱いやすい ・中古での買取価格が高い(メルカリで1.5万円程) デメリット ・入門者向けに比べ高価（エアライドの1.8倍程) ・ガットを張らなければならない MSフォースというガットが無料のガットなので好きな色を選んで、 テンションは 25ポンドがオススメです。 中学生では一番人気(個人的見解)のシリーズです！ ジオブレイクはラケットのしなりからボールの回転，威力を生むコンセプトですが VSはオールラウンドモデルの為、前後衛どちらになっても長く使えるラケットです！ メリット ・しなりのあるジオブレイクシリーズではあるが50VSはしなりが少ないので扱いやすい ・中級者向けオールラウンダーモデルなので長く使える ・中古での買取価格が高い(メルカリで1万円程) デメリット ・入門者向けに比べ高価（エアライドの1.8倍程) ・ガットを張らなければならない カラー：マリン 楽天市場 ショップ：フライトスポーツ 様 19,258円 (2/24現在) ④ YONEX GEOBREAK 50VS (ジオブレイク 50バーサス） です。 理由はシンプルで、 「コスパが良い」 ＋ 「性能に早く慣れられる」 この2つが圧倒的に大きいからです。 理由①：結果的にコスパが一番良い エアライドのような入門モデルは低価格で購入しやすい反面、 半年〜1年ほどで「もっと良いラケットが欲しい」と感じやすく、 結局 買い替えが早くなる 傾向があります。 その点、ジオブレイク50VSは 中級者向けのオールラウンドモデルで長く使える ため、 最低でも中学2年生までは使用できるでしょう。 その分中学校で購入するラケットの本数が1本増えるようなイメージです。 つまり、 最初から50VSを買った方が結果的にお得 というわけです。 ※ただし、ラケットを傷から守る エッジガードは必須。 （アイテム編で詳しく紹介します） 理由②：しなりのあるラケットに早く慣れられる エアライドは軽くて振りやすいですが、 「軽い＝良い球が打てる」ではありません。 入門モデルから中級者モデルに変えたとき、 重さ・しなり・打球感の違いに戸惑う子が多いのも事実です。 高校生や大人のように筋力がある選手は、 軽いラケットでも自分のパワーで速くて重い球を打つことができます。 しかし、中学生はまだ筋力が発展途中。 そのため ラケット自体の性能をしっかり活かして打てるかどうか が、 ボールのスピードや威力に大きく影響 します。 つまり、 性能の良いラケットを使う＝ 中学生でも強い球が打ちやすくなる ということなんです。 だからこそ、扱いやすさと性能のバランスが良い title: Xuxa lança XSPB 14 mesmo após inseguranças: “não gosto da minha voz”
meta_description: Apresentadora conta o que mudou na geração atual de crianças e revela sua maior dificuldade durante as gravações
headings: Xuxa lança XSPB 14 mesmo após inseguranças: “não gosto da minha voz”
Apresentadora conta o que mudou na geração atual de crianças e revela sua maior dificuldade durante as gravações
body_text: Para celebrar seus 62 anos, Xuxa Meneghel preparou um presente especial para os fãs: o lançamento do aguardado “Xuxa Só Para Baixinhos 14” (“XSPB 14”). O novo trabalho da Rainha dos Baixinhos, com o tema “Cores”, foi lançado nesta quinta-feira (27). Disponível no Youtube e plataformas digitais, o projeto vem após 9 anos do último lançamento, o “XSPB 13”, em 2016, e conta com a participação especial de Angélica na música “Qual é a Cor da Amizade”. São 13 faixas inéditas, e xplorando a temática das cores de forma divertida e educativa, com utilização de Inteligência Artificial para os modelos, cenários e animação. A direção é assinada pela própria Xuxa, enquanto a direção musical ficou nas mãos do namorado da estrela, Junno Andrade (que também participa da faixa “Vermelho”). “A grande diferença do ‘XSPB 14’ é a maturidade. Aprendemos com os erros e acertos, e, desta vez, o projeto tem um olhar mais maduro em todos os sentidos. O fato de eu nunca ter usado Inteligência Artificial também é um diferencial bem grande”, analisa a apresentadora. Em entrevista exclusiva à CLAUDIA , Xuxa explica como foram as gravações do projeto, o convite para Angélica participar e analisa o que mudou entre a geração que curtiu o “XSPB 13”, lançado em 2016, e a atual. CLAUDIA: Quanto tempo levou para definição do tema ‘Cores’ e realizar as gravações? XUXA : Desde o dia que a gente fez o ‘XSPB 13’ eu sabia que o 14 seria ‘Cores’. As cores estão no mundo inteiro, na vida de todos nós e, no audiovisual, as pessoas vão entender, mesmo que não falem a mesma língua. Eu já tenho o 15 na minha cabeça, já tenho o 16, o 17, já tenho até o 20 e alguma coisa. A gente botou voz e gravou tudo se não me engano em 5 dias, e a pós-produção foi de 4 a 5 meses. XUXA: Botar a voz, para mim, é sempre é um pouco difícil porque eu não gosto da minha voz. Eu acho que eu fico rouca, não curto o resultado, reclamo porque acho que não estou cantando legal… Até depois de ficar ouvindo bastante, eu me acostumar que essa é minha voz mesmo e é isso. Então acho que a minha maior dificuldade ainda é colocar a voz. Mas quando eu coloco, imagino já a roupa que eu vou botar, o cenário, já imagino tudo pronto, então isso pra mim é bem legal. CLAUDIA: Como foi o convite para Angélica participar? XUXA: Eu apenas a convidei, dizendo que eu tinha uma música para cantar sobre amizade e que eu não conseguia ver outra pessoa que não fosse ela. Ela já topou sem antes ouvir a música: ouvindo, ficou mais apaixonada. Ela colocou a voz em 30 minutos só, rapidamente, aquela voz doce, linda que ela tem. Falei que a gente ia fazer esse clipe em Inteligência Artificial, ela aprovou os desenhos que o Haroldo ( Guimarães Neto, ilustrador ) fez e, nossa, foi muito gostoso poder gravar com ela. Eu me emocionei vendo ela cantando, eu me emocionei gravando com ela, foi tudo lindo! CLAUDIA: O que mudou no gosto das crianças entre 2016, quando saiu o XSPB 13, e 2025? XUXA: Eu acho que muita coisa mudou porque a gente não tem mais o projeto ali, na mão da gente, não existe mais essa coisa, é tudo virtual. Então as crianças hoje em dia não conseguem mais pegar um CD, um DVD, um disco, não tem mais essa coisa física. Eles aceitam isso numa boa. Eu vejo as crianças daquela época que cresceram falando: ‘puxa vida, por que não tem o trabalho físico? A gente quer ver essa capa, a gente quer tocar, ver…’ As crianças para quem eu estou trabalhando e falando agora não querem e acho que não tem nem espaço na vida delas para um projeto físico como esse. A gente tem que aprender, né, acho que essa é a grande mudança. CLAUDIA: Uma regravação da música “Arco-Íris” chegou a ser cogitada para os “baixinhos” que já são pais? Xuxa : Eu já regravei ‘Arco-Íris’ no ‘Xuxa Dance’ (1996) que foi o Gringo Cardia que dirigiu. Então tem já uma nova versão, mas não agrada muito, as pessoas gostam daquela que elas ouviram lá nos anos 80 e e eu respeito, concordo e aceito. Notícias “Que Xou da Xuxa é esse?”: descubra quem é a menina do meme Famosos Angélica revela detalhes de grupo no Whatsapp com Eliana e Xuxa; confira Cultura 7 filmes divertidos e educativos para maratonar com as crianças em casa Receba seleções especiais de receitas, além das melhores dicas de amor & sexo. E o melhor: sem pagar nada. Inscreva-se abaixo para receber as nossas newsletters: Quer receber as últimas notícias, receitas e matérias incríveis de CLAUDIA direto no seu celular? É só se inscrever aqui, no nosso canal no WhatsApp . Com o aplicativo de CLAUDIA, disponível para iOS e Android , você confere as edições impressas na íntegra, e ainda ganha acesso ilimitado ao conteúdo dos apps de todos os títulos Abril, como Veja e Superinteressante.

title: Rude Neighbor Freezes When He Discovers What's Inside A Tree He Was Asked Not To Cut Down
body_text: Adam couldn’t believe his eyes as he stared out over his yard. A strong smell hung in the air, burning the small crowd of people’s eyes as they stared. He didn’t know what to say. They were all staring at him, bewildered by the sight before them. Adam felt rage bubbling deep within him, he couldn’t believe what his neighbor had done. Looking into the distance, he saw the blue lights approaching.

title: TEN MUGSHOTS: Meet The Militants Who Allegedly Ambushed An ICE Facility Near Ft. Worth
meta_description: Anarchist militants allegedly launched an “ambush” outside an ICE detention center near Fort Worth on July 4.
body_text: Anarchist militants allegedly launched an "ambush" outside an ICE detention center near Fort Worth on July 4, resulting in 11 suspects being charged for the attack, including three counts of attempted murder of federal officers and three counts of firing a gun in a violent crime. The suspects, who were dressed in black military-style clothing and some armed, were detained near the detention center soon after the attack, with weapons including a pistol and AR-15 rifle found in their possession, along with other gear like radios, helmets, and ballistic vests. FBI searches of suspects' homes and apartments revealed anti-government propaganda and anarchist materials, with officials vowing to prosecute anyone who attacks law enforcement with the toughest criminal statutes and penalties available. Anarchist militants allegedly launched an “ambush” outside an ICE detention center near Fort Worth on July 4. Now, officials have published their identities, and The Dallas Express has found their mugshots. The U.S. Attorney for the Northern District of Texas charged 11 suspects for the attack, in which a gunman shot a police officer in the neck, as The Dallas Express previously reported. Acting U.S. Attorney Nancy Larson called it “a planned ambush with the intent to kill ICE corrections officers.” Militants in black body armor allegedly surrounded the ICE Prairieland Detention Facility in Alvarado, south of Fort Worth, late on July 4. Gunmen took their positions and shot an Alvarado police officer in the neck, and fired at ICE corrections officers. Federal officials are charging 10 suspects each with three counts of attempted murder of federal officers and three counts of firing a gun in a violent crime. An eleventh suspect was charged with obstruction of justice and conspiracy for allegedly attempting to destroy evidence. The suspects are currently detained in the Johnson County Jail, and they all face charges including attempted murder of a federal officer, discharging a firearm in a violent crime, aggravated assault on a public servant with a deadly weapon, and terrorism. Across the board, the latter two charges both carry $5 million bonds. Cameron Arnold is also known as “Autumn Hill,” according to the criminal complaint. Jail records list Hill’s sex as male , and he is from Dallas. Bradford Morris is, similarly, also known as “Meagan Morris.” Jail records lis t Morris’ sex as male. According to the criminal complaint, Morris is from Dallas. Johnson County Sheriff’s Office deputies arrested Baumann, Rueda, Sikes, Gibson, Batten, and the Sotos near the detention center soon after the attack, according to the criminal complaint. “They were dressed in black, military-style clothing, some had on body armor, some were covered in mud, some were armed, and some had radios.” Baumann was reportedly “wet” and “covered with burs,” suggesting he was “crawling around in the woods.” Soon after, deputies also pulled over Morris’ van, where they saw weapons including a pistol and AR-15 rifle in “plain view,” the complaint read. They arrested Morris, then found a “loaded magazine” for the pistol, another AR-15, a hand-held radio, a ballistic helmet, and two “Kevlar ballistic style vests.” The next day, police searched Morris’ house on 56th Street in south Dallas – a “staging” location for the attack, according to the complaint. There, they found Arnold, who another suspect said was at the ICE facility during the shooting. Police also found fireworks, spray paint, three “body-armor vests,” and nine more guns. On Monday, the FBI followed Daniel Rolando Sanchez, charged in a separate complaint, from his home in Garland to an apartment in Denton. The FBI searched the apartment and found “anti-government propaganda” calling for “insurrectionary anarchy.” At multiple stages in the investigation, officers found anarchist propaganda , including a flag that read “resist fascism, fight oligarchy” and flyers that said “fight ICE terror with class war” and “free all political prisoners.” Larson warned officials will find anyone who attacks law enforcement, and prosecute them with “the toughest criminal statutes and penalties that we have available.”

title: Menos de uma semana após medida protetiva, mulher é encontrada morta
meta_description: Paloma Ribeiro da Silva, de 33 anos, foi encontrada morta em uma fazenda na Vila Samauma, em Alto Alegre, nessa quinta-feira (12). A família de Paloma
body_text: Paloma Ribeiro da Silva, de 33 anos, foi encontrada morta em uma fazenda na Vila Samauma, em Alto Alegre, nessa quinta-feira (12). A família de Paloma registrou um Boletim de Ocorrência onde relata a suspeita de que a morte não tenha sido natural, e citou o histórico de agressões que ela sofria no casamento. Menos de uma semana antes da morte de Paloma, uma medida protetiva contra marido G.M.A, havia sido concedida pela Justiça. De acordo com informações apuradas pela FolhaBV, G.M.A. e Paloma possuíam um histórico turbulento no relacionamento, com registros de boletins de ocorrência por agressões. O último deles, ocorreu no dia 6 de março, quando ela teria sido agredida por G.M.A. na presença do filho do casal, uma criança de 9 anos, e de outros moradores da casa. O homem chegou a ser preso e foi posto em liberdade após passar por audiência de custódia, que determinou a instauração de uma medida protetiva para a vítima. Em vídeo gravado Paloma mostra ferimentos na boca, supostamente ocasionados devido a agressão sofrida pelo companheiro. Após essa situação, os dois teriam reatado a relação e estavam juntos na noite do dia 11, em uma fazenda onde o G.M.A estaria prestando serviços. Por volta de 12h30 do dia 12, o homem teria deixado o local para ir pescar e teria deixado Paloma dormindo em uma rede. Por volta das 15h, uma testemunha desconfiou da demora de Paloma em acordar e foi até o local indicado pelo suspeito, e encontrou a vítima já morta. A testemunha registrou um boletim de ocorrência informando a morte da mulher. A FolhaBV solicitou mais informações sobre o caso à Polícia Civil de Roraima (PCRR) e aguarda o retorno.

title: Azure AD B2C: Clients must send a client_secret when redeeming a confidential grant
meta_description: I try to setup authentification for an Angular app using authorization code and Azure AD B2C (oidc-client on client side), but I'm getting these errors from Angular: After looking in B2C audit logs...
body_text: I try to setup authentification for an Angular app using authorization code and Azure AD B2C (oidc-client on client side), but I'm getting these errors from Angular: After looking in B2C audit logs, I found this error message: Clients must send a client_secret when redeeming a confidential grant. If I switch the above configuration to use a local IdentityServer instance, everthings works has expected. Is someone able to point me out where or how I should investigate this? 2 Who will redeem code for you? I guess you are using SPA and trying to integrate Auth Code flow. If you are using SPA then you must use implicit flow. B2C is showing client_secret is must because it needs that to redeem code. replace response_type value with 'token' and see what it is doing Ramakrishna – Ramakrishna 2019-12-04 01:47:15 +00:00 Commented Dec 4, 2019 at 1:47 1 B2C currently supporting PKCE flow for Native Applications but not for Web. Native Applications can redeem auth code by using PKCE flow but not web applications. Ramakrishna – Ramakrishna 2019-12-05 10:43:51 +00:00 Commented Dec 5, 2019 at 10:43 I had the exact same issue as you and was just able to resolve it. AD is requesting the client_secret from you, because it isn't configured for PKCE yet. To tell AD that you want to use PKCE for a specific redirect url you need to set its type from 'Web' to 'Spa' . This can be done in the manifest. Search for the property replyUrlsWithType in the Manifest and look for your .../signin-callback url. Change its type to 'Spa' and you should be good. The configured url will now disappear from your Authorization page but thats ok -> it's still present in the Manifest. The MS team is working on this new type. Also make sure you marked your application as a public client. For more information, see my answer here: Is Active Directory not supporting Authorization Code Flow with PKCE? In the Azure AD B2C App there is now a simpler option to do that. In the Authentication tab where the Web Redirect URIs are you will probably see a message This app has implicit grant settings enabled. If you are using any of these URIs in a SPA with MSAL.js 2.0, you should migrate URIs . When you click that, a new window will let you choose which Redirect URI you want to move to the SPA Redirect URIs instead: After that just click Configure and it should work. The Redirect URI will now be located in the SPA section instead of the Web one. In few comments, I read it as 'ADB2C currently supporting PKCE flow for Native Applications but not for Web', I know it's bit old discussion thread but I just wanted to inform that currently PKCE is recommended for all applications including web application. I'm not sure if oidc-client works OOTB with B2C. It's more for identityserver. I suspect that your code is fine but ... The last I heard, Azure AD does not allow cross origin calls to the token endpoint - and therefore does not support the Authorization Code Flow (PKCE) that SPAs should use in 2019. Unless I'm mistaken this will mean you need to use the (unrecommended) implicit flow when integrating with Azure AD. There have been problems for SPAs for a couple of years now. Out of interest I wrote a couple of posts on Azure SPA workrounds a couple of years ag - I suspect some of this is still relevant: https://authguidance.com/2017/11/30/azure-active-directory-setup/ Find the answer to your question by asking.
