title: Make Your Garden Patio Look Expensive With An Affordable Planting Solution - House Digest
meta_description: Repetition planting is an excellent solution to fill in a lot of space on a budget, making your patio look much more expensive than it is.
headings: Make Your Garden Patio Look Expensive With An Affordable Planting Solution
Use mass planting techniques to create a high end patio aesthetic
Choosing the right perennials simplifies your outdoor design process
body_text: Many people think a high-end patio requires a massive budget for stonework or custom water features, but the real secret to a luxury look is found at the plant store. You can mimic the aesthetic of a five-star resort by using a design principle called plant repetition. Instead of picking out one of every flower that catches your eye, you should buy a large quantity of a single type. This creates a cohesive design that looks like it was planned by a professional. That repetition pulls the look of your outdoor space together, making a basic concrete slab feel more like a custom space. When you simplify your color palette and focus on one type of plant, the whole area looks balanced and more expensive than it actually was to put together. An example of a perfect plant for this elevated style is English lavender (Lavandula angustifolia). This perennial is cold-tolerant in USDA Hardiness Zones 5 through 8 and offers a classic look. If you line your patio with matching lavender plants, you create a fragrant, soft border that looks much more intentional than a random assortment of pots. This same strategy works to create a cohesive front garden that improves curb appeal. Since they all grow to the same height and have the same color, they give the space a clean look that ties everything together. Buying in bulk like this can often lead to discounts at local garden centers, so don't forget to ask about this cost-effective strategy. What you're doing is using living plants to create the clean lines and boundaries that most people try to build with pricey stone or wood. It turns a ho-hum seating area into a sophisticated retreat with a budget-friendly price tag. Professional designers use plant repetition to give a space a sense of unity. When a patio feels expensive, it's usually because the greenery looks intentional and purposeful rather than cluttered or messy. You can get this look by using a structural grass like feather reed grass (Calamagrostis acutiflora). This grows well in zones 4 through 9 and adds a tall, vertical texture that works as a living privacy screen. Using a single species is one of the best ways to get a lush and natural look on your landscaping borders . Planting a long row of these around your garden patio creates a secluded, high-end vibe similar to a private terrace. When they sway together in the breeze, it adds a polished look that you won't get from a mix of different plants. This look can be quite beautiful because of how it interacts with light. Having all the same plants means they catch the sun all at once, creating a dynamic visual effect. Using plant repetition in your space also makes your life a lot easier because you only have to manage one type of plant. You won't have to worry about different watering schedules or soil needs, which helps keep the garden looking healthy and uniform. This consistency is also great for covering up any less-than-perfect spots on your patio. If your pavers are a bit worn or the concrete is stained, a thick, border of grass draws the focus toward the edge of the space. Finding the right plant for your specific climate is an important step in making your patio look expensive all year long. If you're in a warmer climate, the sago palm (Cycas revoluta) is an excellent choice for an architectural, tropical look. These are hardy in zones 9 through 11 and look incredible when you plant them in identical pots at each corner of your seating area. Arranging them with this kind of symmetry creates an upscale feel that makes even budget-friendly furniture look like an elegant part of the space. Those dark fronds stay green long after other flowers have finished for the year, maintaining your the patio's refined look. For a softer look in a more shaded area, hostas a good pick for zones 3 through 8. These plants have glossy leaves that grow quickly to fill in empty spaces. When you plant several of these in a row, the leaves overlap to form a professional-looking border. This growth also naturally blocks out weeds, which keeps your patio looking tidy without much effort. Buying a few large-leaved plants gives you a much bigger visual impact for your money than a bunch of small, scattered flowers. By using plant repetition, you can avoid one of the biggest mistakes when designing an outdoor garden by tricking the eye into seeing a high-end designer space created on a modest budget.

title: Chappell Roan Nails a Non-Naked Style 180 at the Mugler Fashion Show
meta_description: No nipple pasties here.
body_text: Chappell Roan made a statement at the 2026 Grammys with a naked Mugler dress featuring faux nipple rings and tattoos, securing her a front-row seat at Mugler's Fall 2026 fashion show. Despite expectations for a revealing outfit, Roan opted for an entirely-opaque skirt set at the Mugler Fall 2026 show, paired with unique heeled nude flip-flops that debuted alongside a starry version of her Grammys gown during the Spring 2026 show. Roan's iconic naked dress moment at the Grammys, featuring faux piercings and a thong-revealing skirt, has solidified her place in Grammys fashion history, eliciting gasps from the audience at Crypto.com Arena. When you buy through links on our articles, Future and its syndication partners may earn a commission. It's been a month since Chappell Roan arrived at the 2026 Grammys , with a naked Mugler dress dangling from faux nipple rings over a set of faux tattoos . A moment that iconic all but guaranteed her a seat at Mugler's Fall 2026 fashion show on March 6. Roan's front-row dress still went nude, but only with its color palette, not thong-revealing sheer chiffon. The Grammy winner has attended three Paris Fashion Week shows this season, including Acne Studios, Rick Owens, and most recently, Mugler. For the latter, I expected the style muse to pick up where she left off at the Grammy Awards. Perhaps a Spring 2026 top that's more necklace than bodice would've enticed Roan and her stylist, Genesis Webb . My guess was partially correct: Roan still sourced the maison's Spring 2026 runway. However, she opted for an entirely-opaque skirt set instead. A nude, skintight corset-turned-turtleneck served as the base of her set. Ruched, partial puff-sleeves atop the bone-in bodice became khaki to match her drop-waist, fit-and-flare skirt. It seems Mugler padded the low-rise hips to emphasize the already-cinched nature of Roan's belted waist. Contrary to the model's no-makeup makeup look, the "Pink Pony Club" singer's smokey eye shined as bright as her mixed-metal earrings. On Mugler's Fall 2026 runway, this austere outfit came with sharp, square-toe mules, garnished in a cool-toned python print. Roan, on the other hand, chose uniquely heeled, nude flip-flops . Turns out, the sandals debuted alongside a starry version of her Grammys gown during the Spring 2026 show. The 2026 Grammys have come and gone, but Roan's naked dress still lives rent-free in my mind. (I assume that's precisely the reaction she wanted her red carpet look to have.) For a quick recap: The singer didn't reveal her faux piercings right away. First, she posed in an elongated burgundy cape, her medieval tattoos and piercings awaiting their close-up. Once photographers got the shot, Roan removed her cloak and elicited gasps from the whole of Crypto.com Arena. Cowl-like, burgundy-colored chiffon hung from built-in piercings atop faux nipple pasties. A matching thong peeked through her high-low skirt, similar to an archival nipple-ring dress from Mugler's Spring 1998 Couture show. However, the original nude dress became burgundy for Roan. Roan has yet to share her thoughts on the Mugler Fall 2026 show ( a ritual she's stayed true to all Paris Fashion Week long). But she wasted no time uploading her post-Grammys reaction on Instagram . "Giggling because I don’t even think this is THAT outrageous of an outfit. The look’s actually so awesome and weird," Roan captioned her post. "I recommend just exercising your free will, it’s really fun and silly."

title: Paige Spiranac Gets Naked & Dives Into A Tub Of Golf Balls For The Internet
meta_description: Well, well, well...did Paige 'Paigeviews' Spiranac do it again or did Paigeviews do it again.
body_text: Well, well, well...did Paige "Paigeviews" Spiranac do it again or did Paigeviews do it again. The world's No. 1 golf influencer, who has Grace Charis charging hard for her crown , got naked and jumped into a bathtub full of golf balls because that's exactly the type of content that will click on the Internet, and as a golf influencer, your ass must get results or the Internet forgets about you. Once again, Paigeviews Spiranac knows how to get clicks. "Got balls?" the 30-year-old legend of the industry asked her Instagram audience. I don't dabble in the Paigeviews content these days like many of the OutKick Culture Department employees, but I know enough to know that the living legend clearly understands what is at stake in 2023 as Charis begins her march towards the No. 1 golf influencer ranking. Paigeviews built this industry and she's been open the last few days on what it means to see someone like Charis come along and copy her content to the point where it's starting to feel like a legitimate feud is developing between these two. Monday, in a fiery Instagram Story Q&A, Spiranac took a shot at copycats -- and she's specifically talking about Charis without naming names. "They say that imitation is the greatest form of flattery, so I am proud to be an inspiration for all of these women. They've seen me be successful and they know that if they copy exactly what I'm doing or follow in my footsteps and the exact path that I have created and built for myself that they'll also be successful and we have seen that," Paigeviews told her captivated audience. Grace Charis copied Paige Spiranac's iconic green jacket Masters content that sent her skyrocketing to great fame. / Instagram "That's pretty cool and shows how powerful my brand has been that if they copy everything I do they will also be successful. I will say that I do value creativity and I wish that it wasn't copied exactly from like the exact outfits to how I do my hair, how I caption, to how it was shot. It would be nice if there was just a little bit of a difference. "And I think it would actually be beneficial for them to be different and not have it be exactly the same." She's talking directly to Charis and now we have an official war. You can tell Paigeviews is pissed. Charis is like 23 and raking up millions of followers as the rising star golf girl while let's face it, Paigeviews is on the back nine of her career. It was bound to happen and now the claws are coming out. Tensions are officially rising within the golf influencer world. Will Charis get naked and jump into a tub of golf balls? If it happens, this is going to be like China invading Taiwan. Paigeviews will have no choice but to fight back to defend her semiconductor/pageview turf.

title: Fabulous Food Friday #66
meta_description: I found my new favorite roast recipe. Recently I have seen it on multiple blogs and websites so just in case you've missed it, here you go! Of course I had to make it my
body_text: I found my new favorite roast recipe. Recently I have seen it on multiple blogs and websites so just in case you’ve missed it, here you go! Of course I had to make it my own so I added an extra packet. This makes THE best gravy! Pair up with mashed potatoes, veggies of your choice and most definitely some homemade bread or rolls! Sunday Roast 1 (4-6 lb) chuck roast or rump roast 1 packet Brown Gravy mix 1 packet Au Jus mix 1 packet Hidden Valley Ranch Dressing mix 1 packet Good Seasonings Italian Dressing mix 2 cups water Place roast in crock pot. In a bowl whisk together all 4 packets. Sprinkle mixture over roast. Pour water around the roast and cover with lid. I cooked mine in high for 8 hours, but all crock pots cook differently so make yours accordingly until cooked completely through. To make gravy…Drain all of the crock pot juices into a medium pan. Bring to a boil. In a small bowl whisk together 2 TBSP cornstarch with 4 TBSP cold water. Once to a boil, slowly pour in cornstarch mixture while whisking briskly. Let it thicken then remove from burner and serve. Over the late summer I had scored some fresh blueberries for a fantastic price. I bought a few containers and flash froze them so I could use them in delicious recipes like this one . It made it seem so gourmet to me with both the zucchini and blueberries. A great pair up! Zucchini and Blueberry Bread 1/2 cup oil or applesauce 1/2 cup sugar 3/4 cup brown sugar 1 large egg plus 1 egg white 2 tsp vanilla 1 1/4 cup shredded zucchini (one large or two small zucchini) 1 1/2 cup all purpose flour 1/2 tsp salt 1/2 tsp baking powder 1/4 tsp baking soda 2 tsp ground cinnamon 2 c. fresh blueberries 2 TBSP flour Preheat oven to 350 degrees and prepare one regular loaf pan or 3 mini loaf pans. In large bowl, combine oil and/or applesauce, sugars, egg and vanilla. Stir to combine well. Stir in zucchini to combine. Add 1 1/2 cups flour, salt, baking powder and soda, cinnamon and fold ingredients until just barely combined. In a separate bowl toss blueberries in 2 TBSP flour. This will help them be evenly distributed throughout your bread. Gently fold in blueberries. Place into loaf pan(s). Place in center of oven to bake until toothpick inserted into center comes out clean, about 60-75 minutes for loaf pan and 35-40 minutes for miniature loaf pans. Let cool in pans for 15 minutes and remove to let cool completely before slicing and serving. Here are some other yummy dinner recipes…

title: Fire On Babylon by Sinéad O'Connor - Songfacts
meta_description: Fire On Babylon by Sinéad O'Connor song meaning, lyric interpretation, video and chart position
body_text: "Fire On Babylon" is about Sinéad O'Connor's mother, a common topic in her songs. O'Connor has accused her mother, who died in 1985, of abusing her and her siblings. In this song, she levels another accusation when she sings: "Look what she did to her son." "It had to do with something I found out she'd done to one of my brothers that just really made me angry," O'Connor explained in her memoir Rememberings . "Truth to tell, it's very hard for me to get angry about my mother. It's the way I've survived. I've convinced myself she didn't know what she was doing. People will do that, but of course, I've misplaced that anger and it might be more mature for me to accept it." The song was released as a single from O'Connor's fourth album, Universal Mother . By this time, she had burned many bridges in the industry, so she had trouble getting radio play or positive publicity. In 1990, she had a huge hit with " Nothing Compares 2 U ," a song from her second album, which made her a global star. But she kicked back against the notoriety and became more political, veering as far from pop stardom as she could. Her next album, released in 1992, was a collection of covers called Am I Not Your Girl? By the time Universal Mother came out in 1994, her star had fallen, which was fine with her. She still had enough fans to get the album on the charts in many countries. O'Connor wrote the song with John Reynolds, her first husband and the father of her first child. They divorced in 1991 but shared a lasting bond and kept working together. He also produced the track (along with O'Connor and Tim Simenon) and played drums on it. Michel Gondry directed the music video, which shows O'Connor in what looks like a doll house as she shares screen time with her younger self. At one point, she presents her mother with a birthday cake that catches fire, a symbol of her resentment. Gondry has many very clever videos with bright colors and shifting perspectives. Others include " Everlong " by Foo Fighters and " Human Behaviour " by Björk. A few things were different about O'Connor on the Universal Mother album. For one, she had hair. Not a lot, but enough to cover her head. Also, she sang in her Irish accent instead of suppressing it like she had done before. The trumpet riff is a sample of "Dr. Jekyll," a 1958 track by jazz great Miles Davis. More songs that deal with child abuse or domestic violence More songs with videos directed by Michel Gondry "Stay" by Lisa Loeb was the first #1 hit by an unsigned artist. It got a boost from Ethan Hawke, who got it into a movie he starred in called Reality Bites. Eddie Van Halen played the guitar solo on "Beat It" as a favor to Quincy Jones, who produced the album. Before she was famous, Lady Gaga was a staff songwriter, and wrote the song "Quicksand," which Britney Spears recorded in 2008. Staind's big moment came in 1999 when lead singer Aaron Lewis played "Outside" on Limp Bizkit's Family Values tour. The live, acoustic version earned lots of radio play. The "Don't Stop Believin'" lyric was inspired by Sunset Boulevard, making it perfect for the Rock of Ages musical. Zach Bryan and Kacey Musgraves had both gone through divorces when they wrote and recorded the wrenching duet "I Remember Everything." It was the first #1 Hot 100 hit for each of them. It took him seven years to recover from his American hit "Fool (If You Think It's Over)," but Chris Rea became one of the top singer-songwriters in his native UK. The Scorpions and UFO guitarist is also a very prolific songwriter - he explains how he writes with his various groups, and why he was so keen to get out of Germany and into England. John tells the "St. Elmo's Fire (Man In Motion)" story and explains why he disappeared for so long. The lead singer and pianist for Procol Harum, Gary talks about finding the musical ideas to match the words. Christopher Cross with Deep Purple? Kenny Loggins in Caddyshack? A Fact or Fiction all about yacht rock and those who made it. Revisit the awesome glory of Night Ranger and Damn Yankees: cheesily-acted videos, catchy guitar licks, long hair, and lyrics that are just plain relatable.

title: Right Stories - Funny & True Stories | NotAlwaysRight.com
meta_description: The customer is NOT always right!
body_text: A woman tried on some shoes from the sale rack. They fit her, and she was happy. I ring them through at the counter, and she says: Customer: “Oh, actually, can you get me a duplicate pair? I don’t want to wear the shoes everyone else has been wearing.” Me: “These ones out are the only ones we have, because they’re the sale shoes.” Customer: *Angry.* “Well, go check the back anyways!” Instead of telling her and missing out on a sale, I just wrapped up the ones she tried on and gave them to her. She tried them on and immediately went: Customer: “Yep, I can tell these are new. They feel stiffer and look cleaner! See? wasn’t that hard, was it? I knew you’d have some in the back.”

title: Android 14 could let you clone apps so you can use two accounts at the same time
meta_description: Android 14 is preparing to add an app cloning feature that will let you clone an app so you can use two accounts at the same time.
body_text: There are plenty of reasons why one might have multiple accounts for an online service, but if that service’s Android app doesn’t let you switch between your accounts, then you’ll have to find a workaround. Maybe you have one account signed in on the app and another on the website, or maybe you cloned the app so you have two instances of it installed on your phone. App cloning is a really popular solution to this problem, but unless your device’s OEM implemented the feature themselves, you’ll have to find a third-party app cloner on Google Play or elsewhere to do the job. That’s because Android doesn’t natively support cloning apps, but that could change with Android 14. Google is testing a new “cloned apps” feature in Android 14 that will let you “create a second instance of an app so that you can use two accounts at the same time.” This feature may be made available to users through the Settings app under Settings > Apps > Cloned Apps, as shown in the screenshots embedded below. When you clone your first app through the “cloned apps” feature, Android creates what’s called a “clone user profile” and installs that app onto the profile. Any apps you clone afterward are installed onto the same clone profile that was created before. Since the clone profile doesn’t share app data with the parent user profile, that means any apps you clone won’t retain your login information or settings, so you’ll have to set them up from scratch. If you decide you don’t need the cloned app anymore, you can delete the app through the “cloned apps” page or through the standard “app info” interface. Although the “cloned apps” feature is new in Android 14, the clone user profile itself was first introduced in Android 12 . However, Android hasn’t offered a way, outside of ADB commands, for users to create that profile or to choose which apps to install apps onto it — until now. Another key piece that was missing in prior releases (and is still missing in Android 14 DP1) is launcher integration. Cloned apps need to be differentiated from their originals on the home screen in some way, otherwise you’d mistakenly open the wrong app all the time. In earlier releases, the launcher would mistakenly apply badges signifying the cloned apps belonged to a work profile. In fact, the launcher would think your device has a work profile when none might exist at all, cluttering your app drawer with a work profile tab. While the Pixel Launcher in Android 14 DP1 doesn't apply a work profile badge to cloned apps, it doesn't differentiate them at all, and also continues the bug of creating a work profile tab. At least it's clear now that app cloning in the process of being upgraded to actually be usable in Android 14. That’s not to say that you can’t clone apps if your device isn’t running Android 14, though. As I mentioned before, many OEM builds of Android ship with their own take on this feature, and there are also plenty of third-party apps that provide this functionality. For the most part, these existing implementations clone apps by installing them onto secondary user profiles. OnePlus’ OxygenOS, for example, creates a dedicated user profile for its “Parallel Apps” feature that it then installs the apps of your choice onto. Most third-party app cloners like Shelter create a work profile because that’s the only kind of profile they can create with the APIs that are available to them and the privileges they can attain. While installing an app onto a work profile does effectively clone it, using a work profile this way poses some challenges. Besides the UI clash, work profiles are fully isolated from the primary user account, so file access and file sharing between profiles can be tricky. A clone profile, on the other hand, is only partially isolated from its parent profile. Apps installed on the clone profile have their own private app data directories and can’t access files saved in the private app data directories of the original apps on the parent profile, but they can access storage or data that’s also accessible to apps on the parent profile. The implementation and use of Android’s clone profile is defined by the CDD, or Compatibility Definition Document. The Android 13 CDD states that creating a clone profile for the purposes of running dual instances of the same app is optional, but I’m not sure if it’ll become a required feature for OEMs to implement in Android 14. Now that the feature seems to have support from Google, however, I anticipate that OEMs that have yet to implement this kind of feature already will simply adopt Android 14’s app cloning implementation. There are a few caveats I should note, though. First of all, this feature is currently being tested by Google, so there’s no guarantee it’ll make its way to the stable Android 14 release. This feature is disabled by default in Android 14 DP1, so I had to toggle a developer flag to get this UI to appear in the Settings app. Furthermore, the list of apps that are cloneable is currently restricted to a handful of apps defined by the OEM; I had to modify a flag to get Discord, Facebook, and Telegram to appear in the screenshots shown above. There’s no mechanism or API to opt in or opt out of this, and I don’t know if such a thing is currently being developed. I’ll keep an eye out for any further development on this feature.

glossary 6 terms ChloeOsu11ivan Preview Financial Analysis 13 terms quizlette77080130 Preview Single Entry Accounting Systems and Cash Flow Management 54 terms Christian_Ballis1 Preview ACCT3011 FINAL 31 terms quizlette53031262 Preview Financial Maths Definitions 21 terms Bianca_454 Preview accounting 6 terms astylianou2008 Preview Accounting Assumptions 7 terms yymmbbgg Preview HSC Business Studies: Limitations to Financial Reports 9 terms quizlette48746419 Preview Influences and Strategies in Financial Management 483 terms T_obee Preview Topic 7 Cost volume profit 35 terms bsavage21115 Preview Understanding Foreign Investment in Australia 12 terms Luggy77 Preview HSC Business Studies: Financial Management Strategies 91 terms William_Moore494 Preview Finance Basics Regulatory Guide 105: AFS Licensing Competence Introduction to Accounting Concepts and Processes Year 10 Accounting: Budgeting and Financial Basics Business Registration and Financial Management in Australia Fundamentals of Accounting Concepts and Principles Managing Exchange Rate Risk with Currency Derivatives Accounting for Sustainability and Stakeholder Value Financial Accounting Fundamentals and Conventions Financial Calculations: Fractions, Decimals, and Percentages Recursion and Financial Modelling Key Terms Mathematical Formulas and Financial Concepts Terms in this set (90) Accounting Assumption Period assumption Accrual accounting assumption Going concern assumption Entity assumption Period Assumption Reports are prepared for a particular period of time, such as a month or a year, in order to obtain comparability of results. Accrual Accounting Assumption Revenue should be recorded when it's earned and expenses should be recorded when they are incurred Going Concern Assumption The assumption that the company will continue in operation for the foreseeable future. Entity Assumption The records of the entity are kept completely separate from those of the owner as well as from those of the entities. Qualitative Characteristics Timeliness Understandability Relevance Faithful representation Comparability Verifiability Timeliness Having information available to decision-makers before it loses its capacity to influence decisions Understandability Financial information to be comprehensible to users with reasonable knowledge of business and economic activities. Relevance Information is relevant if it has the potential to affect the decision making of users of the report. Faithful Representation Information must faithfully represent the economic events being reported and should be free from personal bias. Comparability Enables users to identify and understand similarities and differences among items Verifiability Information should be presented accurately and be supported by evidence such as business documents. Assets Economic resources controlled by an entity that are likely to produce economic benefits in the future. Liabilities Present obligations of an entity to transfer economic resources to other entities. Owner's Equity The residual value of the assets of an entity after deducting all its liabilities.

title: How do I repair the modern Lionel Poultry Dispatch Car? - Trains
meta_description: How do I repair the modern Lionel Poultry Dispatch Car? Learn the answer from Classic Toy Trains and Bruk Bannister.
body_text: I have two modern Lionel operating cars based on the postwar No. 3434 Poultry Dispatch Car. One, the No. 29825 Postwar Celebration version of the 3434, has the figure suspended on the operating arm between two springs. On the other, a No. 16739 Looney Tunes Foghorn Leghorn Poultry Car, the operating arm appears to have been supported by a rubber band twisted in an “X” through two clip ears with the other end hooked onto a hanger above to suspend Foghorn inside the boxcar. Once I took the car out of its box, I realized everything was loose and the rubber band had deteriorated. The instructions placed inside the box give no indication and offer no drawing of the operating mechanism, in particular the rubber band. Also, the only part listed on Lionel’s website is the figure. Do you have any information about the size of the rubber band so I can try to replace it? – Al Wussler, Smyrna, Ga. Bruk Bannister recommends first identifying the Lionel part number for the rubber band as 610-9819-020. Unfortunately, that part number no longer comes up on the Lionel website, which means it’s not being made. A replacement is a no. 10-size rubber band. You’ll have to route it as pictured in Fig. 1. Lionel also used .005″ piano wire; just twist it as shown in Fig 1. You may want to upgrade to a spring setup, which Lionel uses on newer versions of the Poultry Dispatch Car. Bruk recommends using Lionel part No. 600-0342-118. It isn’t the exact part used on the newer items, but it should get you a good result. For tips on installing it, check out Fig. 2. Be sure to make any needed adjustments, just as you would have to do if you opted for what’s shown in Fig. 1.

title: Radioactive Emergency ending explained: Who was responsible and what happened to the victims?
meta_description: Radioactive Emergency was released on Netflix on March 18, 2026. All episodes of the Brazilian miniseries were released together.
headings: What became of the victims in Radioactive Emergency ?
Who was responsible for the disaster?
What action was taken by the authorities in this crisis?
So what does the ending of Radioactive Emergency really mean?
body_text: Radioactive Emergency was released on Netflix on March 18, 2026. All episodes of the Brazilian miniseries were released together. The series brings us one of the most horrific disasters the world has ever seen: the Goiânia radiation accident of 1987. The series doesn’t just show us the story of this tragedy, but the human toll, the mistakes made, and the recovery process. By the end of the series, it becomes clear that the accident was not just a tragic event, but a culmination of many mistakes, negligence, and systemic failure that paved the way for the damage to countless lives. With the last episode of Radioactive Emergency , we move on from chaos to consequences. After days of an increasing number of ill and dying people, the authorities are aware of what has happened. The glowing blue powder has been identified as the radioactive element cesium-137, which has already contaminated a large part of the city. The government and CNEN (National Nuclear Energy Commission) take full steps. A massive operation is underway. Individuals are checked for radiation contamination, and those who are contaminated are taken away to the Navy Hospital. Entire homes are demolished, streets are cleaned, and people’s belongings are burned to prevent further contamination. These scenes are amongst the most heartbreaking in Radioactive Emergency as families watch their entire lives being burned away to survive. Meanwhile, the officials agree to confine the radioactive waste in Abadia de Goiás. The protesters oppose this decision because their land would be damaged with eternal consequences. But finally, the state constructs a safety structure. The ending of Radioactive Emergency does not soften the tragedy. There are many deaths from radiation poisoning, including Celeste and Antonia, who were two of the most affected characters, and also the men working at the scrap yard, Josias and Valencio. Not everyone meets the same fate. Quite a few survivors respond to treatment. It leads to them being discharged and beginning life afresh, devastated they may be because they have lost their homes, possessions, and the world as they know it. However, in one touching moment, hope is restored by the original survivors who tell each other they can start over their lives as long as they are alive and united. Some reunions flicker hope. By chance or by force, families finally return from quarantine. Radioactive Emergency notes that a single person did not cause the disaster. It was a series of failures. After a medical institution left the radiotherapy machine, which had a load of Cesium-137, abandoned during a legal battle, they had not adequately warned the authorities, and scavengers discovered the kill, which unknowingly started the chain of events. Due to the investigation, both the medical institute and CNEN are being questioned. During questioning, it was admitted that negligence played a key role. Beny Orenstein, the head of CNEN, takes responsibility rather than blaming others. Because of his faults, this becomes the turning point of Radioactive Emergency . In nominal terms, the series shows that five professionals from the medical facility were found guilty of manslaughter, and CNEN was required to pay damages to the victims. The first reaction is hasty and chaotic, but subsequent attempts are more vigorous and methodical. It is scientists and health specialists, in particular Marcio, who are responsible for discovering the danger and cleaning it up. Authorities monitored contamination, identified victims, and decontaminated on a large scale. New safety standards are introduced, including reinforced checks on radiotherapy machines. His dedication is finally appreciated, and at the end of Radioactive Emergency, he is offered a job at CNEN. His path is depicted as the importance of science and responsibility in the face of such a disaster. Beyond the events themselves, the ending conveys a powerful message. It illustrates how something as simple as leaving hazardous material unattended can turn into a huge tragedy when coupled with poor communication, inadequate systems, and a lack of awareness. It also draws our attention to more profound issues such as inequality and lack of education. It would be true to say that the people who would be affected didn’t know about the danger they faced, and their position in society led them to handle something lethal without realizing it. Once people are saved, they still suffer trauma and are distrustful of the system. It is too late after negligence has occurred. The government may finally help, but the damage already exists.

title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
meta_description: Spoilers for The Super Mario Bros. Movie will remain unmarked. You have been warned. Mario... IN SPACE!... THE MOVIE! The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is a 2026 animated fantasy adventure comedy film based on the Super Mario Bros. video game series, …
headings: Spoilers for The Super Mario Bros. Movie will remain unmarked. You have been warned.
You are now arriving at the Troping Galaxy:
body_text: http://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/WesternAnimation/TheSuperMarioGalaxyMovie Spoilers for The Super Mario Bros. Movie will remain unmarked. You have been warned. The Galaxy awaits. note Bowser Jr., Bowser, Peach, Luigi, Mario, Wart, Mouser, Birdo, Toad, Yoshi, Rosalina and the Lumas. The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is a 2026 animated fantasy adventure comedy film based on the Super Mario Bros. video game series, chiefly Super Mario Galaxy and Super Mario Galaxy 2 , and the sequel to 2023's The Super Mario Bros. Movie , produced by Nintendo and Illumination Entertainment . It is once again directed by Aaron Horvath and Michael Jelenic , written by Matthew Fogel, and stars Chris Pratt , Anya Taylor-Joy , Charlie Day , Jack Black , Keegan-Michael Key , and Kevin Michael Richardson . Newcomers include Brie Larson as Princess Rosalina, Benny Safdie as Bowser Jr, Donald Glover as Yoshi, Luis Guzmán as Wart, Issa Rae as the Honey Queen, and Glen Powell as Fox McCloud . Having saved the Mushroom Kingdom from the machinations of Bowser — now shrunk to a harmless size and imprisoned in Peach's Castle via upgrade from the birdcage to the form of a dollhouse that resembles his own castle — Mario, Luigi, Peach, Toad, and their new friend Yoshi face a new threat in Bowser Jr. whose ambitions threaten not just their world, but the universe itself. The heroes must now embark on an intergalactic quest to stop the tiny tyrant and rescue the cosmic Princess Rosalina, whom Bowser Jr. kidnapped to drain her cosmic power and build his own planet. The film was released on April 1, 2026, as part of the franchise's 40th anniversary . The first glimpse of what the film would be like was a teaser released on September 12, 2025, becoming one of the first things shown for that day's Nintendo Direct . Previews: Nintendo Direct Announcement , Teaser Nintendo Direct Official Trailer Reveal , Trailer , Brie Larson Voices Rosalina clip , Benny Safdie Voices Bowser Jr. clip The Super Mario Galaxy Movie Direct 1.25.26 , Yoshi First Look Trailer , "Level Up" commercial , "Intense" commercial , The Super Mario Galaxy Movie Direct 3.09.26 , Final Trailer You are now arriving at the Troping Galaxy: Accent Adaptation : Whittles speak with a You No Take Candle speech pattern in Galaxy 2 , but in this movie, they speak with Southern accents with more complex grammar. Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal : During Yoshi's brief adventure in Brooklyn, he’s wearing a baseball cap and sunglasses before losing them upon arriving in the Mushroom Kingdom. A Monty Mole in a baseball cap can be glimpsed in the Gateway Galaxy. Actionized Sequel : There's more intense action scenes, bigger set-pieces and higher stakes compared to its predecessor, as Bowser Jr.'s plans threaten the universe. Actor Allusion : In the Nintendo Direct revealing the first trailer and cast, right before announcing that Benny Safdie is the voice of Bowser Jr., Jack Black describes him as an " uncut gem ". It's probably not a coincidence that the Honey Queen's punishment for Bowser was hard labor via breaking down boulders with a pickaxe. Wart, voiced by Luis Guzmán , is the boss of a casino that Peach and Toad visit when trying to catch the Ukiki that stole his backpack and shows his vicious side by releasing an army of Ninjis to fight them, as well as informing the Koopa Troop about Peach, much like José Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha's temper during Narcos , who was played by Guzman during Season 1 of the show. Glen Powell , who had previously starred in Top Gun: Maverick , plays Nintendo's own premiere Ace Pilot Fox McCloud . Adaptational Badass : Zig-Zagged . While Rosalina isn't at all weak in the games, she was rarely confrontational. Only offering Mario her assistance in rescuing Peach and stepping in directly when situations actually required her power. Here, when Megaleg shows up on her doorstep, she's able to handle it with relative ease, only being captured when the Lumas got caught up. And even after this, she was able to break out of her restraints to protect the Luma captured alongside her, only being restrained once again because she was caught off-guard in the ensuing fight. Bowser Jr. was more a troublemaker in the games and he hardly ever took on Mario and Luigi without Bowser (if he wasn't asking for their help) or at least without his Junior Clown Car. In the film, thanks to the magic paint brush he carries and a ton of other weapons, he captures Rosalina to start and foils her escape attempt, confronts the brothers head-on to rescue his father, and even almost takes out Mario, forcing Luigi to jump in to save him. He was also the one to reform Bowser's army, create an entire planet to serve as their new home base, and build a doomsday weapon with the power to destroy the universe. The Frog Suit in Super Mario Bros. 3 , while it allowed the player to swim ridiculously fast underwater, was useless on land. In the movie, when Luigi acquires it, he not only jumps ridiculously high, but performs a powerful stomp: one strong enough to squash a Big Cheep Cheep. Yoshi stops a rampaging Chain Chomp in the "On Patrol" Montage by eating it, a feat typically impossible in the games. He is also able to outrun the falling Peach's Castle with Mario and Luigi ( two grown men ) on his back. The Cloud Flower powerup in Super Mario Galaxy 2 is limited for creating cloud platforms. In the movie, Luigi uses the clouds in combat against the Bowsers. He also gets to create far more clouds than the game’s limit of three. The Comet Observatory can fly through space under its own power. The Power Stars that were required for this in the original game have been Adapted Out . Dry Bowser in the games was finished off by being dropped hard enough to separate his bones. Here, that just results in a Losing Your Head -ish scenario. The Boomsday Machine in Super Mario Galaxy 2 was a multitiered tank which served as Bowser Jr’s last resort, but seemed incapable of any kind of mass destruction. In the movie, it drains Rosalina’s power and is stated to be capable of wiping out planets . Adaptational Context Change : In the original games, Bowser Jr. is very much a young child in both his voice and mannerisms. Here, Benny Safdie's voice performance and various bits of character dialogue imply that his movie-self is a teenager . Bowser Jr.'s Wonder transformation was originally triggered by Bowser's Wonder energy in Super Mario Bros. Wonder . Here, it's the result of Junior coating himself in paint from his Magic Paintbrush. The Galaxy games take place during the Star Festival, an event where a special comet passes over the Mushroom Kingdom skies once a century, bringing about a shower of star bits. Although there is a similar festival in the movie (complete with a star shower), it’s actually for Peach’s birthday — or, more accurately, the anniversary of when she was found by the Toads — with the special comet being Adapted Out . In the first Galaxy game, the UFO was commanded by Bowser in order to seize Princess Peach with her castle. Here, it is Bowser Jr. commanding the UFO to seize the castle in order to rescue his father. Furthermore, Bowser used airships to lift the castle with chains, while Bowser Jr. used the UFO's tractor beam to lift the castle. In Odyssey , the Cascade Kingdom is an island on the same planet as the Mushroom Kingdom that just so happens to be populated with realistic dinosaurs. Here, it is a separate planet. Mr. Game and Watch, instead of being a digital construct that his original games and Super Smash Bros. ran with the idea of, is recontextualized in this movie as a painting brought to life by Luigi attempting to use Bowser Jr.'s paintbrush, with him claiming the minimalist design is due to a lack of actual artistry skills. Much like the realistic dinosaurs in Odyssey , the Lord of Lightning was a non-cartoony dragon who was recruited by Bowser to stall Mario and is native to the Ruined Kingdom on the same planet as the Mushroom Kingdom. Here, The Lord of Lightning is a paint construct created by Junior's Magic Paintbrush as a Hail Mary against Mario, Luigi and Yoshi and his more intense design is implied to be Junior overcompensating for how Luigi took out Dry Bowser with his simple doodle that became Mr. Game and Watch. Adaptational Early Appearance : The form that Bowser Jr. takes when trying to free his father in Peach's Castle is based off his transformation from Super Mario Bros. Wonder , which was released 16 years after Galaxy . Adaptational Expansion : In Galaxy , the Gateway Galaxy was a simple cluster of small planetoids with nothing more than a few cottages and rocks with the main feature being a tower for observation. In the film, Gateway Galaxy is a bustling spaceport with a secret Vice City hidden underneath. Adaptational Explanation : It isn't explained why Bowser Jr didn't appear prior to Super Mario Sunshine in the games. Here, it's stated that he was at boarding school for years prior to the events of the film. In the original Super Mario Bros game, the axe on the bridge is a Deus ex Machina for gameplay purposes. Here, the axe has been combined with the hammers Bowser originally threw and Mario is able to grab a tossed axe to fell the bridge with Bowser on it. In the games, Peach's family is never shown or mentioned, with the only allusion to them in the series' entire history outside the occasional comic being the English instruction manual for the original Super Mario Bros. mentioning her father (the Mushroom King). Here, the film picks up the plot point of Peach's unknown origins from the first movie to reveal that Peach and Rosalina are blood sisters born and made from stardust, making them Ambiguously Human beings from space . In the games, it is never explained why Rosalina looks so much like Peach, with it ultimately just being The Artifact from an early development idea for them to be related. The final game still has Rosalina's mother look like a composite of the two and living in a castle that looks vaguely like Peach's, which combined with Rosalina living though universal resets has resulted in countless theories that one is the other's parent from an alternate timeline. In the movie canon, they are sisters, with Rosalina's own parents and brother being Adapted Out . Adaptational Jerkass : Honey Queen in the games is a kind and pleasant queen that aids Mario. In the movie, she tries to have him and Luigi imprisoned for ruining her flowers , and is rudely dismissive of them even after being convinced to help. She also treats her prisoners as slaves , whether they be Bowser or her own bee subjects. Adaptational Location Change : In the original Super Mario World , Mario met Yoshi on Yoshi's Island note Not the same Yoshi's Island from the first film based on Super Mario World 2: Yoshi's Island , but a separate location with the Japanese name of Yo'ster Island . In the movie, Mario and Luigi instead meet him in Tostarena’s Inverted Pyramid. In Super Mario Sunshine , Mario meets Bowser Jr for what's implied to the first time at Isle Delfino, while Luigi's first interaction with him implicitly occurs a short walking distance away from Peach's Castle in the opening cutscene of New Super Mario Bros. 1 . Here, both brothers meet him for the first time inside the castle. Adaptational Nice Guy : In the games, the Mandibugs were neighbors of the bees that became part of the Koopa Troop after becoming hostile towards them from the first Galaxy game onwards. Here, they're steads of the bees and one helps Mario, Luigi, and Yoshi go to the Gateway Galaxy after Bowser takes their place of working in the Honeyhive Galaxy's mines. Adaptational Ugliness : Compared to the Black Bead Eyes Bowser Jr. has in the games, the bloodshot sclerae around his pupils can be seen here, most likely to make him more expressive and look more threatening in the previews. This is downplayed in the movie proper, since his bloodshot sclerae are only visible in certain shots. Adaptational Villainy : While they tend to be a nuisance whenever they appear as enemies, the Ukikis from the games are at best Mischief Making Monkeys that get a kick out of annoying the protagonists. The Ukiki seen in this movie, however, is an actively malicious thief that manipulates people in order to steal their valuables. They also work for Wart and the 8-Bits, and it's implied that they deliberately lured Peach and Toad to Wart so he could try and kidnap her. Adaptational Wimp : Rosalina effortlessly destroys Megaleg in the opening fight, which is not only smaller than its game counterpart, which was the epitome of Colossus Climb , but puts up even less of a fight. Downplayed when Megaleg manages to survive the damage and remains functional enough to eventually overpower and capture Rosalina. Downplayed with Bowser Jr.'s Magic Paintbrush. While it does amplify his combat prowess significantly, and can still summon paint enemies, he never uses it to turn into Shadow Mario. In the games, Bowser’s Dry Bowser form was invincible and Mario would need outside help to take him down. In the movie, Bowser in said form can still be staggered and even shares the same weakness as Dry Bones if he gets enough hits. Adaptation Amalgamation : Though the film's central story is based on Super Mario Galaxy , Yoshi's biggest scenes take inspiration from Super Mario World and its prequel Super Mario World 2: Yoshi's Island , while locations and characters from Super Mario Bros. 2 and Super Mario Odyssey are also thrown in the mix. The climax features a dramatisised recreation of the original Bowser Fight from Super Mario Bros. 1 and Dry Bowser's resurrection from New Super Mario Bros. 1 . Adapted Out : Bowser Jr.'s Magic Paintbrush uses the design introduced in Super Nintendo World and Bowser's Fury , which bears his personal insignia instead of Professor E. Gadd 's like in Super Mario Sunshine . E. Gadd's insignia does appear on Kamek's coffee cup in the intro, however. Rosalina's backstory was changed so that she and Peach were born from stardust, meaning her mother, father, and brother from the original storybook don't exist. Lubba is absent, with her role in Galaxy 2 essentially given to Fox — a pilot whose ship needs repairs and then escorts the heroes, though in this case the Arwing instead of Starship Mario ). Fox also ends up taking part of Rosalina's role in the original Galaxy game as he pilots the Comet Observatory towards the final battle with Boswer, but stays on the ship rather than joining the action on foot. Advertised Extra : Despite receiving top-billing and being a major character in the original Super Mario Galaxy game, Rosalina doesn't play that much of an active role after her fight against Megaleg in the opening scene due to being captured by Bowser Jr. Birdo was promoted in promotional materials for the film and also made it seem like she was going to be one of the major characters, but in reality, she only has 21 seconds of screen time. Affectionate Gesture to the Head : Surprisingly, Bowser does this to Mario, even making his hat fall off, after Mario steps on him, reversing the effect of the blue mushroom and earning a big kiss from Bowser. Ageless Birthday Episode : Justified; after encountering Yoshi in Tostarena, the brothers attend Peach's birthday party in the Mushroom Kingdom. Her age not being mentioned is justified by Peach noting that the event isn't even her actual birthday, given no one has any clue of her origins, but rather the day the Toads found her in the Mushroom Kingdom. Alas, Poor Villain : While he might have reverted to being a full-on villain by this point, Bowser falling into the lava in his castle is played for drama, with Mario and Peach both horrified to have dealt him such a fate — and even more so when Bowser Jr. starts weeping over his father's demise. At least until he rises back up as Dry Bowser , at which point he and Jr. go back on the attack. Amusing Injuries : The burrowing Monty Mole in the teaser trailer accidentally crashes head first into a boulder. He quickly surfaces and grasps his head in obvious pain. Bowser accidentally shuts the door on his tail, prompting a scream of pain from him. Luigi's dramatic moment where he attacks Bowser Jr. with a knee strike is undercut by him whimpering in pain and rubbing his knee afterwards. Animals Not to Scale : While escaping from the T. rex chasing him, Yoshi runs along the back of a dinosaur skeleton which appears to be a Stegosaurus , judging from its back plates. It is far larger than any stegosaur in real life, as it dwarfs a Tyrannosaurus that is also able to run along its back. Arson, Murder, and Jaywalking : When Peach explains Mario why she's frustrated. Mario: Hey! We're gonna find a way off this planet. Peach: Mario, we're galaxies away from Rosalina, we can't fix the ship, and somehow, we keep adding dinosaurs! When Bowser reminds Peach the "terrible" things she did to him. Princess Peach: Let us pass! Now! Bowser: OR WHAT?! You'll humiliate me at my wedding?! Shrink me again and put me in a jar?! Make me join a book club?! Art Evolution : The film presents more detailed and polished backgrounds than in the previous film, which were already very high quality. The change also includes minor adjustments to the character designs. Art Shift : When Fox McCloud is introduced, his backstory with the Star Fox team and the explanation of how he entered Mario's universe is told in the style of a 90s American action cartoon. When turned into a baby, the T. rex goes from realistic to cartoony, complete with a diaper. Bowser Jr.'s gauntlet is controlled via a screen that renders his view of the action in the style of Super Mario Maker ' s Super Mario Bros. mode. The montage of building Peach a new castle at the end is depicted through spritework based on Super Mario World , with Luigi and Toad's sprites taken from Super Mario Maker 2 and Rosalina given all new sprites based on Peach's. Ascended Extra : The UFO in Galaxy was only in the intro cutscene where it helps cut the ground around Peach's castle, and in the cutscene when Mario goes to the final Bowser level but don't play a role. In the movie, it serves as Bowser Jr.'s flagship when he goes out into space, with scenes set inside the craft. In the first Galaxy , Kamek appears in the beginning to blast Mario away from Peach's castle and into deep space, and then never shows up again after that. In this movie, while he does not attack Mario and company directly, he is shown assisting Bowser and his son more often throughout the story. Ascended Meme : Fox states during his introduction that one of the things he loves doing is "extreme dangerous barrel rolls", with Toad asking him to do one with the line "Do a barrel roll!" later on in the film. Bowser Jr's real-time obstacle course modification brings to life the joke that all the sadistic fan-made levels in Super Mario Maker were really made by Bowser or Bowser Jr. Auto-Tune : R.O.B. speaks with a monotonous voice that often has abrupt pitch shifts several times. Justified, as he is a robot . Ass Kicks You : Peach was seen doing her signature Peach Bomber move from Super Smash Bros. , slamming her butt on one of the Ninjis which sends them flying. Baby Morph Episode : When Bowser Jr. hits the Mario Bros. with a Super Scope, they're turned into babies and have to be rescued from a hungry T. rex in Fossil Falls. Toad later gets turned into a baby during the subsequent chase scene and the T. rex is turned into one when the babies inadvertently use the Super Scope on it. Badass Biker : Invoked with Mario and Luigi, with the sequence of them riding across the Sand Kingdom on motorbikes. The brothers excitedly declare themselves as “Cool Motorcycle Guys” while congratulating each other. Badass in Distress : Powerful as she is, Rosalina spends most of the movie being held by Bowser Jr. She puts up a good fight in the opening, holding her own until her Lumas are threatened, but she was at no point going to win, if the ease with which Bowser Jr. defeats her the second time is any indication. This is due to most of her powers being dependent on her being able to channel magic through her wand, and Junior's magic paint is able to easily prevent this by coating her hands. Bad Guy Bar : Peach and Toad end up in one after chasing a Ukiki for Toad's backpack, finding a bar in the Gateway Underground filled with various Minions such as Goombas, Koopa Troopas, Hammer Bros, Sledge Bros, Octoombas, Octoguys, Chargin' Chucks, Shy Guys, Snifits and Coin Coffers of various colors, who glare at the duo when they enter. Bad Is Good and Good Is Bad : Things that would get any other child a harsh scolding from any reasonable parent, such as getting expelled from boarding school and denouncing friendship, just makes Bowser proud of his little boy. Bait-and-Switch : The teaser begins with a medley of tracks from Super Mario World as the camera pans throughout the Mushroom Kingdom, implying the movie will take cues from said game... before the camera pans all the way up to space to reveal the title, complete with the main theme from Super Mario Galaxy playing in the background. The bait-and-switch was added by a then-recent Universal press release containing release dates for their upcoming films called the film Super Mario World as a false title to hide the Galaxy connection, so Nintendo could reveal it themselves. Bowser's first scene shows him, seemingly restored, painting in his castle... only for a giant Luigi to appear in the window, revealing that Bowser is still shrunken down and imprisoned in Peach's Castle, albeit now in a replica of his own castle. During the "On Patrol" Montage scene, a Chain Chomp is rampaging through the streets of Toad Town, sending the panicking civilians running for their lives. Mario and Luigi stand in its path, posing triumphantly in preparation for the impending fight. Yoshi jumps between them and nips the threat in the bud by eating the Chain Chomp. The flabbergasted looks on the brothers' faces says it all. Once Mario is finally goaded into punching Bowser hard enough to undo the Mini Mushroom's effects, Bowser returns to full size clutching him tightly in one of his claws and staring at him menacingly, making it look like everything he's said about turning over a new leaf was just a lie and he's about to immediately attack him. Instead, he gives Mario a giant smooch on the cheek and promises he won't regret it, showing that he was sincere after all. Bowser ultimately does return to being evil later, but his attempt at redemption was Not an Act . When Peach first introduces the Bros and Yoshi to Fox, Mario and Fox have a long stare-down suggesting that they're going to form an immediate rivalry over Peach's affection. Instead, they genially exchange compliments (Mario likes Fox's ship and Fox likes Mario's mustache) and get along well the whole time, with Fox even commenting at one point, "I like this guy!" When Luigi gets his hands on Bowser Jr.'s paintbrush, it looks like he's about to summon a giant abomination to fight on the heroes' behalf. Instead he draws Mr. Game & Watch, with him stating that he never took art lessons. However , Mr. Game & Watch still has enough power to use Judgment to strike Dry Bowser into skeletal parts. Bash Brothers : After settling into the Mushroom Kingdom, Mario and Luigi are now its protectors. They’re shown to be helping Toads and Tostarenans, as well as carrying out their plumbing duties. Beware the Nice Ones : Luigi is shown to be somewhat friendly towards Bowser, and tries to greet Bowser Jr. and calm the situation down. When Bowser Jr. is dangerously close to killing Mario, Luigi stops him by kneeing him in the face. Big Bad : The entire story begins with Bowser Jr. taking his father's place and remaking the army into a far more fearsome force to the point where he created a miniature planet with a weapon capable of leveling the entire universe with the right power source. Once he properly rescues his father, quickly swaying him to return into his villainous ways , the two become the Big Bad Duumvirate of the movie that needs to be taken down. Big Brother Instinct : Considering that Mario struggles to even look at Bowser in their first scene together, it's clear that he still hasn't gotten over his nemesis nearly killing his brother. Inverted when Mario, Luigi and Yoshi fight Bowser Jr. The Koopa has Mario pinned on the floor, with the only thing keeping Mario from being chopped to pieces from the Koopa kid's paintbrush axe being a book holding the axe in place. Luigi then lunges in, kneecapping Bowser Jr. in the face. After the reveal that Peach and Rosalina are sisters , the latter plays it straight and the former inverts it, too. In the backstory, the teenage Rosalina sent Baby Peach away in a warp pipe to somewhere that she would be safe when villains tried to come after them for their combined power. And once Peach discovers they're related, she becomes even more determined in Storming the Castle with her friends to save her. Big Damn Reunion : Between Peach and Rosalina in the finale, especially after it is revealed they were long-lost sisters. Big "WHAT?!" : Mario lets out one when Luigi teases him on having romantic feelings for Princess Peach. Bizarrchitecture : Peach fights with Birdo, Mouser, and Clawgrip inside a casino that takes up the inside sides of a cube. Black Comedy : One of the amusement park rides on Planet Bowser is a rollercoaster that scorches Koopas with lava, instantly transforming them into Dry Bones. Surprisingly, they seem to be perfectly fine with this. Body Motifs : Hands. Mario attempts to hold hands with Peach after they reunite in the Gateway Galaxy, and later extends his hand to her when the two go through Bowser Jr's. gauntlet . Peach's flashback upon seeing a picture of Princess Rosalina is that of a younger Rosalina letting go of baby Peach's hands as she was sucked into a warp pipe. and both of their powers activate when their hands are pressed together. In the second stinger, Princess Daisy's first shown punching out the Ukiki . Bookends : One that calls back to the first film. The first introduction to the Mario Bros. shows their commercial, which has a tacked-on shot of Mario flying with a cape. This film concludes with Mario flying upward with an actual cape a la Super Mario World for its final shot. Borrowed Catchphrase : The teaser ends on a yellow Luma quoting Mario's famous "let's-a go" phrase. Another Luma then says it in the film proper itself before Mario and the gang head into Bowser’s planet. Brick Joke : In the aftermath of Bowser Jr.'s attempted capture of Peach's castle, it ends up being destroyed and left a pile of rubble in Honeyhive Kingdom . At the end of the film, when Rosalina and the Lumas agree to visit Princess Peach's home , the Mario brothers give each other a quick Oh, Crap! look. Smash cut to everyone staring at the large hole in the Mushroom Kingdom where the castle used to be. After voicing his support for Mario's affections for Peach, Luigi shyly mumbles that maybe she could help find somebody for him too. The final Stinger of the film does just that, introducing Princess Daisy. Brutal Honesty : Bowser shows Mario and Luigi his "masterpiece", a self-aggrandizing portrait of himself with a swooning Peach. While Luigi praises the use of colors , Mario doesn't even bother trying to sugarcoat how he feels and remarks that "it's trash". Call-Back : After Mario and Luigi meet Yoshi within Sand Kingdom's Inverted Pyramid, Mario tells the Tostarenans that they "found the problem," a call-back to the line he says in the first movie after the plumbing scene. Bowser's tiny castle has the same tiny piano from the first movie. Luigi's bad knees from the first movie get a nod with Luigi painfully rubbing one of them after kneeing Bowser Jr.'s Wonder Form in the face. When Mario meets Fox for the first time, he compliments the Arwing. As seen in the first film, Mario owns a model of the same ship. After Peach reunites with Rosalina in the climax, Mario and Luigi meet up with them, and Luigi states, "I love a family reunion," followed up by him and Mario saying "ayy" with an arms open pose that they've used in the first movie after their handshake. The Cameo : Donkey Kong and Foreman Spike make brief appearances in Brooklyn while Yoshi is explaining his time there. Doc Louis and Little Mac also appear as a Freeze-Frame Bonus . Some Pikmin appear at the Gateway Galaxy spaceport, in a Bait-and-Switch for an appearance of Olimar. The rest of Star Fox is shown in Fox's flashback. Luigi conjures up Mr. Game and Watch using Bowser Jr.'s paint brush. During the mid-credits scene, Lumalee makes a return as Bowser and Bowser Jr.'s jailer. In the post-credits scene, Princess Daisy punches out Ukiki before they can steal a tourist's bag. Canon Welding : While the first film subtly alluded to other Nintendo properties through Easter Eggs , this installment utilizes its galactic setting to integrate other franchises into the canon. Fox McCloud , hero of the Star Fox franchise, plays a major supporting role. He was transported to here from the Lylat System when his ship got damaged in a mission, and is currently seeking a way back home. A group of Pikmin is shown landing their ship at Gateway Galaxy. R.O.B. , the robotic peripheral from Nintendo's heyday (and a known Guest Fighter from both Mario Kart DS and Super Smash Bros. ) works at Gateway Galaxy as a desk attendant. Mr. Game & Watch appears as a living drawing, using sounds and moves from Super Smash Bros. . Though this isn't the first time he's been directly associated with the Mario brand, having a subseries of remakes in Game & Watch Gallery and background cameos in-person in games like Donkey Kong Country Returns . Casino Park : The casino that Peach and Toad chase the Ukiki into has a giant roulette wheel on one of its sides, and Peach at one point fights a horde of Ninji on it, even using the giant roulette ball to knock out some of the horde. Central Theme : Families are a recurring theme throughout the movie. Found family can be found throughout the good guys. Yoshi is immediately a part of the Mario gang without hesitation, Rosalina is the mother of the Lumas, and Princess Peach's birthday celebration is celebrating the day the Toads found her. Fox McCloud speaks of fondness of his crew, Star Fox, like family. Family influence. The Toads raised Princess Peach as one of their own, which she reciprocates. Mario only snaps and attacks the shrunken Bowser when Bowser calls him a "failure of a plumber", a reminder of his father's criticisms. Luigi acts like a wingman for Mario's feelings towards Princess Peach. Last because of how much of a Toxic Family Influence Bowser was on his son, Bowser Jr. manages to pull his dad back out of his redemption to conquer the universe. Chekhov's Gag : Played for Drama . While riding a rollercoaster on Planet Bowser, some Koopas get doused in lava and get turned into Dry Bones. When Bowser himself falls in lava, he turns into Dry Bowser. Chekhov's Gun : Peach's parasol which Mario gave her as a gift for the Star Festival. It comes in handy during the fight in Wart's casino lair and later used as a way to get out of a trap Bowser Jr. sets for Mario and Peach. Chekhov's Skill : We see in a flashback that Peach and Rosalina touching hands can send out an energy wave. Peach was younger at the time, so it wasn't very powerful. In the present, when she and Rosalina do so just as the Bowser Jr.'s doomsday machine is powering up, it's enough to destroy it and preform a World-Healing Wave over the planet Bowser Jr. made as a base. Luigi's habit of rapidly throwing things. He first does it with some fireballs after getting spooked by a Buzzy Beetle, then cowardly chucks rocks to block the Warp Pipe containing Yoshi (whom he initially thinks is a monster). In the Final Battle , he barrages Dry Bowser with clouds in the same fashion, and this time, it's not out of cowardice. Chronic Villainy : Unsurprisingly, Bowser chooses to become evil again in the end, despite his claims of having reformed and genuine showcases of respect to Mario throughout the film. Company Cross-References : A few to Super Smash Bros. : During the fight against Bowser Jr.'s Wonder Form, Luigi is shown doing Captain Falcon 's Knee of Justice on Bowser Jr. During Mario and Luigi's investigation of the Sand Kingdom's Inverted Pyramid, Luigi is spooked by a Buzzy Beetle and haphazardly flails fireballs at it. The way he does so is identical to one of his victory animations. One of Peach's attacks while fighting the Ninjis is her side-b move from the games, the Peach Bomber, minus the hearts that appear after a hit. When the Lumas are messing around in the Comet Observatory's library in Rosalina's absence, a pink one does a floating motion while puffing out their cheeks, much like a certain pink puffball. Conservation of Ninjutsu : Wart summons an army of Ninjis to fight Peach and Toad, who have little trouble dealing with them despite their overwhelming numbers. Creative Closing Credits : The credits before the first post-credits scene are depicted in the same style as the puppet show Bowser puts on for Junior, this time recreating various scenes from the movie, with Bowser's hand appearing at times, notably when he pushes down a Mario puppet. Creator Cameo : Ed Skudder, the story supervisor, voices R.O.B. Cringe Comedy : When Mario and Peach reunite in Gateway Galaxy, Peach clearly goes for a hug, but Mario awkwardly goes for a handshake in an attempt to appear "cool", ruining the moment. It's so bad even Luigi, Toad and Yoshi can't help but facepalm . Crossing the Desert : Mario and Luigi’s first scene sees them crossing the Sand Kingdom from Super Mario Odyssey to head into Tostarena. Crying at Your Birthday Party : Mario tells Peach that Luigi cried at his tenth birthday while yelling that his childhood is over. Cultural Translation : In the original dub, the few lines spoken by the Tostarenans (a Fantasy Counterpart Culture of Mexico, with a design inspired by a mishmash of pre-Columbian, Mariachi and Día de Muertos aesthetics) are done with Mexican-sounding but otherwise indistinct voices. In the Latin American Spanish dub, they are instead given a very distinct norteño accent, which better matches the desert environment of Tostarena. Curb-Stomp Battle : Peach easily handles Birdo and later shuts Wart up . The routine missions the Mario Brothers have that involve enemies have the plumbers steamroll their opposition. Damned by Faint Praise : When Bowser shows his painting of himself alongside Peach fawning after him to the Mario Brothers, Luigi says that he likes the colors. Mario simply tells Bowser that his painting is trash . Dem Bones : After Mario cuts the chains holding up the bridge above a river of lava, Bowser falls in. He is briefly believed to be dead... only to climb out in his skeletal "Dry Bowser" form. Demoted to Extra : After serving as a major character in the previous film, Donkey Kong is reduced to making a small cameo during the montage of Yoshi's adventures in Brooklyn this time around. The Baby Luma from Galaxy does not accompany Mario on his quest, instead simply being a part of crowd shots with the rest of the Lumas. Desert Skull : The first shot of the Sand Kingdom depicts a Dry Bones's skull on the sand that a Mini Goomba comes across. Disney Death : Bowser seemingly dies when he falls into a pit of lava, to Bowser Jr.’s distraught… only to emerge back alive as Dry Bowser. Disney Villain Death : Subverted. Bowser seemingly meets his end by falling into a pit of lava… only to come back as Dry Bowser. Dissonant Serenity : Yoshi, Toad, Mario and Luigi (the latter two having been turned into babies via Bowser Jr.'s Super Scope) are facing against the T. rex from Super Mario Odyssey , and the babies aren't scared at all by the giant dinosaur and its roars while Yoshi and Toad are terrified. Does This Remind You of Anything? : After Bowser Jr. returns to Planet Bowser with the captive Rosalina in tow, he brags to the Koopa Troop about kidnapping his first princess, much like a frat boy telling his pals about his conquest of a potential girlfriend he met. The Door Slams You : After his meltdown from Mario's criticism, Bowser goes back into his castle for his eight hours, accidentally slamming the door on his own tail and breathes fire. Dramatic Irony : Bowser takes the blame for ruining Honey Queen's garden, seemingly out of a genuine change of heart so the Mario Bros. can find and save Peach. He fully intended to stay behind and serve their sentence for them; it's just that Bowser Jr. unexpectedly arrived to rescue him from imprisonment . When Bowser accompanies Junior off the bow of their spaceship as his son's gunning after Mario and crew, he's very hesitant to see any harm come to those he genuinely considers his friends. Upon seeing him next to Junior, Mario cynically assumes what Bowser did earlier was a ploy to dupe them, and for extra irony, Bowser requesting a non-lethal attack is what prevents Junior from ending them right there. Unbeknownst to the main cast, it takes Bowser a moment of seeing his son's planet before he slips back into his old ways . Dropped Ice Cream of Sadness : While Mario, Luigi and Yoshi are taking care of the Mushroom Kingdom during Peach's absence, they respond to an incident where a Toad has dropped his ice cream. Mario uses an Ice Flower to conjure a new ice cream for the Toad. Dual Boss : The finale is a final fight against Bowser and his son. Wouldn't seem bad except Bowser turns into Dry Bowser after being knocked into the lava and Bowser Jr. uses the power of the magic paintbush to give himself a super form. Dwelling in the Dollhouse : The still shrunken Bowser has been consigned to a playhouse-sized version of his castle, complete with a miniature bonsai tree and the same tiny piano from the end of the previous movie, as opposed to the birdcage he was imprisoned in. Eat the Camera : The first stinger ends with the camera zooming into Bowser Jr.’s mouth as he screams. Earn Your Happy Ending : Mario spent the movie worrying that Peach would never want to go out with him, but by the end of the film, it's clear that they are on their way to becoming a real item when Peach kisses Mario on the cheek after he helped rebuild her castle. There's also the fact that Everyone Can See It . Peach was able to find out where she came from and that Rosalina is her long-lost sister and was able to rescue and reunite with her. Ending with a Scream : The first stinger ends with Bowser and Bowser Jr. screaming in terror after getting verbally tormented by Lumalee . Entitled to Have You : If Bowser's painting of him (portrayed as majestically buff) and Peach standing together is any indicator, he still shows signs of swooning over Peach. The fact that he threatens to incinerate Mario when the plumber insults his art is proof that Bowser can’t (borderline won't) accept that Peach doesn’t love him. Epic Flail : Bowser Jr. is shown to be capable of transforming his magic paintbrush into one of these. Even Evil Has Loved Ones : Bowser and Bowser Jr. really care about one another. Bowser worries about being a good father to Junior and is concerned that he resents him for sending him off to boarding school, while Junior broke down the front door of Princess Peach's Castle to bust his father out of confinement. Sadly, it's this same love for each other that causes Bowser to give up on reforming, as he decides being a good (read: bad) influence on his son is more important than his newfound respect for the Mario Bros. Everyone Can See It : By the end of the sequel, most characters have taken notice of the blossoming love between Mario and Peach, if the Toad population reacting with joy upon seeing Peach kiss Mario on the cheek after her castle is rebuilt and Luigi and Yoshi teasing Mario is of any indication. Every Man Has His Price : At the Gateway Galaxy, Peach and Toad need a pilot to get them to the Space Junk Galaxy where Rosalina is held, but not a single pilot is interested due to how dangerous that galaxy is. While the mercenary Fox is not as quick to jump in, Toad's hefty payment of coins convinces him otherwise. Evil Is Not a Toy : While the specifics of why are not clear, when Junior overuses the power of his (very evil-looking) magic paint brush to create the most powerful thing he can, the resulting dragon construct immediately breaks free of his control and tries to kill him. He has to be saved by Mario. Exact Words : Bowser Jr. attacks the Bros with a weapon that will "make them cry for their mamas." The weapon de-ages them into crying babies . Expressive Mask : The mouth on Bowser Jr.'s mask moves as he talks while in his Wonder form. Extremely Protective Child : When Bowser Jr. takes Rosalina hostage, one of the Lumas appear and tells Bowser Jr. to leave his mother alone. Extreme Omnivore : Yoshi, as per usual. The Yoshi First Look and Yoshi Chomps Back trailers show him eating Toad's candied apple (stick and all) and a Chain Chomp note which he can't eat in the games . The "Level Up" trailer likewise had him swallow ''Kamek", though he likewise turns him into an egg. Face, Nod, Action : Twice. When the Mario Bros. are about to use a Fire Flower to find a blocked pipe, Mario gives a look to Luigi, and Luigi silently nods before they give themselves the Fire Flower powers. Mario looks at Peach, who gives him a nod, before they exit the Comet Observatory to go to Planet Bowser. Falsely Reformed Villain : Double-subverted. Between his spiteful paintings of Mario, occasional slippage into his usual Hair-Trigger Temper and still wanting to get together with Peach, it seems as if Bowser is just faking his rehabilitation in order to be released and continue terrorizing the Mushroom Kingdom. But after he tricks Mario into making him big again, Bowser shows that he actually did turn over a new leaf and is willing to help Mario, Luigi, and Yoshi. His return to villainy is due to his son's influence, as he decides over the remainder of the film that he values being the villain that his son looks up to more than the relationships and progress he made during the interim, fully abandoning any attempts at reforming by the film's climax. Fingore : After Peach's Castle lands on Honeyhive Galaxy, Bowser tries to run after getting freed, but his small size makes it easy for Mario to catch him. But Bowser gets out of his grip by fiercely biting his thumb. First Kiss : At the end, after her castle is rebuilt, Mario unexpectedly receives one from Peach in the form of a kiss on the cheek. It's significant in that Mario spent the whole movie worrying that Peach wouldn't be interested in him romantically, but this moment proves that he's got nothing to worry about as it indicates that she does like him back. Five-Second Foreshadowing : It's obvious in the first teaser trailer that it's a Mario movie, but the yellow butterfly served as a hint that it's specifically an adaptation of Super Mario Galaxy , as it calls back to the end of the first Galaxy game, where Mario wakes up with a little yellow butterfly on his hat. Bonus points for Mario himself sleeping on the grass in the announcement too. The second stinger shows a short gloved hand punching out the Ukiki. Fans of the character would be able to tell that the specific lacy design of said glove belongs to that of Princess Daisy before her face is shown moments later. Fly-at-the-Camera Ending : The final shot before the credits has Mario donning the super cape from Super Mario World and flying up to put a flag at the top of the newly rebuilt Peach's Castle, before flying higher up to the sky and into the camera. Flying Saucer : A large saucer-shaped spaceship carries Bowser Jr.'s Megaleg away when he has Princess Rosalina. Foreshadowing : Rosalina, for an unknown reason, has been telling strangely accurate stories that focus on Princess Peach and her friends, despite the two having never met before. Later on, Peach discovers that Rosalina is her sister , who sent Peach away for safety but has been keeping track of her ever since. Thus why she knows about Peach and her friends' lives.
