Company Cross-References : A few to Super Smash Bros. : During the fight against Bowser Jr.'s Wonder Form, Luigi is shown doing Captain Falcon 's Knee of Justice on Bowser Jr. During Mario and Luigi's investigation of the Sand Kingdom's Inverted Pyramid, Luigi is spooked by a Buzzy Beetle and haphazardly flails fireballs at it. The way he does so is identical to one of his victory animations. One of Peach's attacks while fighting the Ninjis is her side-b move from the games, the Peach Bomber, minus the hearts that appear after a hit. When the Lumas are messing around in the Comet Observatory's library in Rosalina's absence, a pink one does a floating motion while puffing out their cheeks, much like a certain pink puffball. Conservation of Ninjutsu : Wart summons an army of Ninjis to fight Peach and Toad, who have little trouble dealing with them despite their overwhelming numbers. Creative Closing Credits : The credits before the first post-credits scene are depicted in the same style as the puppet show Bowser puts on for Junior, this time recreating various scenes from the movie, with Bowser's hand appearing at times, notably when he pushes down a Mario puppet. Creator Cameo : Ed Skudder, the story supervisor, voices R.O.B. Cringe Comedy : When Mario and Peach reunite in Gateway Galaxy, Peach clearly goes for a hug, but Mario awkwardly goes for a handshake in an attempt to appear "cool", ruining the moment. It's so bad even Luigi, Toad and Yoshi can't help but facepalm . Crossing the Desert : Mario and Luigi’s first scene sees them crossing the Sand Kingdom from Super Mario Odyssey to head into Tostarena. Crying at Your Birthday Party : Mario tells Peach that Luigi cried at his tenth birthday while yelling that his childhood is over. Cultural Translation : In the original dub, the few lines spoken by the Tostarenans (a Fantasy Counterpart Culture of Mexico, with a design inspired by a mishmash of pre-Columbian, Mariachi and Día de Muertos aesthetics) are done with Mexican-sounding but otherwise indistinct voices. In the Latin American Spanish dub, they are instead given a very distinct norteño accent, which better matches the desert environment of Tostarena. Curb-Stomp Battle : Peach easily handles Birdo and later shuts Wart up . The routine missions the Mario Brothers have that involve enemies have the plumbers steamroll their opposition. Peach easily handles Birdo and later shuts Wart up . The routine missions the Mario Brothers have that involve enemies have the plumbers steamroll their opposition. Damned by Faint Praise : When Bowser shows his painting of himself alongside Peach fawning after him to the Mario Brothers, Luigi says that he likes the colors. Mario simply tells Bowser that his painting is trash . Dem Bones : After Mario cuts the chains holding up the bridge above a river of lava, Bowser falls in. He is briefly believed to be dead... only to climb out in his skeletal "Dry Bowser" form. Demoted to Extra : After serving as a major character in the previous film, Donkey Kong is reduced to making a small cameo during the montage of Yoshi's adventures in Brooklyn this time around. The Baby Luma from Galaxy does not accompany Mario on his quest, instead simply being a part of crowd shots with the rest of the Lumas. The Baby Luma from Galaxy does not accompany Mario on his quest, instead simply being a part of crowd shots with the rest of the Lumas. Desert Skull : The first shot of the Sand Kingdom depicts a Dry Bones's skull on the sand that a Mini Goomba comes across. Disney Death : Bowser seemingly dies when he falls into a pit of lava, to Bowser Jr.’s distraught… only to emerge back alive as Dry Bowser. Disney Villain Death : Subverted. Bowser seemingly meets his end by falling into a pit of lava… only to come back as Dry Bowser. Dissonant Serenity : Yoshi, Toad, Mario and Luigi (the latter two having been turned into babies via Bowser Jr.'s Super Scope) are facing against the T. rex from Super Mario Odyssey , and the babies aren't scared at all by the giant dinosaur and its roars while Yoshi and Toad are terrified. Does This Remind You of Anything? : After Bowser Jr. returns to Planet Bowser with the captive Rosalina in tow, he brags to the Koopa Troop about kidnapping his first princess, much like a frat boy telling his pals about his conquest of a potential girlfriend he met. The Door Slams You : After his meltdown from Mario's criticism, Bowser goes back into his castle for his eight hours, accidentally slamming the door on his own tail and breathes fire. Dramatic Irony : Bowser takes the blame for ruining Honey Queen's garden, seemingly out of a genuine change of heart so the Mario Bros. can find and save Peach. He fully intended to stay behind and serve their sentence for them; it's just that Bowser Jr. unexpectedly arrived to rescue him from imprisonment . When Bowser accompanies Junior off the bow of their spaceship as his son's gunning after Mario and crew, he's very hesitant to see any harm come to those he genuinely considers his friends. Upon seeing him next to Junior, Mario cynically assumes what Bowser did earlier was a ploy to dupe them, and for extra irony, Bowser requesting a non-lethal attack is what prevents Junior from ending them right there. Unbeknownst to the main cast, it takes Bowser a moment of seeing his son's planet before he slips back into his old ways . Dropped Ice Cream of Sadness : While Mario, Luigi and Yoshi are taking care of the Mushroom Kingdom during Peach's absence, they respond to an incident where a Toad has dropped his ice cream. Mario uses an Ice Flower to conjure a new ice cream for the Toad. Dual Boss : The finale is a final fight against Bowser and his son. Wouldn't seem bad except Bowser turns into Dry Bowser after being knocked into the lava and Bowser Jr. uses the power of the magic paintbush to give himself a super form. Dwelling in the Dollhouse : The still shrunken Bowser has been consigned to a playhouse-sized version of his castle, complete with a miniature bonsai tree and the same tiny piano from the end of the previous movie, as opposed to the birdcage he was imprisoned in. Eat the Camera : The first stinger ends with the camera zooming into Bowser Jr.’s mouth as he screams. Earn Your Happy Ending : Mario spent the movie worrying that Peach would never want to go out with him, but by the end of the film, it's clear that they are on their way to becoming a real item when Peach kisses Mario on the cheek after he helped rebuild her castle. There's also the fact that Everyone Can See It . Peach was able to find out where she came from and that Rosalina is her long-lost sister and was able to rescue and reunite with her. Mario spent the movie worrying that Peach would never want to go out with him, but by the end of the film, it's clear that they are on their way to becoming a real item when Peach kisses Mario on the cheek after he helped rebuild her castle. There's also the fact that Everyone Can See It . Peach was able to find out where she came from and that Rosalina is her long-lost sister and was able to rescue and reunite with her. Ending with a Scream : The first stinger ends with Bowser and Bowser Jr. screaming in terror after getting verbally tormented by Lumalee . Entitled to Have You : If Bowser's painting of him (portrayed as majestically buff) and Peach standing together is any indicator, he still shows signs of swooning over Peach. The fact that he threatens to incinerate Mario when the plumber insults his art is proof that Bowser can’t (borderline won't) accept that Peach doesn’t love him. Epic Flail : Bowser Jr. is shown to be capable of transforming his magic paintbrush into one of these. Even Evil Has Loved Ones : Bowser and Bowser Jr. really care about one another. Bowser worries about being a good father to Junior and is concerned that he resents him for sending him off to boarding school, while Junior broke down the front door of Princess Peach's Castle to bust his father out of confinement. Sadly, it's this same love for each other that causes Bowser to give up on reforming, as he decides being a good (read: bad) influence on his son is more important than his newfound respect for the Mario Bros. Everyone Can See It : By the end of the sequel, most characters have taken notice of the blossoming love between Mario and Peach, if the Toad population reacting with joy upon seeing Peach kiss Mario on the cheek after her castle is rebuilt and Luigi and Yoshi teasing Mario is of any indication. Every Man Has His Price : At the Gateway Galaxy, Peach and Toad need a pilot to get them to the Space Junk Galaxy where Rosalina is held, but not a single pilot is interested due to how dangerous that galaxy is. While the mercenary Fox is not as quick to jump in, Toad's hefty payment of coins convinces him otherwise. Evil Is Not a Toy : While the specifics of why are not clear, when Junior overuses the power of his (very evil-looking) magic paint brush to create the most powerful thing he can, the resulting dragon construct immediately breaks free of his control and tries to kill him. He has to be saved by Mario. Exact Words : Bowser Jr. attacks the Bros with a weapon that will "make them cry for their mamas." The weapon de-ages them into crying babies . Expressive Mask : The mouth on Bowser Jr.'s mask moves as he talks while in his Wonder form. Extremely Protective Child : When Bowser Jr. takes Rosalina hostage, one of the Lumas appear and tells Bowser Jr. to leave his mother alone. Extreme Omnivore : Yoshi, as per usual. The Yoshi First Look and Yoshi Chomps Back trailers show him eating Toad's candied apple (stick and all) and a Chain Chomp note which he can't eat in the games . The "Level Up" trailer likewise had him swallow ''Kamek", though he likewise turns him into an egg. Face, Nod, Action : Twice. When the Mario Bros. are about to use a Fire Flower to find a blocked pipe, Mario gives a look to Luigi, and Luigi silently nods before they give themselves the Fire Flower powers. Mario looks at Peach, who gives him a nod, before they exit the Comet Observatory to go to Planet Bowser. When the Mario Bros. are about to use a Fire Flower to find a blocked pipe, Mario gives a look to Luigi, and Luigi silently nods before they give themselves the Fire Flower powers. Mario looks at Peach, who gives him a nod, before they exit the Comet Observatory to go to Planet Bowser. Falsely Reformed Villain : Double-subverted. Between his spiteful paintings of Mario, occasional slippage into his usual Hair-Trigger Temper and still wanting to get together with Peach, it seems as if Bowser is just faking his rehabilitation in order to be released and continue terrorizing the Mushroom Kingdom. But after he tricks Mario into making him big again, Bowser shows that he actually did turn over a new leaf and is willing to help Mario, Luigi, and Yoshi. His return to villainy is due to his son's influence, as he decides over the remainder of the film that he values being the villain that his son looks up to more than the relationships and progress he made during the interim, fully abandoning any attempts at reforming by the film's climax. Fingore : After Peach's Castle lands on Honeyhive Galaxy, Bowser tries to run after getting freed, but his small size makes it easy for Mario to catch him. But Bowser gets out of his grip by fiercely biting his thumb. First Kiss : At the end, after her castle is rebuilt, Mario unexpectedly receives one from Peach in the form of a kiss on the cheek. It's significant in that Mario spent the whole movie worrying that Peach wouldn't be interested in him romantically, but this moment proves that he's got nothing to worry about as it indicates that she does like him back. Five-Second Foreshadowing : It's obvious in the first teaser trailer that it's a Mario movie, but the yellow butterfly served as a hint that it's specifically an adaptation of Super Mario Galaxy , as it calls back to the end of the first Galaxy game, where Mario wakes up with a little yellow butterfly on his hat. Bonus points for Mario himself sleeping on the grass in the announcement too. The second stinger shows a short gloved hand punching out the Ukiki. Fans of the character would be able to tell that the specific lacy design of said glove belongs to that of Princess Daisy before her face is shown moments later. Fly-at-the-Camera Ending : The final shot before the credits has Mario donning the super cape from Super Mario World and flying up to put a flag at the top of the newly rebuilt Peach's Castle, before flying higher up to the sky and into the camera. Flying Saucer : A large saucer-shaped spaceship carries Bowser Jr.'s Megaleg away when he has Princess Rosalina. Foreshadowing : Rosalina, for an unknown reason, has been telling strangely accurate stories that focus on Princess Peach and her friends, despite the two having never met before. Later on, Peach discovers that Rosalina is her sister , who sent Peach away for safety but has been keeping track of her ever since. Thus why she knows about Peach and her friends' lives. In Yoshi's flashback, Yoshi gets in trouble after accidentally knocking over the skeleton of a Tyrannosaurus rex in a Brooklyn museum. He would later end up getting into trouble with a real live T. rex and then facing a real undead reptilian skeleton . Early in the film, while Luigi is convincing Mario to ask Peach out, he makes a note of asking him to ask her if she has a friend he could hook up with. While his doesn't come to pass within the movie itself, it does hint at the second Stinger revealing Princess Daisy, Luigi's Implied Love Interest in the games . After Mario calls Bowser's painting "trash", Bowser gets enraged and threatens Mario, saying he'll "burn the skin off [Mario's] bones". Guess what happens to Bowser in the final battle? During the tour of Planet Bowser, one of the rides has several Koopa Troopas riding a roller coaster. The ride dunks them into lava, where they harmlessly come out as Dry Bones. For those who may not be versed in Dry Bowser lore, this hints that Bowser falling into lava wouldn't kill him but rather turn him into an undead skeleton . Speaking of which, Bowser at one point paints a picture of Mario as a skeleton. Bowser himself would become a skeleton in the form of Dry Bowser when Mario drops him in a lava pit during the climax. Freeze-Frame Bonus : The fight between Mario and Bowser Jr.'s Wonder form has the former use a book to trap Bowser Jr.'s paintbrush axe, and the page shown right before it happens is a recipe that has a Theet decoration on it. Two ships that are in the background in the flight deck are the Falcon Flyer and Samus's gunship . The Mario Bros.' NES has made it to their house in the Mushroom Kingdom, and it looks like they've been playing Excitebike recently. Friendly Enemy : Bowser is on better terms with Luigi than Mario this time around, greeting Luigi more sincerely and Luigi complimenting Bowser's painting, which Mario calls "trash". In fact, after regaining his size, he actually helps them , where he willingly let himself stay on the Honeyhive Galaxy to work out the damage they unintentionally caused while the two brothers and Yoshi are sent to find Princess Peach at the Gateway Galaxy. Sadly, he decides to return to his villainous ways for his son's sake and any real friendliness he showed for the Bros. before pretty much dies during the climax of the film. Furry Confusion : During Yoshi’s flashback of him in Brooklyn, he goes into a museum and accidentally knocks down the skeleton of a T. rex . Later, while stranded in Fossil Falls, Yoshi, a cartoony, semi-anthropomorphic dinosaur, encounters... the realistic and non-anthropomorphic Tyrannosaurus rex that first appeared in Super Mario Odyssey . Getting Eaten Is Harmless : Three different occasions by three different characters, no less: The first is Kamek getting eaten by Yoshi, popping out of him as an egg. Kamek later emerges from the egg no worse for wear. The second is Baby Mario venturing into the alien T-Rex 's mouth. Granted, he (probably) never got further down than that and he was only in there for a couple of seconds, but a dinosaur's mouth likely isn't very pleasant either. The last one is Bowser Jr. being eaten by the Ruined Dragon he painted. Pretty justified since it was just made out of ink and not intended to have a digestive system. Granted, he (probably) never got further down than that and he was only in there for a couple of seconds, but a dinosaur's mouth likely isn't very pleasant either. The last one is Bowser Jr. being eaten by the Ruined Dragon he painted. Pretty justified since it was just made out of ink and not intended to have a digestive system. Giant Mook : A Big Cheep Cheep appears eating a Blue Toad. It takes Luigi in a Frog Suit saving that Toad by dive kicking the creature. Big Goombas also appear in the movie, where they are fought by Mario, Luigi, and Yoshi at a location that resembles both Giant Land from their game debut and Supermassive Galaxy from Super Mario Galaxy 2 . Going Native : Hilariously, Yoshi's flashback reveals that after being born on Earth, he travelled around and acted no different from a tourist. Visiting different places while adjusting to the city rapidly with no intent in returning home... until he caused a dinosaur skeleton to collapse at the Museum of Natural History and was forced to run away or be jailed. Go-Karting with Bowser : Deconstructed. Bowser is trying to rehabilitate himself, and he does so by attempting to befriend the Mario Bros. with painting showcases and book club sessions. Luigi is convinced by Bowser's progress and is open to lighten Bowser's sentence (namely letting him grow big). Mario, however, believes Bowser is faking his friendliness so he can then escape and get his revenge on the brothers for defeating him last time. It turns out that Bowser is genuinely trying to reform, as he helps the brothers out even after becoming big again. But Bowser Jr. finds his dad's softness on his former enemies to be a weakness and so he convinces his father that friends will eventually backstab him while family is forever. Bowser ends up sliding back into a villain and attempts to kill Mario, Luigi, and Peach for his son's happiness . Good Is Not Soft : Due to the events of the previous movie ( especially the ones about endangering Luigi ), Mario holds a very visible grudge over Bowser, and isn't hesitant to show his contempt for him by doing things like insult his painting as seen in the first trailer. Good Parents : Bowser is evil and goes back to Big Bad status again, but he is very much a good parent to his son, who loves and adores Bowser in turn and wants to be just as evil. The Greatest Story Never Told : Played for Laughs . Yoshi is more than happy to recount his tale of living in New York before coming back to the Mushroom Kingdom, but since he only speaks Yoshi, the audience sees it while Mario and Luigi are clueless. Mario: I guess we'll never know. Greater-Scope Villain : The unseen great evil that wanted Peach and Rosalina's powers, resulting in Rosalina sending Peach far away to the Mushroom Kingdom. Green-Eyed Monster : Toad is a bit jealous of the newly added Yoshi upon being introduced to him by Mario and Luigi. In the Japanese version, he's specifically jealous of how Mario and Luigi seem to think he's cuter, with cute being an important part of the Japanese movie Toads' culture. Hand Signals : Mario attempts to use an elaborate set of hand signals to communicate that he and Peach will go one way, while Yoshi and Luigi should go another. Luigi doesn't understand it at all. Harmless Freezing : During the "On Patrol" Montage of fixing the Mushroom Kingdom's problems, Ice Mario fist-bumps Luigi but accidentally freezes him in a block of ice in the process after fixing a Toad's ice cream. Hard Truth Aesop : People have the capacity to change, but can just refuse to. Both Mario Bros. give Bowser the benefit of the doubt and even Bowser hesitated over his son wanting to murder the brothers, but eventually (thanks to his son's influence), he decides to fully revert to being a villain. Hates Reading : It’s implied Bowser might hate reading, since his tirade against Peach in the climax has him mention being forced to join a book club with Luigi as one of the things he’s mad at her for . Heel–Face Revolving Door : Bowser appears to have reformed during his time as Peach's prisoner, and genuinely grows to respect the Mario Bros. throughout their adventure. Unfortunately, however, his son's Toxic Family Influence ends up causing him to go back to being evil in the climax. And unlike other examples of this trope, it is highly unlikely that he will try to go back and be good and a better person ever again, not only because he actively doesn't want to anymore , but by this point, he has already gone well past that point . Held Gaze : Like in the first film, Mario and Peach exchange these of the romantic variety to show how much they've grown to care (maybe even love) each other. Heroic BSoD : When Peach first spots a poster of Rosalina at the Gateway Galaxy, seeing the space princess's face for the first time, she freezes up as she has a brief traumatic flashback of her as a baby being forced to be sent away by a younger Rosalina and is left shell shocked for a brief while, needing Toad to snap her out of it. Hidden Depths : For the cruel, tyrannical monster he is, Bowser is shown to be a very good painter, on par with his piano skills. He is also a part of a book club with Luigi. Hit Me, Dammit! : Bowser looks for ways for Mario to punch him so he can grow back to normal size. He eventually gets it when he calls Mario a "failure of a plumber" (triggering how Mario's father initially looked down on the brothers going out on their own) so he punches the Koopa King. Hoist by His Own Petard : During the climax, Bowser Jr. creates the Ruined Dragon with his paintbrush, but in the midst of doing his Pre-Asskicking One-Liner , he immediately gets eaten and has to be saved by Mario. Hope Spot : When Bowser Jr. transports the captive Rosalina to Planet Bowser and announces his intentions to use her power to destroy the universe as well as her Lumas, she angrily explodes her way out of her restraints and starts fighting her way through Jr.'s army of minions. But before she can escape entirely, Bowser Jr. uses his Magic Paintbrush to cover her in paint, immobilizing her and putting her into a much stronger cage. When Peach's castle crashes in the Honeyhive Galaxy, Mario, Luigi and Yoshi race to avoid getting crushed underneath. They seem to evade this fate, and breath a sigh of relief... just before the castle tilts in their direction. Horrifying the Horror : Lumalee is the warden of the prison Dry Bowser and Bowser Jr. got locked up in, and his fatalistic ramblings manage to make both villains scream in horror. Human Aliens : As it turns out, both Princess Peach and Rosalina, who are sisters in the series, are cosmic beings made of stardust who simply look human. Hypocritical Humor : Toad takes an immediate dislike to Yoshi, complaining about how Mario and Luigi are allowing a "dinosaur" to join "the group" despite not knowing him for long. Not only is Toad a talking mushroom humanoid, but he invited himself on Mario and Peach's quest in the first movie despite barely knowing Mario at the time. Iconic Attribute Adoption Moment : Mario gives Peach her signature parasol from the games as a birthday present, which she uses throughout the film. Interspecies Romance : Not quite full-on romance just yet, but by the end, Mario and Peach are clearly on their way to becoming a real item, and this was after it was revealed that Peach, alongside her sister, is a human alien made from stardust. In the Hood : During their visit to the Sand Kingdom, Mario and Luigi are seen wearing cloaks with goggles whilst in the desert, likely to protect themselves from the heat and sand rather than as a disguise. I Resemble That Remark! : When Mario tries to calm Peach down by acknowledging she's frustrated, Peach yells "Princesses don't get frustrated!" Jumping Fish : As the camera pans over a lake in the teaser, a school of Cheep-Cheeps leap in and out of the water. Just Here for Godzilla : In-Universe . After the Lumas practically beg Rosalina to read the story about the Mario Brothers, she barely gets a word in before they ask her to skip ahead to the part with Donkey Kong . Know When to Fold 'Em : Yoshi tries to frighten off a Tyrannosaurus rex by roaring at it. Unfortunately, the T. rex does a far more efficient roar that causes Yoshi and Toad (who's carrying Baby Mario and Baby Luigi) to run away in panic. Lady of War : Like in the last film, Peach once more shows off her fighting skills as she takes on some of Bowser and Wart's forces. The beginning of the movie also shows Rosalina single-handedly decimating a Megaleg using only her wand. Lampshade Hanging : Toad lampshades how Yoshi quickly joins the brothers and becomes part of the gang after his introduction. Wart takes a jab at how princesses seem to have a habit of getting kidnapped. Toad lampshades how Yoshi quickly joins the brothers and becomes part of the gang after his introduction. Wart takes a jab at how princesses seem to have a habit of getting kidnapped. Laser-Guided Karma : In the first movie, Bowser had captured Lumalee, whose imprisonment seems to have shaped her nihilistic personality. But as seen in The Stinger for this film, Lumalee has now become the guard at the prison where Bowser and his son have been imprisoned, and intends to make sure that they're the ones suffering now. Bowser attempted to dunk numerous innocents in cages in a pit of lava in the first movie. Here he ends up being dunked into a lava pit himself after Mario cut off the supports in self-defense. Bowser attempted to dunk numerous innocents in cages in a pit of lava in the first movie. Here he ends up being dunked into a lava pit himself after Mario cut off the supports in self-defense. Late-Arrival Spoiler : The first official trailer opens with Bowser's still miniature size, which he was reduced to after the final battle in the first movie. Le Parkour : The "On Patrol" Montage has a brief sequence of Mario, Luigi and Yoshi jumping and vaulting their way past a pair of Lakitus in the Clouds, before jumping one by one into a Warp Pipe with the Blue Toad they rescued from the Big Cheep Cheep. Lighter and Softer : While Bowser Jr. has higher goals than his father did in the first film with larger stakes and comes close to killing Rosalina and the heroes a few times , the film is considerably lighter than the first being bereft of a major character arc for the protagonists who have moved past the self-doubt they struggled with in the first one. There are less pathos-driven scenes, less scenes of peril such as the attempted mass ritualistic sacrifice on Bowser's prisoners, and a much more adventurous plot and tone as a result. This is especially the case compared to the original Galaxy . Rosalina no longer has her original backstory or mother she had to accept was gone forever, instead being born from stardust and getting to happily reunite with her only living relative. The universe does not get destroyed and recreated either, with Bowser's attempt at doing so being prevented. The stakes are still higher than they were in Galaxy 2 , however. Living Battery : The main reason Bowser Jr. kidnaps Rosalina, to have her cosmic power be used as the power source for his doomsday weapon. Logo Joke : The Illumination logo has one of the Minions grab the hammer power-up from Donkey Kong (1981) , only to go on a rampage when another Minion plays the Hammer theme from that game, destroying the Illumination logo and smashing the latter into the ground in the process. The Nintendo logo features a small animation at the end commemorating the 40th anniversary of Super Mario Bros. 1 . invoked The Illumination logo has one of the Minions grab the hammer power-up from Donkey Kong (1981) , only to go on a rampage when another Minion plays the Hammer theme from that game, destroying the Illumination logo and smashing the latter into the ground in the process. Mario and Luigi are clear favorites outside of Brooklyn and the Mushroom Kingdom. Luxury Prison Suite : For Bowser, his birdcage prison has been upgraded to what looks like a toy playset version of his castle fortress, complete with some amenities like scaled-down painting supplies. Mama Bear : Rosalina is shown to be very protective of her adopted children the Lumas, even willing to defend them when Bowser Jr. attacked. Meaningful Gift : Mario gifts Princess Peach her trademark parasol to utilize as a weapon and as a parachute to escape from harm. Medium-Shift Gag : During Fox's introduction, he explains his brief backstory in a more anime style animation in trying to hype himself up. And within that same backstory, the brief comment on blaming Slippy Toad for his warp-drive malfunction cuts to Slippy's profile from Star Fox 64 . Misplaced Retribution : Queen Bee quickly decides to punish Mario, Luigi, and Yoshi just because Peach's castle fell into her domain when really it was an unintentional accident. Regardless, she doesn't give the boys a chance to explain themselves and would've thrown all of them into the mines if Bowser hadn't volunteered to serve the punishment in the former three's place in exchange for letting them go. Moral Myopia : Before Mario and Peach are about to fight Bowser at Bowser Planet, Bowser angrily recalls how Peach "humiliated him at his wedding". Of course Bowser failed to mention that before Peach did that he invaded her kingdom and tortured one of her friends in order to force her to marry him. Morph Weapon : The first trailer and its extended version shows Bowser Jr. changing the tip of his Magic Paintbrush into different weapons, like a flail to throw at the Mario brothers, a spear, or an axe. Motifs : Skeletons are shown throughout the movie. The opening shot of the movie after the prologue is a Dry Bones run over by Mario and Luigi on their motorbikes. One of Bowser's paintings is a skeletal Mario. Yoshi flees New York City after destroying a dinosaur skeleton in a museum and runs on a dinosaur skeleton bridge in Fossil Forest. In Planet Bowser, a bunch of Koopas turn into Dry Bones after a dip in lava, the same with Bowser via Dry Bowser. Mundane Utility : Mario and Luigi using their fire forms via their Fire Flowers so their fireballs can be used as torches in the Inverted Pyramid. Kamek uses his wand as a airport/construction signal lighter. Mario uses the power of an Ice Flower to conjure an ice cream cone for a Toad. Musical Nod : An orchestral version of the Overworld theme from Super Mario World can be heard in the teaser as the camera flies through the kingdom. Another appearance of it is in the scene in the Yoshi First Look trailer where Mario is asking Yoshi to come out. At the end of the teaser, the opening notes of the main theme of Super Mario Galaxy play during the Title Drop . The little ditty Bowser hums to himself is in the same tune as his theme from Super Mario 64 . Rosalina's battle with Megaleg at the end of the first trailer is underscored by a rearrangement of the opening fanfare of the Gusty Garden Galaxy theme. When Mario and Luigi are riding their motorcycles on the way to Tostarena, the title screen music from the obscure Japan-only game "Famicom Grand Prix II: 3D Hot Rally" can be heard playing, as the game featured Mario and Luigi in promotional artwork. The scene of Frog Luigi rescuing a Toad is fittingly underscored by the Underwater music from Super Mario Bros. 3 . The scene where the UFO lasers the ground around Peach’s Castle and lifts it into space comes complete with the same theme that played when it happened in the original Galaxy game, along with Bowser Jr’s theme from that game as well when he first appears before Mario, Luigi and Yoshi. The hidden casino has a rendition of " Jump Up, Super Star! " playing. As tension builds with Wart, the score references the boss theme from Super Mario Bros. 2 before incorporating the final boss theme shortly after. When a tiny spaceship carrying Pikmin lands on the Gateway Galaxy, a small snippet of the "End of Day" theme from their home series plays. Fox's introduction is fittingly underscored by the main theme from Star Fox 64 , while the Arwing taking fire from Bowser Jr. is set to " Venom " (popularized as the Corneria stage music in Super Smash Bros. My God, What Have I Done? : When Bowser falls to the lava and everyone assumes he perished, Mario and Peach look at each other in shock when they see Junior mourning him once it hits them that they may have rendered him an orphan. Mythology Gag : Still too many to count as in the previous film, so it has its own page here . During the casino fight, Birdo fires eggs at Peach while the latter uses her parasol as a shield. Birdo has been able to do this since its first appearance in Super Mario Bros. 2 , while Peach using her parasol has been a recurring ability in games like Super Princess Peach and Super Paper Mario , among other titles. Mouser and Clawgrip, also from Super Mario Bros. 2 , can also be seen in the scene. The chase for Ukiki in the Gateway Galaxy and its Underground is much like in the Yoshi's Island games and Super Mario 64 , where players chase them if they steal babies or caps. Another movie poster depicts Peach and Toad looking up at the sky. In other parts of it you can see Piantas , Star Bunnies , Whittles , and a Conkdor . There is also a planet in the background that heavily resembles the Gateway Galaxy serving as the very first planet in the first Galaxy game. Bowser Jr. pins Rosalina against a chair with a purple goopy Piranha Plant known as a Painted Piranha Plant when she tries to fight back on the under construction Planet Bowser. The reference here is twofold: both to the Proto Piranhas that Bowser Jr made with his brush in his debut game , and this particular Piranha Plant greatly resembles the Cloud Piranhas . The latter is especially appropriate, given Jr is in his Wonder appearance when he does this. Among the holographic signs in the Gateway Underground is the Nintendo 64 logo. Peach defeats Wart by throwing a vegetable into his mouth, just like how he is defeated in Super Mario Bros. 2 . After a failed attempt at reaching Bowser Jr.'s flying saucer, Fox's Arwing crash lands on a Dinosaur Planet . Even his mission to save the god-like Rosalina from being weaponized as a Planet Destroyer by Bowser Jr. is similar to how his old nemesis Andross weaponized the Krazoa Spirits to return to life as an Eldritch Abomination . The powered-up form Bowser Jr. takes on via his magic paintbrush has elements of both Bowser's "Fury" form from Bowser's Fury , and his own "Wonder" form from Super Mario Bros. Wonder . When Luigi suggests that they rebuild Peach's castle, Rosalina puts on a construction workers hat, much like E. Gadd, Luigi and the ghosts did when they prepared to rebuild The Last Resort hotel in Luigi's Mansion 3 . Birdo has been able to do this since its first appearance in Super Mario Bros. 2 , while Peach using her parasol has been a recurring ability in games like Super Princess Peach and Super Paper Mario , among other titles. Mouser and Clawgrip, also from Super Mario Bros. 2 , can also be seen in the scene. The chase for Ukiki in the Gateway Galaxy and its Underground is much like in the Yoshi's Island games and Super Mario 64 , where players chase them if they steal babies or caps. Another movie poster depicts Peach and Toad looking up at the sky. In other parts of it you can see Piantas , Star Bunnies , Whittles , and a Conkdor . There is also a planet in the background that heavily resembles the Gateway Galaxy serving as the very first planet in the first Galaxy game. Bowser Jr. pins Rosalina against a chair with a purple goopy Piranha Plant known as a Painted Piranha Plant when she tries to fight back on the under construction Planet Bowser. The reference here is twofold: both to the Proto Piranhas that Bowser Jr made with his brush in his debut game , and this particular Piranha Plant greatly resembles the Cloud Piranhas . The latter is especially appropriate, given Jr is in his Wonder appearance when he does this. Among the holographic signs in the Gateway Underground is the Nintendo 64 logo. Peach defeats Wart by throwing a vegetable into his mouth, just like how he is defeated in Super Mario Bros. 2 . After a failed attempt at reaching Bowser Jr.'s flying saucer, Fox's Arwing crash lands on a Dinosaur Planet . Even his mission to save the god-like Rosalina from being weaponized as a Planet Destroyer by Bowser Jr. is similar to how his old nemesis Andross weaponized the Krazoa Spirits to return to life as an Eldritch Abomination . The powered-up form Bowser Jr. takes on via his magic paintbrush has elements of both Bowser's "Fury" form from Bowser's Fury , and his own "Wonder" form from Super Mario Bros. Wonder . When Luigi suggests that they rebuild Peach's castle, Rosalina puts on a construction workers hat, much like E. Gadd, Luigi and the ghosts did when they prepared to rebuild The Last Resort hotel in Luigi's Mansion 3 . But just as she's about to finish it off, one of her Lumas peeks out from the door, and Megaleg immediately fires on it, and by extension, the other Lumas. Forced into a Sadistic Choice , Rosalina immediately throws herself at the Bullet Bill, freezing it right before impact and throwing it away, but that leaves her wide open for Megaleg to strike her from behind and capture her. When Bowser Jr. taunts the captive Rosalina by threatening the Lumas, she grows furious, escapes her restraints, and carves through his army only for him to stop her himself with magic paint and put her in a more secure prison. Nothing Is Scarier : The unknown evil entity/forces that attacked Peach and Rosalina as children and tried to use their combined power for nefarious purposes are not shown at all onscreen and that is actively done on purpose to give their presence more of an eerie mystery as to what the heck they actually are and why they want Peach and Rosalina's combined power. Not Quite Dead : When Bowser attacks Mario and Peach on a bridge, Mario causes the bridge to collapse, leaving Bowser to fall into the lava where Bowser Jr. could only cry out, believing his father to be dead with Mario and Peach thinking the same. Bowser comes back as Dry Bowser not long after, ready to continue the fight with his son . Object-Tracking Shot : The teaser trailer follows the journey of a butterfly, which takes off from a napping Mario's hat, flies across the Mushroom Kingdom up to Princess Peach's castle, and into the sky before finally landing on the movie's logo above the clouds. Offscreen Karma : The last shot of Bowser and Bowser Jr. has them trying to pick up the pieces of Bowser's skeleton to reassemble him, with the heroes having seemingly forgotten about them. Then The Stinger reveals they were captured off-screen and thrown in jail. they’re going to escape the prison, but it might take them a while with that prison guard Lumalee, and it would take Bowser a long time to get back to normal. Ominous Crack : The Koopa Troop's UFO using lasers to cut Peach's Castle out of the ground shows the iconic stained glass window portrait of Peach starting to crack, before her face shatters and then things get worse. One-Man Army : Peach practically decimates all of Wart's forces, including Birdo and Mouser, which some assistance from Toad. Even his army of Ninjis barely slows her down and she has virtually no trouble taking care of Wart personally and forcing him to cough up Rosalina's location. Once More, with Clarity : The film begins when Rosalina begins reading a bedtime story about Princess Peach, before the Lumas ask her to talk about the Mario Brothers again and Bowser Jr. arrives at her doorstep. Later, when the Lumas pick up Mario, Peach, and their allies, Peach finds and reads the same story book. Leading to a flashback of the first film, it reveals that Rosalina and Peach are sisters with cosmic powers, but Rosalina had to send her away when an unknown force comes after them. The book ends with hopes that Peach would grow up to protect others, leading to her being the ruler of the Mushroom Kingdom. The Oner : Nearly the entire teaser is a continuous shot, following a butterfly soaring over a sleeping Mario, the Mushroom Kingdom, and Peach's Castle before ending in the sky above. "On Patrol" Montage : After Peach departs into space to find and rescue Rosalina, a brief montage of Mario, Luigi and Yoshi protecting the Mushroom Kingdom follows. Out of Focus : While still a major character, Bowser's appearance is slightly downplayed in lieu of Bowser Jr. He doesn't even appear in the proper movie until nearly a third in. Overly Long Gag : Peach and Toad ask R.O.B. where they can find pilots at the Gateway Galaxy, but his movements when pointing towards the map monitor goes very slowly, doing it enough times with Peach demanding a quicker answer. Mario, Luigi and Yoshi later end up experiencing the same issue. Over-the-Top Roller Coaster : At Planet Bowser, several Koopa Troopas are shown riding a roller that immediately plunges into a Lava Pit , shortly before all of them emerge out of it as Dry Bones. Parasol of Pain : A clip reveals Peach receiving a parasol from Mario as a birthday gift, and trailers show her using said parasol to fight Ninjis and defend against Birdo's eggs. Planet Destroyer : Key to Bowser Jr.'s scheme is a weapon on Planet Bowser designed to destroy worlds, using Rosalina's powers as the fuel. As Peach and Rosalina managed to invoke their cosmic powers to terraform Planet Bowser before the weapon was fully active, it isn't known if the weapon truly can destroy worlds. Post-Victory Collapse : After a long hard day of traveling all over the Mushroom Kingdom helping anyone who needs help, the Mario Bros. and Yoshi can only lie down on the floor completely exhausted. Mario and Luigi don't even have the energy to do their usual hand bump. Power Trio : Mario, Luigi and Yoshi become this after the Bros find Yoshi in the Inverted Pyramid. During the "On Patrol" Montage , they’re seen rescuing Toads and stopping a Chain Chomp from terrorising Toad Town, and later work together to find Peach and Rosalina and defeat Bowser and Bowser Jr. Pragmatic Adaptation : While it's based on Super Mario Galaxy , the movie is less a retelling of that game's story and more an original Mario movie that reuses the "Mario in space" idea and reimagines characters and concepts from Galaxy to tell its own story, not unlike the first movie. Given how long Galaxy is (being 13-15 hours without 100% completion), on top of the film having to juggle other elements not from Galaxy , like the introduction of Yoshi and Bowser Jr., this is an understandable decision. Rosalina's backstory from the game is replaced with a simpler backstory that explains her and Peach to be sisters made from stardust . Likely because Rosalina's original backstory had little plot relevance (even in the Galaxy game, it was an optional Lore Codex that was isolated from the main plot) and would've clashed too much with the film's more lighthearted and comedic tone compared to the game (since part of it deals with Rosalina grieving the death of her mother ). The new backstory, while different, is more in line with the films tonally, gives Rosalina a stronger connection to one of the characters right off the bat, allows Peach to have a more significant role in the story, and ties back to the first film by building upon Peach's backstory from that film . Because of Rosalina's new backstory, the night of the Star Festival would not align well, so it is replaced with a celebration of Peach's arrival in the Mushroom Kingdom. Had the Arwing been a single-seat Space Fighter like in the original games, Fox's role in the movie — providing passage to Space Junk Galaxy — would have been more difficult to justify. As such, the movie version of the Arwing instead fits six. In several Mario games (e.g., Super Mario World , New Super Mario Bros. U , etc), Yoshi is usually born as a baby before growing to his adult size (usually by eating fruit). Since Yoshi has to be established early on, however, the film skips Baby Yoshi in favor of him being born already grown. Since the Comet Observatory is Demoted to Extra here, the Gateway Galaxy is made a spaceport here instead of a galaxy connected to the observatory. It also helps provide an explanation as to how locales like Fossil Falls and characters like Fox, the 8 bits, and Daisy can appear in the movie setting. It also allows Princess Daisy to appear without creating a Continuity Snarl with the first film, since Peach's backstory in that film establishes that she was the only human-ish character on the Mushroom Kingdom planet until Mario and Luigi's arrival . Redemption Rejection : Despite genuinely growing fond of Mario and Luigi, Bowser ultimately decides to go back to his villainous ways. Mario sadly acknowledges that it didn't have to be this way, to which Bowser responds by attacking him. Reformed, but Not Tamed : Downplayed. Bowser willingly helps the Bros once he's back to normal, even letting himself be imprisoned by the Queen Bee in exchange for their freedom; however, his aggressive nature remains. Once he reunites with his son, he's is hesitant about turning on the brothers. Unfortunately, Jr's influence sets him back to villainy, undoing the "reformed" part of this trope. Related in the Adaptation : Peach finds out in the middle of the film that Rosalina, another princess that she sets out to rescue, is her older sister in this continuity . Returning Big Bad : After Bowser Jr. convinces Bowser to return to villainy, he later appears on Planet Bowser back to his normal size, declaring his return to the Koopa Army with Bowser Jr. by his side. This was due to Mario giving in to his anger and hitting him on Honeyhive Galaxy, though he initially stays friendly with the Mario Bros and Yoshi until his son and his army convince him to go back to his villainous ways. The Reveal : It is revealed midway through the film that Princess Peach is the younger sister of Rosalina, who sent her to the Mushroom Kingdom to prevent an Unseen Evil from stealing their cosmic power. Save the Princess : The movie is predicated on saving Rosalina, who Bowser Jr. kidnaps to steal her cosmic powers. Save the Villain : The climax has Bowser Jr. get Swallowed Whole by his own paint-made Ruined Dragon, much to Dry Bowser's horror. Fortunately, Mario is able to power up with a Red Star, using his flight to needle-drive through the dragon's stomach and rescue Bowser Jr. from being eaten . "The Scream" Parody : Bowser paints a screaming skeletal Mario in his castle dollhouse. Screams Like a Little Girl : Mario and Luigi's reaction when they see Yoshi's glowing green eyes in the green pipe. Sequel Escalation : By virtue of being based on Super Mario Galaxy . The first movie was set on Earth, even if it was in another dimension. This one moves the action out into space and goes into more diverse locations and galaxies, and the action sequences are way more explosive than the first movie was. Sequel Hook : Fox gets some heavy focus in his final scene as he jets off to return to his adventures in space, with Toad loudly exclaiming aloud that he wants to see what comes next for him. When will Mario eventually work up the courage to finally ask Peach out on a date after she kissed him on the cheek in the end after her castle is rebuilt is left up in the air. Who or what originally tried to kidnap the princesses for their cosmic power is left open. Princess Daisy shows up at Gateway Galaxy in the second stinger, setting her up as a major player for a future sequel . Shaking the Rump : After crashing into the Honeyhive Galaxy, a tiny Bowser, realising he’s free, celebrates and shakes his rear at Mario before trying to escape. Bowser: Bye, suckers!!! She Is Not My Girlfriend : Mario protests that he is "not in love with Peach" , after Luigi asked him if he has feelings for her. Shipper on Deck : Luigi and Yoshi enjoy teasing Mario about his interest towards Princess Peach but also try encouraging him at the same time to reveal his feelings to her. Implied with Fox. While flying them to the Space Junk Galaxy, he exclaims that he likes Mario while glancing between him and Peach. The Toads react with joy when Peach kisses Mario on the cheek at the end of the film when her castle is repaired. Ship Tease : The film makes it explicitly clear that Mario and Peach have grown closer than they were in the previous film, with Mario gifting Peach her iconic parasol, and Peach having entrusted Mario to watch over her kingdom while she goes out to save Rosalina. The climax of the film has them holding hands more often, and by the end of the film, it reached the point where Peach kisses Mario on the cheek after helping rebuild her castle. Shot-for-Shot Remake : When Dry Bowser rises out of the lava, his movements and the camera angles match exactly with the scene of the first movie when he rises from the rubble of his castle in Brooklyn. Shout-Out : During the scene where Bowser Jr. introduces himself to Mario and Luigi during the first official trailer, a brief but familiar sounding Scare Chord can be heard while Bowser Jr. transforms into his Wonder form. When teasing Mario about his feelings for Peach, Luigi burst into the song "That's amore", sung by Dean Martin in The Caddy . The closeup of the Tyrannosaurus opening its eye is similar to the scene in The Land Before Time where Cera accidentally wakes up Sharptooth. Another reference occurs when the Tyrannosaurus , after chasing Yoshi and Toad to a skeletal bridge, falls into a lake below, similar to how Sharptooth met his end, although the Tyrannosaurus in this movie survived. Also during that sequence, Yoshi and co. are chased through a bridge made out of a colossal dead animal's skeleton, resembling a similar scene from Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire . For bonus points, Kong crossed it while being followed by a baby, while Yoshi has to run through it while carrying multiple babies. In Planet Bowser, one of the functions of the rollercoasters is to dip the Koopa Troopa customers in lava, turning them into Dem Bones ... er, Dry Bones. Story-Breaker Power : Rosalina is viewed by Bowser Jr. as "the most powerful princess in the universe" and she demonstrates being borderline unstoppable at the start of the film. He manages to capture her when he gets some of the Lumas involved and she is held captive until the end of film when Peach saves her. Sweet and Sour Grapes : Fox is trapped in Mario's universe due to his Arwing's warp drive malfunctioning and tearing a reality hole in the space-time continuum. When Peach hires him to help transport her and her friends, his Arwing becomes seemingly damaged beyond repair. But Fox still decides to stick with and aid the heroes, and he proves instrumental in defeating Bowser and Bowser Jr. in the film's climax. Rosalina rewards Fox's help by fixing the Arwing's warp drive, allowing him to fly back home to the Lylat System. Sympathy for the Devil : Bowser's captors moved him from the tiny and full enclosed bird cage to a bigger and better mini-castle with art supplies and plants. And Luigi compliments Bowser's painting, but with Mario, this is averted . Take That! : One invoked online promo (login required) where Bowser recaps the events of the first movie has him briefly rant about how his song "Peaches" lost the nomination for the Academy Award for Best Original Song to "I’m Just Ken" from Barbie (2023) . Bowser: I should've got an Oscar nom. I'll get you, Gosling . During his flashback, Fox McCloud blames Slippy Toad for ending up in another universe in a clear jab at the latter's status as an Annoying Video Game Helper (particularly in Star Fox 64 ). There Is Another : You thought Peach and Rosalina are the only princesses in the whole galaxy? Thwarted Escape : Rosalina attempts to escape Bowser Jr. after initially being captured, but he uses his Magic Paintbrush to cover her in paint via a Painted Piranha Plant, immobilizing her and putting her into a much stronger cage. Title: The Adaptation : The title, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie , indicates that this film will primarily adapt the two Super Mario Galaxy games. Took a Level in Badass : Both Mario and Luigi have improved their fighting capability since the events of the first film. Not only are they considered reliable by numerous characters in the film, the two can go toe-to-toe against Bowser and his son, Bowser Jr., during the final battle with greater ease compared to their first fight with Bowser. Toxic Family Influence : Goes both ways with Bowser and Bowser Jr. As expected, Bowser's evil nature led Jr. to going down the same path. But after Bowser grows genuinely fond of Mario and Luigi, actively choosing to help them and not wanting them hurt, it's Jr. that pulls Bowser back to the side of evil by repeating the villainous lessons Bowser himself taught him. Trailers Always Spoil : The final trailer has a shot of Bowser at full size greeting his army, spoiling that he will be freed at some point before the film even released. The final trailer also shows Lumalee returning as a prison warden , something that doesn't appear in the movie until The Stinger . The final trailer also shows Lumalee returning as a prison warden , something that doesn't appear in the movie until The Stinger . Trapped in Another World : How the film explains Fox's presence. He was on a mission with the rest of Star Fox when an explosion transported him to Mario's world. In The Stinger , he reveals that Rosalina fixed his warp drive, and he goes back to his home universe. Trash the Set : Princess Peach's Castle gets completely wrecked after Bowser Jr. abducts it. Aside from the aforementioned abduction already causing damages to it, the Megaleg controlled by Kamek appears inside it, wrecking it even more. Then when Bowser Jr.'s UFO accidentally drops it, it crashes down upon the Honeyhive Galaxy where it first slides down a slope before falling over a ledge, where it becomes nothing more than a pile of debris. At the end of the film, Princess Peach can only stare when, after trying to show Rosalina and her children her castle, she discovers her home is gone. Thankfully, everybody pitches in to build a new castle. Truer to the Text : In modern games recreating the original Bowser fight from Super Mario Bros. , the axe that destroys the draw bridge is changed to either a button or a massive hinged axe to sidestep the implications of Mario using a bladed weapon that the original implied. Here, when facing Bowser, Mario grabs an axe Bowser earlier threw at him and uses it to directly destroy the bridge by taking out the supports. Turned Against Their Masters : Late in the movie, Bowser Jr. gets so pissed at Mario and Luigi for "killing" his dad that he creates a giant dragon in a fit of vengeance to fight the brothers. Said dragon almost instantly turns on him and eats him instead, forcing Mario to save Bowser Jr. from his own creation . Turn the Other Cheek : Averted with Mario, who shows Bowser utter contempt, with Bowser showing the same sentiment towards the plumber in red. However, there is a two-way example with Luigi and Bowser, who interact quite cordially considering: Bowser tore off part of his mustache when they first met, later tried to dunk Luigi in a lava pit as a wedding tradition and then tried to incinerate Mario to stop him getting the Super Star. Luigi ruined any chance Bowser had of winning by getting the Super Star with Mario and leaving the Koopa King with no power, fortress, or army. Bowser tore off part of his mustache when they first met, later tried to dunk Luigi in a lava pit as a wedding tradition and then tried to incinerate Mario to stop him getting the Super Star. Luigi ruined any chance Bowser had of winning by getting the Super Star with Mario and leaving the Koopa King with no power, fortress, or army. Unflinching Walk : Rosalina casually walks away from Megaleg after blowing it up, hands clasped in the front like a regal pose. The Unintelligible : Yoshi attempts to explain how he got into the pipe in the Sand Kingdom and his misadventures in Brooklyn to Mario and Luigi, but it ends up falling on deaf ears due him being only able to say his own name . Unseen Evil : Rosalina's storybook, which Peach discovers during her time at the Comet Observatory, reveals that the two sisters were separated because an unknown evil entity tried to kidnap them for their cosmic powers. This is why Rosalina sent Peach to the Mushroom Kingdom, where she eventually became its monarch after being raised by the Toads. Unusually Uninteresting Sight : Yoshi's recollection of his time in New York shows that he was treated like any other tourist in spite of being a cartoon dinosaur, right up until he caused chaos in a museum and had to flee. Presumably, New Yorkers have gotten used to strange visitors from the Mushroom Kingdom. Also, they're New Yorkers . Villain Decay : Deconstructed . Bowser goes from an unrepentant (though comedic) villain to a friendly turtle with anger issues who's reluctant to harm his new friends. Bowser Jr. sees this as sign of " corruption ", and thus takes Bowser on a little tour of Planet Bowser to remind him of who he once was and always meant to be, leading Bowser to fully return to being the genuinely threatening and mercilessly evil tyrant that he was in the first movie. Villain Forgot to Level Grind : Justified, given that Bowser spent some time in prison and the Mario Bros. took several leaps in the power scale after the first movie. By the time of the final battle, Mario, Luigi, and Yoshi are able to counter Dry Bowser and a transformed Bowser Jr. Wardens Are Evil : Lumalee appears in the first stinger as the prison warden for Bowser and Bowser Jr., and he certainly takes delight in freaking them out with his creepy, nihilistic ranting. We Hardly Knew Ye : Mr. Game & Watch has less than a minute of screen time before he is dispatched and killed by Bowser Jr., and is never mentioned again afterwards . Wham Shot : When Peach sees Rosalina's image in Gateway Galaxy, she flashes back to her as a baby and a teenage Rosalina letting go of her hand . What Happened to the Mouse? : Kamek vanishes shortly after Mario and company arrive on Planet Bowser. He isn't even seen in prison with Bowser and Jr. in the Mid Credits Scene . Wart is never mentioned again after being seen on a screen in Bowser Junior's UFO, fulfilling his word to warn him. The Bee Queen and her prisoners don't appear again after Bowser is taken by Bowser Jr. UFO. The alien Tyrannosaurus that was turned into a baby is seen briefly at the site of the crashed Arwing and is never seen again. It's also unknown if the cast transformed it back to normal or not. Kamek vanishes shortly after Mario and company arrive on Planet Bowser. He isn't even seen in prison with Bowser and Jr. in the Mid Credits Scene . Wart is never mentioned again after being seen on a screen in Bowser Junior's UFO, fulfilling his word to warn him. The Bee Queen and her prisoners don't appear again after Bowser is taken by Bowser Jr. UFO. The alien Tyrannosaurus that was turned into a baby is seen briefly at the site of the crashed Arwing and is never seen again. It's also unknown if the cast transformed it back to normal or not. Wormsign : As to be expected whenever a Monty Mole appears. The Mole in the teaser burrows and leaves a visible trail as it attempts to outrun the butterfly — at least up until the moment the Mole accidentally crashes head first into a boulder. Wounded Gazelle Gambit : Ukiki pretends to be lost and alone so Toad will lower his guard, allowing the monkey to steal his backpack. He later tries to do the same thing with a Whittle in the second stinger , only to get punched out by Princess Daisy herself. You Can't Thwart Stage One : The ending of the second act sees Bowser at full size greeting his army to say he's returned. Turns out he reverted to normal size after Mario stomped on him after the Koopa King provoked him by calling him a failure and Bowser Jr. later returning him to the Koopa Troop via a tractor beam. You Have GOT to Be Kidding Me! : The Lumas react incredulously when Mario and Luigi reveal that Peach's Castle has been completely destroyed. Luma: What are you talking about?! title: The Bank Trump Is Relying On for Rare-Earth Minerals
meta_description: The Export-Import Bank is providing a $10 billion loan to Project Vault, an initiative to stockpile critical minerals. The project is the administration’s latest effort to reduce reliance on China.
body_text: When President Trump made his first run for the White House more than a decade ago, he derided the Export-Import Bank of the United States as a “featherbedding” corporate welfare scheme. “I don’t like it because I don’t think it’s necessary,” Mr. Trump said in 2015, echoing the concerns of many Republicans who wanted to abolish America’s export credit agency. But the obscure bank, which was established in 1934 to give loan guarantees to foreign buyers of American-made products, survived Mr. Trump’s first term. It is now being reimagined as a government catalyst for imports and sits at the center of the Trump administration’s global race for critical minerals. In early February, the White House announced a new initiative — Project Vault — to reduce America’s reliance on Chinese rare earths. The government’s plan is to work with the private sector to scour the world for minerals and then stockpile them in the United States. The initiative will be backed by a $10 billion loan from the bank, the largest in its 92-year history, along with $2 billion in private-sector funds. The effort is an extension of Mr. Trump’s aggressive style of industrial policy, which has included tariffs and government stakes in companies from key sectors. Now Project Vault has emerged as another intervention that aims to shield companies from crisis if China again opts to withhold the critical magnets that power cars and computers, as it did last year. “It’s actually overdue for there to be coordinated action from some of the largest U.S. companies that are exposed to these supply shocks coming from China to actually insulate themselves from the risk,” said Heidi Crebo-Rediker, senior fellow in the Center for Geoeconomic Studies at the Council on Foreign Relations and a former chief economist at the State Department. title: Supreme Court New Rules: तारीख पे तारीख बंद! सुप्रीम कोर्टाचे स्थगितीबाबत कडक नियम जाहीर
meta_description: Supreme Court Adjournment Rules: सर्वोच्च न्यायालयाचा मोठा निर्णय! आता विनाकारण तारखा मिळणार नाहीत; स्थगितीसाठी १८ मार्चपासून नवे कडक नियम लागू. वाचा सविस्तर.
body_text: Supreme Court New Rules 2026 : सर्वोच्च न्यायालयाने बुधवारी खटल्यांच्या सुनावणी पुढे ढकलण्याबाबत कडक पावले उचलली आहेत. आता केवळ अपवादात्मक परिस्थितीत आणि विशिष्ट नियमांच्या अधीन राहूनच सुनावणी स्थगित करण्याची विनंती विचारात घेतली जाईल, असे न्यायालयाने स्पष्ट केले आहे. याबाबत सर्वोच्च न्यायालयाने परिपत्रक जारी केले आहे. बार अॅन्ड बेंचने यासंदर्भातील वृत्त दिलं आहे. सुनावणी स्थगितीसाठीचे पत्र ‘ॲडव्होकेट-ऑन-रेकॉर्ड’ किंवा स्वतः पक्षकारांकडून दिले जाऊ शकते. हे विनंती पत्र दुसऱ्या पक्षाला किंवा कॅव्हेटरला (Caveator) आगाऊ देणे अनिवार्य आहे. सुनावणीच्या आदल्या कामकाजाच्या दिवशी सकाळी ११:०० वाजेपूर्वी अशा विनंतीची प्रत आणि पोहोच पावती सादर करणे आवश्यक आहे. विरुद्ध पक्ष अशा विनंतीवर आक्षेप घेऊ शकतो. हे आक्षेप आदल्या दिवशी दुपारी १२:०० वाजेपूर्वी ईमेलद्वारे पाठवणे आवश्यक आहे, जे न्यायालयासमोर ठेवले जातील. स्थगितीच्या अर्जात स्पष्ट कारणे आणि त्या खटल्यात आतापर्यंत किती वेळा स्थगिती घेतली आहे, याचा उल्लेख असणे आवश्यक आहे. कुटुंबातील मृत्यू, वकिलाचे किंवा पक्षकाराचे आजारपण किंवा न्यायालयाचे समाधान होईल असे इतर कोणतेही अत्यंत महत्त्वाचे कारण असल्यासच स्थगिती दिली जाईल. नवीन प्रकरणांमध्ये केवळ एकदाच स्थगितीसाठी विनंती करता येईल. तसेच, न्यायालयासमोर प्रकरण आल्याशिवाय सलग दोन वेळा स्थगिती दिली जाणार नाही. न्यायालयासमोर नियमित सुनावणीसाठी (Regular Matters) सूचीबद्ध असलेल्या प्रकरणांमध्ये स्थगितीसाठी कोणतेही पत्र स्वीकारले जाणार नाही. नॅशनल ज्युडिशियल डेटा ग्रिडच्या मार्च २०२६ च्या आकडेवारीनुसार, सर्वोच्च न्यायालयात सध्या ९२,८८२ प्रकरणे प्रलंबित आहेत. यामध्ये ७३,२०९ दिवाणी (Civil) आणि १९,६७३ फौजदारी (Criminal) प्रकरणांचा समावेश आहे. प्रकरणांचा हा मोठा डोंगर पाहता न्यायालयाने हे कडक नियम लागू केले आहेत.

title: Play Gridlock online at Coolmath Games
meta_description: Find your way out of the crowded parking lot in Gridlock! Clear a path to the exit in as few moves as possible to earn a gold star.
body_text: Sorry... this game is not playable in your browser. Log in to save all of your favorite Coolmath games. Drag cars out of the way to clear a path for the red car to drive to the exit. Cars can move forwards and reverse but can't turn or move to their sides. These traffic jams can get pretty tricky. Sometimes you'll have to move a car one way to dislodge another car, and then move it back again. If you get stuck, tap the left and right buttons to undo or redo your last move. You can also tap the hint button to see the next correct move, but you won't be eligible to get any star ranking on this level once you use a hint. When you finish the level, you can get a Bronze, Silver or Gold Star. Bronze Star: Solve the stage without using the hint or back button. Silver Star: Solve the stage in less than a set number of moves. Gold Star: Solve the stage in the minimum number of moves possible. body_text: Pukei-Pukei Shard is a Master Rank Material in Monster Hunter Rise (MHR) . Pukei-Pukei Shard is a brand new Material debuting in the Sunbreak Expansion . Materials such as Pukei-Pukei Shard are special Items that are obtained from looting the environment, completing Quests and objectives, and by carving specific Monsters. Materials are usually harvested off a Monster after completing a hunt and these are primarily used for Crafting and upgrading a hunter's equipment. title: Michigan's 7th Congressional District
body_text: Michigan's 7th Congressional District in the United States House of Representatives is represented by Tom Barrett (R). As of the 2020 Census, Michigan representatives represented an average of 775,726 residents. After the 2010 Census, each member represented 707,973 residents. 1 Elections 1.1 2016 1.2 2014 1.3 2012 1.3.1 Primary results 3 Redistricting 3.1 2020-2021 3.2 2020 3.3 2024 3.4 2010-2011 4 District analysis 4.1 2026 4.2 2024 4.3 2022 4.4 2018 See also: Michigan's 7th Congressional District election, 2030 There are no official candidates yet for this election. Rachel Dailey (L) advanced from the Libertarian Party convention for U.S. House Michigan District 7 on July 10, 2022. See also: Michigan's 7th Congressional District election, 2020 Incumbent Tim Walberg (R) defeated Gretchen Driskell (D) in the general election for U.S. House Michigan District 7 on November 3, 2020. Gretchen Driskell (D) advanced from the Democratic primary for U.S. House Michigan District 7 on August 4, 2020. Incumbent Tim Walberg (R) advanced from the Republican primary for U.S. House Michigan District 7 on August 4, 2020. See also: United States House of Representatives elections in Michigan, 2018 Incumbent Tim Walberg (R) defeated Gretchen Driskell (D) in the general election for U.S. House Michigan District 7 on November 6, 2018. Gretchen Driskell (D) defeated Steven Friday (D) in the Democratic primary for U.S. House Michigan District 7 on August 7, 2018. Incumbent Tim Walberg (R) advanced from the Republican primary for U.S. House Michigan District 7 on August 7, 2018. Heading into the election, Ballotpedia rated this race as a race to watch . Incumbent Tim Walberg (R) defeated Gretchen Driskell (D) and Ken Proctor (L) in the general election on November 8, 2016. Walberg defeated Doug North in the Republican primary on August 2, 2016. [1] [2] [3] [4] The 7th Congressional District of Michigan held an election for the U.S. House of Representatives on November 4, 2014. Incumbent Tim Walberg (R) defeated challengers Pam Byrnes (D), Ken Proctor (L), Rick Strawcutter (UST) and David Swartout (I) in the general election. The 7th Congressional District of Michigan held an election for the U.S. House of Representatives on November 6, 2012. Incumbent Tim Walberg won re-election in the district. [5] 2010 On November 2, 2010, Tim Walberg won election to the United States House . He defeated incumbent Mark Schauer (D), Scott Eugene Aughney (U.S. Taxpayers), Greg Merle (Libertarian), Richard Wunsch (Green) and Danny Davis (Write-in) in the general election. [6] 2008 On November 4, 2008, Mark Schauer won election to the United States House . He defeated incumbent Tim Walberg (R), Lynn Meadows (Green), Ken Proctor (Libertarian) and Sharon Renier (Write-in) in the general election. [7] 2006 On November 7, 2006, Tim Walberg won election to the United States House . He defeated Sharon Marie Renier (D), Robert Hutchinson (Libertarian), David Horn (U.S. Taxpayers) and Joe Schwarz (Write-in) in the general election. [8] 2004 On November 2, 2004, Joe Schwarz won election to the United States House . He defeated Sharon Marie Renier (D), David Horn (U.S. Taxpayers), Jason Seagraves (G) and Kenneth Proctor (L) in the general election. [9] 2002 On November 5, 2002, Nick Smith won re-election to the United States House . He defeated Mike Simpson (D) and Ken Proctor (L) in the general election. [10] 2000 On November 7, 2000, Nick Smith won re-election to the United States House . He defeated Jennie Crittendon (D), Perry Spencer (Reform), Robert Broda, Jr. (L), Steve Cousino (U.S. Taxpayers) and Gail Anne Petrosoff (Natural Law) in the general election. [11] 1998 On November 3, 1998, Nick Smith won re-election to the United States House . He defeated Jim Berryman (D), Kenneth Proctor (L) and Lynnea Ellison (Natural Law) in the general election. [12] 1996 On November 5, 1996, Nick Smith won re-election to the United States House . He defeated Kim Tunnicliff (D), Robert Broda, Jr. (L) and Scott Williamson (Natural Law) in the general election. [13] 1994 On November 8, 1994, Nick Smith won re-election to the United States House . He defeated Kim McCaughtry (D), Kenneth Proctor (L) and Scott Williamson (Natural Law) in the general election. [14] 1992 On November 3, 1992, Nick Smith won election to the United States House . He defeated Kenneth Proctor (L) in the general election. [15] 1990 On November 6, 1990, Dale Kildee won re-election to the United States House . He defeated David Morrill (R) in the general election. [16] Michigan’s congressional district boundaries became law on March 26, 2022, 60 days after the Michigan Independent Citizens Redistricting Commission (MICRC) published its report on the redistricting plans with the secretary of state. [17] [18] On December 28, 2021, the Michigan Independent Citizens Redistricting Commission (MICRC) approved what was named the "Chestnut" map by a vote of 8-5. Two Democrats, two Republicans, and four nonpartisan members voted to approve the plan with the five remaining commissioners in favor of other plans. As required, "at least two commissioners who affiliate with each major party, and at least two commissioners who do not affiliate with either major party" voted in favor of the adopted map. [19] The MICRC was established after voters approved a 2018 constitutional amendment that transferred the power to draw the state's congressional and legislative districts from the state legislature to a redistricting commission. Under the terms of the amendment, "Within 30 days after adopting a plan, the commission shall publish the plan and the material reports, reference materials, and data used in drawing it, including any programming information used to produce and test the plan." The adopted plan becomes law 60 days after the MICRC publishes that report. [19] Beth LeBlanc of The Detroit News wrote that, “Unlike other congressional maps the commission had to choose from, Chestnut was set apart by its inclusion of Grand Rapids and Muskegon in the same district, its grouping of Battle Creek and Kalamazoo and its ability to keep Jackson County whole, instead of breaking off part of the county into an Ann Arbor area district.” [20] According to Clara Hendrickson and Todd Spangler of the Detroit Free Press , "According to three measures of partisan fairness based on statewide election data from the past decade, the map favors Republicans. But those measures also show a significant reduction in the Republican bias compared to the map drawn a decade ago by a Republican legislature, deemed one of the most politically biased maps in the country. One of the partisan fairness measures used by the commission indicates Democratic candidates would have an advantage under the new map." [21] This map took effect for Michigan’s 2022 congressional elections. How does redistricting in Michigan work? In Michigan, a non-politician commission is responsible for drawing both congressional and state legislative district plans. The commission comprises 13 members, including four Democrats, four Republicans, and five unaffiliated voters or members of minor parties. In order for a map to be enacted, at least seven members must vote for it, including at least two Democrats, two Republicans, and two members not affiliated with either major party. [22] The Michigan Independent Citizens Redistricting Commission prepared this document specifically explaining the redistricting process after the 2020 census. In 2011, the Michigan State Legislature re-drew the congressional districts based on updated population information from the 2010 census. In redistricting, The Hill published a list of the Top Ten House Members who were helped by redistricting . [23] Tim Walberg ranked 6th on the list. [23] The article notes that Tim Walberg lost the district to centrist Mark Schauer ( D ) in 2008. He then beat Schauer in a rematch by 5 points in 2010. The redistricting process , controlled by Republicans , made sure to cut Schauer’s home base out of the district and made it a few points more Republican . [23] According to the article, Schauer said that he would not run for a rematch because of the new map. [23] Heading into the 2026 elections, based on results from the 2024 and 2020 presidential elections, the Cook Partisan Voter Index for this district is EVEN. This meant that in those two presidential elections, this district's results were about the same as the national average. This made Michigan's 7th the 220th most Republican district nationally. [24] Heading into the 2024 elections, based on results from the 2020 and 2016 presidential elections, the Cook Partisan Voter Index for this district was R+2. This meant that in those two presidential elections, this district's results were 2 percentage points more Republican than the national average. This made Michigan's 7th the 211th most Republican district nationally. [25] Daily Kos calculated what the results of the 2020 presidential election in this district would have been following redistricting . Joe Biden (D) would have defeated Donald Trump (R) 49.4%-48.9%. [26] Heading into the 2022 elections, based on results from the 2020 and 2016 presidential elections, the Cook Partisan Voter Index for this district was R+2. This meant that in those two presidential elections, this district's results were 2 percentage points more Republican than the national average. This made Michigan's 7th the 210th most Republican district nationally. [27] Daily Kos calculated what the results of the 2020 presidential election in this district would have been following redistricting . Joe Biden (D) would have received 49.4% of the vote in this district and Donald Trump (R) would have received 48.9%. [28] Heading into the 2018 elections, based on results from the 2016 and 2012 presidential elections, the Cook Partisan Voter Index for this district was R+7. This meant that in those two presidential elections, this district's results were 7 percentage points more Republican than the national average. This made Michigan's 7th Congressional District the 176th most Republican nationally. [29] FiveThirtyEight's September 2018 elasticity score for states and congressional districts measured "how sensitive it is to changes in the national political environment." This district's elasticity score was 1.12. This means that for every 1 point the national political mood moved toward a party, the district was expected to move 1.12 points toward that party. title: Exyerno de Pedro Fernández, está feliz por ver lo mucho que ha crecido su hijo
meta_description: Christopher Dubois se casó con Osmara, hija de Pedro Fernández, en 2014, pero 3 meses después, estando ella embarazada, se separaron.
body_text: Siete años después del nacimiento del único nieto de Pedro Fernández, de 52, hijo de Osmara Cuevas , de 32, y Christopher Dubois , de 33, el cantante presentó al niño a través de un video donde cantan juntos “Feliz Navidad, mi amor”. Hasta el momento, el padre del niño sigue sin conocerlo, pero esto nos dijo al verlo, aunque sea en video: No te pierdas: Tony Balardi se enteró que saqueaban su casa en vivo -Christopher, al fin pudiste ver y escuchar a tu hijo en un video musical, ¿qué sentiste? “Siempre estoy checando sus redes, por si suben alguna foto o algo... Mi hijo acaba de cumplir 7 años el 1 de diciembre”. -¿Ya habías visto algo de él? “Solo la foto que ustedes me mostraron de bebé y luego unas fotos en la playa en las que apenas se le alcanza a ver su carita, pero después nada, ni su rostro ni su voz, hasta ahora con lo del video”. -¿Nunca volviste a tener contacto con Osmara? “No, nunca”. -¿Qué sentiste al verlo? “Emoción, estoy orgulloso de ver en lo que se está convirtiendo, está guapo (ríe)”. -¿Se parece a ti? “Es mi hijo, qué puedo decir, está guapo (ríe). Le deseo mucho éxito con lo que quiera hacer, si quiere ser cantante, tiene mi apoyo incondicional. Desde donde estoy le echo todas las porras. Quiero que sepa que aquí está su papá, que no lo olvido y muero por conocerlo en persona”. Christopher Dubois vio crecer a su hijo a través de un video Revisa: Lorena de la Garza presume su nueva relación pero su novio la está usando -¿Cuántas veces has visto el video? “No las he contado, pero muchas, yo creo más de 20 veces, no me canso de verlo, cada que lo hago encuentro algo nuevo. La verdad, yo sí creo que tiene mucho de mí”. -¿Cuáles son tus expectativas con él? “Mi esperanza es conocerlo, con eso tengo. Estoy agradecido por haberlo podido conocer con ese video y también por haberlo escuchado cantar”. -¿Buscarías una reconciliación con ellos? “Si ellos están en la disposición de una reconciliación, yo también lo estoy; si es lo mejor para mi hijo, haría lo que fuera. Creo que lo más sano es una reconciliación”. -¿Qué pasó con los asuntos legales sobre la custodia y la convivencia? “De eso ya no voy a hablar, no voy a tocar el tema por el bien de mi hijo. No quiero perjudicarlo, solo quiero que sepa que siempre pienso en él y que lo quiero mucho”, finalizó. Christopher Dubois quería formar una familia Checa: El Potrillo recuerda a don Vicente, a un mes de su partida Yerno, familia Christopher Dubois Hijos, madre Pedro Fernández
