title: How do I charge my Magic Mouse: A Simple Guide to Efficiently Powering Up Your Apple Accessory
meta_description: Welcome to our simple guide on efficiently powering up your Apple accessory, the Magic Mouse. The Magic Mouse is a sleek and innovative wireless mouse
headings: Understanding The Magic Mouse Battery: Types And Specifications
Step-by-step Guide To Charging Your Magic Mouse
Choosing The Right Charging Method For Your Magic Mouse
Best Practices For Extending The Battery Life Of Your Magic Mouse
Troubleshooting Common Magic Mouse Charging Issues
Exploring Alternative Charging Options For The Magic Mouse
The Future Of Magic Mouse Charging: New Technologies And Advancements
body_text: Welcome to our simple guide on efficiently powering up your Apple accessory, the Magic Mouse. The Magic Mouse is a sleek and innovative wireless mouse designed specifically for Apple users, offering smooth navigation and customizable features. In this article, we will walk you through the various methods and best practices to charge your Magic Mouse, ensuring you never run out of power when you need it most. The Magic Mouse, designed and manufactured by Apple Inc., is a wireless mouse that requires periodic charging to function efficiently. To ensure a seamless charging experience, it is important to understand the battery types and specifications of the Magic Mouse. The first-generation Magic Mouse is equipped with a built-in rechargeable lithium-ion battery, while the second-generation Magic Mouse uses a removable AA battery. Knowing which model you have is crucial, as different charging methods apply to each. For the first-generation Magic Mouse, the battery is non-removable and can be recharged using a Lightning to USB cable. This ensures convenient charging via any USB port, whether on the computer or an adapter. On the other hand, the second-generation Magic Mouse allows for easy battery replacement by using a regular AA battery, eliminating the need for charging cables entirely. Understanding the battery specifications, such as capacity and voltage, is essential for efficient charging. The first-generation Magic Mouse battery has a capacity of 550mAh, while the second-generation battery capacity can vary depending on the chosen AA battery. By grasping the battery types and specifications of the Magic Mouse, you can make informed decisions about the charging methods and ensure a well-maintained and long-lasting mouse. Step-by-step Guide To Charging Your Magic Mouse Charging your Magic Mouse is a simple and straightforward process. Follow these step-by-step instructions to efficiently power up your Apple accessory: 1. Preparation: Before you begin, ensure that you have a compatible charging cable and power source. The Magic Mouse uses a Lightning port for charging, so make sure you have a Lightning cable ready. 2. Connect the cable: Connect one end of the Lightning cable to the Magic Mouse’s charging port, which is located on the underside of the device. Ensure a secure connection to avoid any interruptions in charging. 3. Connect to a power source: Plug the other end of the Lightning cable into a USB port on your computer or a USB wall adapter. Use a reputable power source to avoid any potential damage to your Magic Mouse. 4. Monitor the charging status: Once connected, you should see a charging icon on your device’s screen. Additionally, the Magic Mouse’s LED indicator light will turn on, showing that it is being charged. 5. Charging time: The Magic Mouse typically takes around two hours to fully charge. However, the exact duration may vary based on the battery level and your charging method. 6. Disconnect and enjoy: Once the Magic Mouse is fully charged, disconnect the Lightning cable from both the device and the power source. Your Magic Mouse is now ready to use wirelessly. By following these straightforward steps, you can keep your Magic Mouse powered up and ready for all your Apple accessory needs. Choosing The Right Charging Method For Your Magic Mouse When it comes to charging your Magic Mouse, it’s essential to choose the right method that suits your needs. Apple offers two primary charging options for the Magic Mouse: wired and wireless. 1. Wired charging: The most straightforward and commonly used method is to charge your Magic Mouse using a Lightning cable. Simply connect one end of the cable to the Lightning port on your Magic Mouse and the other end to a USB power source, such as your computer or a wall adapter. This method ensures a reliable and stable connection, allowing for a faster charging time. It is also recommended for situations where you need to charge the mouse quickly before using it again. 2. Wireless charging: If you prefer a more convenient and cable-free experience, you can opt for wireless charging. Apple offers a charging pad called the Magic Mouse Charging Station, which allows you to charge your Magic Mouse by simply placing it on top of the pad. This charging method is ideal for users who want to maintain a clutter-free workspace or charge their mouse conveniently throughout the day without the hassle of connecting and disconnecting cables. It’s important to note that not all Magic Mouse models support wireless charging. Therefore, before choosing a charging method, ensure compatibility with your specific Magic Mouse model. Best Practices For Extending The Battery Life Of Your Magic Mouse When it comes to getting the most out of your Magic Mouse battery, following some best practices can significantly extend its lifespan. Firstly, adjusting the tracking and scrolling speed in the mouse settings can help conserve battery power. Lowering these settings reduces the workload on the mouse’s sensors and thus reduces power consumption. Another effective way to save energy is to enable the mouse’s automatic sleep mode. This feature ensures that the mouse goes into a low-power state after a period of inactivity. You can adjust the sleep timeout in the mouse settings to best suit your needs. Furthermore, it is recommended to turn off the Magic Mouse when not in use for an extended period. This is especially useful when traveling or when you know you won’t be using the mouse for a while. Turning off the mouse ensures that no unnecessary power is drained from the battery. Regularly cleaning the Magic Mouse’s sensors and tracking surface is also crucial in optimizing its battery life. Dust and debris can interfere with the mouse’s tracking, causing it to work harder and use more power. By keeping it clean, you can ensure smooth and efficient performance while conserving energy. Lastly, keeping the Magic Mouse away from extreme temperatures can help preserve the battery’s lifespan. High temperatures can degrade the battery, while very cold temperatures can temporarily reduce its capacity. It is advisable to store and charge the Magic Mouse in a moderate temperature environment. By following these best practices, you can maximize the battery life of your Magic Mouse, ensuring it stays powered up and ready for use when you need it most. Troubleshooting Common Magic Mouse Charging Issues Troubleshooting common Magic Mouse charging issues is essential in ensuring that your Apple accessory remains powered up efficiently. In this section, we will address some of the most frequently encountered charging problems and help you resolve them. One common issue is when the Magic Mouse doesn’t charge even when connected to a power source. This could be due to a faulty charging cable, a defective power adapter, or an issue with the mouse itself. We will guide you through the steps to check and eliminate each potential cause. Another issue is when the Magic Mouse drains its battery quickly, even after fully charging it. This could be caused by power-hungry applications running in the background or improper use of the mouse. We will provide tips on how to identify and address these issues, helping you maximize your Magic Mouse’s battery life. Additionally, we will discuss common connectivity problems that may prevent the Magic Mouse from charging properly, such as intermittent charging or the device not being recognized by your Mac. We will offer troubleshooting steps to establish a stable connection and ensure successful charging. By following the troubleshooting steps outlined in this section, you can efficiently diagnose and address common Magic Mouse charging issues, enabling you to keep your Apple accessory powered up without any hassle. Exploring Alternative Charging Options For The Magic Mouse If you’re tired of constantly plugging in your Magic Mouse or if you find yourself on the go without access to a power outlet, exploring alternative charging options can be a game-changer. Fortunately, there are a few options available to efficiently power up your Magic Mouse. One of the most popular alternatives is using a wireless charging pad. These pads utilize electromagnetic fields to transfer energy from the pad to your Magic Mouse. Simply place your Magic Mouse on the pad, and it will start charging without the need for cables. It’s a convenient and clutter-free option. Another alternative is using a power bank. These portable chargers hold a significant amount of power and can be a lifesaver when you’re out and about. Just connect your Magic Mouse to the power bank with a Lightning cable, and it will start charging. Keep in mind that the power bank’s capacity will determine how many times it can charge your Magic Mouse. Lastly, if you have an Apple Watch, you can utilize its charging cable as an alternative. The charging cable for the Apple Watch has a USB-A connector that you can plug into your computer or a USB power adapter. Simply connect the magnetic end of the cable to the bottom of your Magic Mouse, and it will charge. By exploring these alternative options, you can ensure that your Magic Mouse remains powered up no matter where you are or what charging resources are available to you. The Future Of Magic Mouse Charging: New Technologies And Advancements As technology continues to evolve, so does the world of charging options for the Magic Mouse. Apple is constantly exploring new technologies and advancements to enhance the charging experience for their users. One promising development on the horizon is wireless charging. With this technology, users will no longer need to physically connect their Magic Mouse to a charging cable. Instead, the device will automatically charge when placed on a compatible charging pad. This convenience eliminates the need for cables and ensures a seamless charging experience. Another potential advancement is the integration of fast charging capabilities. Currently, it takes around two hours to fully charge a Magic Mouse. However, Apple is working on reducing this time significantly. Imagine being able to charge your Magic Mouse to full capacity in just a matter of minutes, providing you with extended usage time. Additionally, advancements in battery technology may lead to longer-lasting batteries for the Magic Mouse. With improved battery life, users can enjoy their Apple accessory for extended periods without worrying about frequent charging. In conclusion, the future of Magic Mouse charging looks promising, with developments in wireless charging, fast charging, and improved battery technology. Apple strives to continually enhance the charging experience for its users, making it more efficient and convenient. FAQs FAQ 1: Why is my Magic Mouse not charging? There could be several reasons why your Magic Mouse is not charging. First, ensure that the charging cable is securely connected to both the mouse and a power source. If it still doesn’t charge, try using a different charging cable, as the cable might be faulty. Additionally, check if the charging port on the Magic Mouse is clean and free from any debris or dust, as that can prevent proper charging. FAQ 2: How long does it take to fully charge a Magic Mouse? The charging time for a Magic Mouse can vary depending on its battery level and the charging method used. Generally, it takes around two hours to fully charge a Magic Mouse using a wired connection. However, when using a wireless charging method, such as a charging pad, it may take slightly longer. It is recommended to keep an eye on the charging indicator light, which turns green when the mouse is fully charged. FAQ 3: Can I charge my Magic Mouse wirelessly? Yes, the Magic Mouse can be charged wirelessly. If you own a compatible Apple device, such as an iPhone or MacBook, you can use a wireless charging pad that supports the Qi standard. Simply place your Magic Mouse on the charging pad, ensuring it aligns with the charging coils, and it will start charging automatically. This eliminates the need for a physical charging cable and provides a convenient and clutter-free charging experience. FAQ 4: Is it possible to use my Magic Mouse while it is charging? Yes, you can use your Magic Mouse while it is charging. If you are using a wired charging method, simply connect the charging cable to the mouse and continue using it. However, keep in mind that the movement might be restricted due to the cable’s length. If you are using a wireless charging pad, you can still use the Magic Mouse by lifting it slightly off the pad while moving the cursor. It’s important to ensure that the charging connections remain undisturbed to maintain a steady charging process. The Bottom Line In conclusion, charging your Magic Mouse is a straightforward process that can be done efficiently with the right steps. Whether you choose to use a Lightning cable or a wireless charging mat, it is important to ensure that you have a reliable power source and follow the recommended charging guidelines provided by Apple. By taking these simple steps, you can power up your Magic Mouse efficiently and enjoy uninterrupted use of your Apple accessory. Firstly, adjusting the tracking and scrolling speed in the mouse settings can help conserve battery power. Lowering these settings reduces the workload on the mouse’s sensors and thus reduces power consumption. Another effective way to save energy is to enable the mouse’s automatic sleep mode. This feature ensures that the mouse goes into a low-power state after a period of inactivity. You can adjust the sleep timeout in the mouse settings to best suit your needs. Furthermore, it is recommended to turn off the Magic Mouse when not in use for an extended period. This is especially useful when traveling or when you know you won’t be using the mouse for a while. Turning off the mouse ensures that no unnecessary power is drained from the battery. Regularly cleaning the Magic Mouse’s sensors and tracking surface is also crucial in optimizing its battery life. Dust and debris can interfere with the mouse’s tracking, causing it to work harder and use more power. By keeping it clean, you can ensure smooth and efficient performance while conserving energy. Lastly, keeping the Magic Mouse away from extreme temperatures can help preserve the battery’s lifespan. High temperatures can degrade the battery, while very cold temperatures can temporarily reduce its capacity. It is advisable to store and charge the Magic Mouse in a moderate temperature environment. By following these best practices, you can maximize the battery life of your Magic Mouse, ensuring it stays powered up and ready for use when you need it most. If it still doesn’t charge, try using a different charging cable, as the cable might be faulty. Additionally, check if the charging port on the Magic Mouse is clean and free from any debris or dust, as that can prevent proper charging. FAQ 2: How long does it take to fully charge a Magic Mouse? The charging time for a Magic Mouse can vary depending on its battery level and the charging method used. Generally, it takes around two hours to fully charge a Magic Mouse using a wired connection. However, when using a wireless charging method, such as a charging pad, it may take slightly longer. It is recommended to keep an eye on the charging indicator light, which turns green when the mouse is fully charged. FAQ 3: Can I charge my Magic Mouse wirelessly? Yes, the Magic Mouse can be charged wirelessly. If you own a compatible Apple device, such as an iPhone or MacBook, you can use a wireless charging pad that supports the Qi standard. Simply place your Magic Mouse on the charging pad, ensuring it aligns with the charging coils, and it will start charging automatically. This eliminates the need for a physical charging cable and provides a convenient and clutter-free charging experience. FAQ 4: Is it possible to use my Magic Mouse while it is charging? Yes, you can use your Magic Mouse while it is charging. If you are using a wired charging method, simply connect the charging cable to the mouse and continue using it. However, keep in mind that the movement might be restricted due to the cable’s length. If you are using a wireless charging pad, you can still use the Magic Mouse by lifting it slightly off the pad while moving the cursor. It’s important to ensure that the charging connections remain undisturbed to maintain a steady charging process. The Bottom Line In conclusion, charging your Magic Mouse is a straightforward process that can be done efficiently with the right steps. Whether you choose to use a Lightning cable or a wireless charging mat, it is important to ensure that you have a reliable power source and follow the recommended charging guidelines provided by Apple. By taking these simple steps, you can power up your Magic Mouse efficiently and enjoy uninterrupted use of your Apple accessory. 5. Charging time: The Magic Mouse typically takes around two hours to fully charge. However, the exact duration may vary based on the battery level and your charging method. 6. Choosing The Right Charging Method For Your Magic Mouse When it comes to charging your Magic Mouse, it’s essential to choose the right method that suits your needs. Apple offers two primary charging options for the Magic Mouse: wired and wireless. 1. Wired charging: The most straightforward and commonly used method is to charge your Magic Mouse using a Lightning cable. Simply connect one end of the cable to the Lightning port on your Magic Mouse and the other end to a USB power source, such as your computer or a wall adapter. This method ensures a reliable and stable connection, allowing for a faster charging time. It is also recommended for situations where you need to charge the mouse quickly before using it again. 2. Wireless charging: If you prefer a more convenient and cable-free experience, you can opt for wireless charging. Apple offers a charging pad called the Magic Mouse Charging Station, which allows you to charge your Magic Mouse by simply placing it on top of the pad. This charging method is ideal for users who want to maintain a clutter-free workspace or charge their mouse conveniently throughout the day without the hassle of connecting and disconnecting cables. It’s important to note that not all Magic Mouse models support wireless charging. Therefore, before choosing a charging method, ensure compatibility with your specific Magic Mouse model. Best Practices For Extending The Battery Life Of Your Magic Mouse When it comes to getting the most out of your Magic Mouse battery, following some best practices can significantly extend its lifespan. Firstly, adjusting the tracking and scrolling speed in the mouse settings can help conserve battery power. Lowering these settings reduces the workload on the mouse’s sensors and thus reduces power consumption. Another effective way to save energy is to enable the mouse’s automatic sleep mode. This feature ensures that the mouse goes into a low-power state after a period of inactivity. You can adjust the sleep timeout in the mouse settings to best suit your needs. Furthermore, it is recommended to turn off the Magic Mouse when not in use for an extended period. This is especially useful when traveling or when you know you won’t be using the mouse for a while. Turning off the mouse ensures that no unnecessary power is drained from the battery. Regularly cleaning the Magic Mouse’s sensors and tracking surface is also crucial in optimizing its battery life. Dust and debris can interfere with the mouse’s tracking, causing it to work harder and use more power. By keeping it clean, you can ensure smooth and efficient performance while conserving energy. Lastly, keeping the Magic Mouse away from extreme temperatures can help preserve the battery’s lifespan. High temperatures can degrade the battery, while very cold temperatures can temporarily reduce its capacity. It is advisable to store and charge the Magic Mouse in a moderate temperature environment. By following these best practices, you can maximize the battery life of your Magic Mouse, ensuring it stays powered up and ready for use when you need it most. Troubleshooting Common Magic Mouse Charging Issues Troubleshooting common Magic Mouse charging issues is essential in ensuring that your Apple accessory remains powered up efficiently. In this section, we will address some of the most frequently encountered charging problems and help you resolve them. One common issue is when the Magic Mouse doesn’t charge even when connected to a power source. This could be due to a faulty charging cable, a defective power adapter, or an issue with the mouse itself. We will guide you through the steps to check and eliminate each potential cause. Another issue is when the Magic Mouse drains its battery quickly, even after fully charging it. This could be caused by power-hungry applications running in the background or improper use of the mouse. We will provide tips on how to identify and address these issues, helping you maximize your Magic Mouse’s battery life. Additionally, we will discuss common connectivity problems that may prevent the Magic Mouse from charging properly, such as intermittent charging or the device not being recognized by your Mac. We will offer troubleshooting steps to establish a stable connection and ensure successful charging. By following the troubleshooting steps outlined in this section, you can efficiently diagnose and address common Magic Mouse charging issues, enabling you to keep your Apple accessory powered up without any hassle. Exploring Alternative Charging Options For The Magic Mouse If you’re tired of constantly plugging in your Magic Mouse or if you find yourself on the go without access to a power outlet, exploring alternative charging options can be a game-changer. Fortunately, there are a few options available to efficiently power up your Magic Mouse. One of the most popular alternatives is using a wireless charging pad. These pads utilize electromagnetic fields to transfer energy from the pad to your Magic Mouse. Simply place your Magic Mouse on the pad, and it will start charging without the need for cables. It’s a convenient and clutter-free option. Another alternative is using a power bank. These portable chargers hold a significant amount of power and can be a lifesaver when you’re out and about. Just connect your Magic Mouse to the power bank with a Lightning cable, and it will start charging. Keep in mind that the power bank’s capacity will determine how many times it can charge your Magic Mouse. Lastly, if you have an Apple Watch, you can utilize its charging cable as an alternative. The charging cable for the Apple Watch has a USB-A connector that you can plug into your computer or a USB power adapter. Simply connect the magnetic end of the cable to the bottom of your Magic Mouse, and it will charge. By exploring these alternative options, you can ensure that your Magic Mouse remains powered up no matter where you are or what charging resources are available to you. There could be several reasons why your Magic Mouse is not charging. First, ensure that the charging cable is securely connected to both the mouse and a power source. If it still doesn’t charge, try using a different charging cable, as the cable might be faulty. Additionally, check if the charging port on the Magic Mouse is clean and free from any debris or dust, as that can prevent proper charging. FAQ 2: How long does it take to fully charge a Magic Mouse? The charging time for a Magic Mouse can vary depending on its battery level and the charging method used. Generally, it takes around two hours to fully charge a Magic Mouse using a wired connection. However, when using a wireless charging method, such as a charging pad, it may take slightly longer. It is recommended to keep an eye on the charging indicator light, which turns green when the mouse is fully charged. FAQ 3: Can I charge my Magic Mouse wirelessly? Yes, the Magic Mouse can be charged wirelessly. If you own a compatible Apple device, such as an iPhone or MacBook, you can use a wireless charging pad that supports the Qi standard. Simply place your Magic Mouse on the charging pad, ensuring it aligns with the charging coils, and it will start charging automatically. This eliminates the need for a physical charging cable and provides a convenient and clutter-free charging experience. FAQ 4: Is it possible to use my Magic Mouse while it is charging? Yes, you can use your Magic Mouse while it is charging. If you are using a wired charging method, simply connect the charging cable to the mouse and continue using it. However, keep in mind that the movement might be restricted due to the cable’s length. If you are using a wireless charging pad, you can still use the Magic Mouse by lifting it slightly off the pad while moving the cursor. It’s important to ensure that the charging connections remain undisturbed to maintain a steady charging process. The Bottom Line In conclusion, charging your Magic Mouse is a straightforward process that can be done efficiently with the right steps. Whether you choose to use a Lightning cable or a wireless charging mat, it is important to ensure that you have a reliable power source and follow the recommended charging guidelines provided by Apple. By taking these simple steps, you can power up your Magic Mouse efficiently and enjoy uninterrupted use of your Apple accessory. It’s important to note that not all Magic Mouse models support wireless charging. Therefore, before choosing a charging method, ensure compatibility with your specific Magic Mouse model. Best Practices For Extending The Battery Life Of Your Magic Mouse When it comes to getting the most out of your Magic Mouse battery, following some best practices can significantly extend its lifespan. Firstly, adjusting the tracking and scrolling speed in the mouse settings can help conserve battery power. Lowering these settings reduces the workload on the mouse’s sensors and thus reduces power consumption. Another effective way to save energy is to enable the mouse’s automatic sleep mode. This feature ensures that the mouse goes into a low-power state after a period of inactivity. You can adjust the sleep timeout in the mouse settings to best suit your needs. Furthermore, it is recommended to turn off the Magic Mouse when not in use for an extended period. This is especially useful when traveling or when you know you won’t be using the mouse for a while. Turning off the mouse ensures that no unnecessary power is drained from the battery. Regularly cleaning the Magic Mouse’s sensors and tracking surface is also crucial in optimizing its battery life. Dust and debris can interfere with the mouse’s tracking, causing it to work harder and use more power. By keeping it clean, you can ensure smooth and efficient performance while conserving energy. Lastly, keeping the Magic Mouse away from extreme temperatures can help preserve the battery’s lifespan. High temperatures can degrade the battery, while very cold temperatures can temporarily reduce its capacity. It is advisable to store and charge the Magic Mouse in a moderate temperature environment. By following these best practices, you can maximize the battery life of your Magic Mouse, ensuring it stays powered up and ready for use when you need it most. Simply place your Magic Mouse on the charging pad, ensuring it aligns with the charging coils, and it will start charging automatically. Yes, you can use your Magic Mouse while it is charging. If you are using a wired charging method, simply connect the charging cable to the mouse and continue using it. However, keep in mind that the movement might be restricted due to the cable’s length. It’s important to ensure that the charging connections remain undisturbed to maintain a steady charging process. title: Things To Do in Westchester in February With Kids - Mommy Poppins
meta_description: We're in love with these family-friendly February events—some are even FREE!
headings: My Top 5 Events this Month, At-a-Glance
body_text: Whether sending valentines or planning a Presidents Day weekend getaway, there is plenty to love about February in Westchester and the Hudson Valley! Families can ring in the Year of the Horse with delicious food and dazzling performances at the Lunar New Year Celebration put on by the Westchester Association of Chinese Americans at SUNY Purchase. Or, check out the all-new Wizard of Oz on Ice at Paramount Hudson Valley. Kids are also invited to celebrate Valentine's Day in a unique way during a Party with Wolves at the Wolf Conservation Center in North Salem. And, don't miss Teatown's annual Hudson River EagleFest in scenic Croton Point Park, which features birds of prey shows, heated tents with crafts, food trucks, and more family fun. Can’t-Miss Lunar New Year Event : WACA Lunar New Year Celebration My Favorite Free Event : Traditions of Chinese Acrobatics at the White Plains Library Best Event for Babies : Valentine's Jam at Peek-a-Boo Family Music Month’s Best Bet for Teens : Lake George Winter Carnival Coolest Hidden Gem Activity : Valentine's Day Party with Wolves I have more picks for every age, interest, and neighborhood below… Want even more fun things to do this weekend? Our Westchester and Hudson Valley Events Calendar is packed with great family activities for every day of the week—and you can filter by age, location, or even free events! Not receiving our newsletter list yet? Subscribe for free and get the best local family fun sent straight to your inbox. Already subscribed? Share it with a friend and start planning your weekend together!

title: 10 Human Foods that are Safe for Cats to Eat
meta_description: There's nothing worse than that look when your pet is sitting on the floor while you're eating and they give you that awful face
body_text: There’s nothing worse than that look when your pet is sitting on the floor while you’re eating and they give you that awful face. You know the face that says “why can’t I have some of that?” And it’s at these times as pet owners we feel horrible and really want to give our cats some of our food. Well, it turns out that we can. In fact, here are 10 human foods that are safe for your cat to eat in moderation…. Fish – Canned fish, like tuna, is fine to share with your kitty in small portions. Though your feline will probably try to beg for a bigger bite, it is important to keep it small. This will prevent your cat from ingesting too much mercury, fatty acids and magnesium, which can cause health problems. Your furry friend’s diet should never solely consist of canned fish – via Cheese – Again, small is the key word when it comes to treating your kitty. Cheese is a good source of protein for felines, but since many cats are lactose intolerant, larger portions can cause digestive issues. Try feeding your friend cottage cheese or low-lactose cheeses before attempting anything richer. Most importantly, make sure to make dairy a special surprise, not an everyday treat – via Broccoli – A cat’s diet is dependent on protein, but sometimes they get a craving for veggies. If you notice your cat chomping on house plants, try feeding it a small portion of steamed broccoli. This could satisfy their desire for greens and keep them away from potentially toxic house plants . Veggies, like plants and grass, can also help your cat clear up digestive troubles. If you are interested in keeping you cat on a vegetarian diet full-time, you should talk it over with your vet before making the switch. – via Eggs – Give your cat a real breakfast by serving up some eggs. Scrambled and hard-boiled eggs are excellent for giving your feline a dose of protein. Just be careful not to use any butter or cooking spray during the preparation of the eggs that you are planning to hand over to your kitties. While there isn’t a consensus, most vets recommend staying away from raw eggs, since the same food-borne illnesses that affect humans can upset pets as well. – via Lean Deli Meats – Serving deli meats such as turkey, ham and chicken can be a delicious way to snazz up your cat’s meals. It is important to check any meat you plan on feeding your cat for preservatives, flavorings and other additives. Additionally, portions should be kept small and used only as a snack, not as your feline’s main source of food – via Melon – Like broccoli, melon could be a good bite to feed cats to stop them from chewing on house plants. Cantaloupe, honeydew and watermelon are fine for your cat to ingest in small quantities, as long as seeds are removed. The fruits can help you feline with digestive issues, but should be offered only occasionally because cats are carnivorous and their bodies can’t digest large amounts of plant matter. – via Spinach – Once again, if your cat is craving some green stuff, spinach can be a good way to go, especially when you are trying to help your pet relieve tummy troubles. Spinach should not be fed to cats with a history of urinary or kidney problems, since the calcium oxalates in the leaf can form crystals in the urinary tract. – via Beef – Though they may look cute and cuddly, cats are downright fericious carnivores. The feline body is built to thrive on meat. If you choose to feed your cat beef, you should give only small amounts and always avoid fatty portions. Kitties have obesity issues too, so make sure you keep their best interest in mind when giving them a bit of beef. Raw meat is also not the best choice due to food-borne illnesses. – via Bananas – If you’ve got a crazy cat, you can make it even more bananas by feeding them the actual fruit. Frozen bananas are a safe snack to feed your cat if it has that craving. As with the other non-meat products on this list, bananas should be fed to your kitty only in small portions, since cats are natural carnivores. – via Canned Baby Food – Meat-based baby foods can be a helpful way to encourage a cat with a health problem, like chronic kidney disease, to eat their needed food and medicine. Before buying, it is crucial to check the ingredients for onion and garlic powder, since both can be extremely harmful to cats. Feeding your cat baby food should not become a part of your cat’s normal diet. – via Pictures of When Classical Art is Infused with Cats 12 Steps to Training Your Cat to Walk with a Leash

title: Universal Parcel Tracking - Global Package Tracking
meta_description: Parcel Tracking Worldwide. Track Parcel in USA. Global postal tracking from eBay, AliExpress, ASOS, Shein, Amazon. Tracking packages from China, UK, Germany
headings: Track any package, freight and shipment
body_text: Having bought in the online store always want to know where the package is now? To save time checking the sites of postal companies manually, I created "Parcels" website and mobile apps which will automatically check every relevant postal, courier and logistics company website on your behalf. To find out where your parcel is, you need to know only tracking number of your package. What is a tracking number? Tracking number or tracking code is a unique number assigned to each package, which allows you to monitor the movement of the parcel between the countries or even inside one country. Tracking numbers can be international or traceable only within the country of the sender. The tracking number in the format of the Universal Postal Union looks like RA123456789CN, where the first 2 letters are the type of package and the last 2 letters are the code of the country of origin. Packages with such numbers can be tracked right until delivery. Other shipments can be delivered by courier, transport and logistics companies, and how tracking codes look can vary a lot: CTAFT0000160477YQ, UPAAB000000251682107, AEL10105033654UA, SGADN143797855. Such packages are not always trackable in the recipient's country, and you'll have to wait for notification from the Post Office or a call from a courier company. Tracking eBay order eBay sellers use multiple delivery and postal companies depending on item price and selected shipping method. Whether it's SpeedPAK, Pitney Bowes, Global Shipping Program, China Post, Winit, Yanwen or any other shipping company we've got your back. Read our extensive guide to tracking eBay orders . Delivery time Wondering how long it will take for your order to arrive to United States, UK, Canada, Australia or any other country? Wonder no more, with our state of the art delivery time estimation algorithm you'll always informed when to expect your parcel. We collect extensive delivery time statistics for each package going into each country and city and will use all of this data to get you most accurate estimated date of arrival. title: Fact Check: Email from Epstein files allegedly proves he's still alive. Here's the truth
meta_description: Online users claimed the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, who died in 2019, was referred to in the present tense in a 2023 email.
headings: Finding the truth about the email
body_text: Social media users speculated about an email in Jeffrey Epstein's federal case files, suggesting it proved he was still alive in 2023. The email in question, sent by Epstein's last girlfriend in 2014, requested "Amy's books" be sent to his private island, not in 2023 as claimed. The parsing error on a third-party website led to the misunderstanding that Epstein was alive in 2023, when in fact, the email was from 2014. The federal case files for Jeffrey Epstein, who died in 2019, feature a 2023 email requesting someone send "Amy's books" to his private island — indicating he's alive. In February and March 2026, social media users speculated about an email in Jeffrey Epstein's federal case files, essentially suggesting the message — which allegedly was sent in 2023 — proved the late sex offender was still alive. The sender, Karyna Shuliak, Epstein's last girlfriend prior to his 2019 death , requested "Amy's books" be sent for him to read on his private Caribbean island of Little St. James. For example, on March 10, X user @iamufohunter posted ( archived ) a screenshot of the email with the caption, "🚨 Epstien died in 2019 right ? Then what is this mail doing in 2023. 🤔" Other X users also shared screenshots of the email. In the screenshots, the date and time were displayed as Sept. 11, 2023, at 12:00 a.m. The subject line read, "FedEx for JE." The recipient's name did not appear, indicating the Justice Department redacted the name. The body of the email, containing grammatical errors and a reference to Little St. James, read, "Jeffrey wants you to fedex on of the Amy's books to LSJ. Can you give me a c=ll please, I'll explain Thank you!" In short, Shuliak did not send the email in 2023, nor did the message prove Epstein was still alive. As explained below, this rumor involved a simple parsing error, meaning software used by the owners of a third-party website inaccurately analyzed the text of the original email. Snopes contacted @iamufohunter for details of the research process used. In response, the user said only, "Its litrally written there in the email 2023." Jmail — the host of the third-party, Gmail-style Jmail.world website, which also contains archives of Epstein's case files — displayed ( archived ) an incorrectly parsed send date that did not appear in the original email hosted elsewhere. The Jmail website has no affiliation with the U.S. Justice Department's official Epstein files library . The original email showed only the day of the week as Monday and "Septem" for September, with the rest of the date and time redacted. A second email, which the Justice Department hosted in four files , displayed the date and time as Tuesday, Sept. 16, 2014, at 11:58 p.m. That email also featured the name Amy, with no last name, and the word "books." The email began, "I put your artifacto prints with the books from Amy." This information suggested Shuliak most likely sent her email on Monday, Sept. 15, 2014, nearly five years before Epstein's death. Jmail featured a handful of other inaccurately parsed emails or replies from Shuliak displaying erroneous send dates years after Epstein's 2019 death. For example, one Jmail-hosted email ( archived ) supposedly documented Shuliak emailing Epstein on Oct. 1, 2022, "Just saw this at the airport…" However, the original Justice Department-hosted email displayed the actual send date: Oct. 15, 2016. We sent an direct message on X to the owner or owners of Jmail to ask about the in-question email, including if they could comment on Jmail-hosted documents containing parsing errors. We will update this article if we receive further information. For further reading, we previously investigated another Epstein files email about a November 2013 party and "a dozen one-year-olds." "A Timeline of the Jeffrey Epstein Investigation and the Fight to Make the Government's Files Public." PBS News , The Associated Press, 6 Feb. 2026, https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/a-timeline-of-the-jeffrey-epstein-investigation-and-the-fight-to-make-the-governments-files-public . "Definition of PARSE." Merriam-Webster , 4 Mar. 2026, https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/parse . "Epstein Library." U.S. Justice Department , https://www.justice.gov/epstein/ . Sisak, Michael R., and Michael Balsamo. "New Details of Jeffrey Epstein's Death and the Frantic Aftermath Revealed in Records Obtained by AP." The Associated Press , 2 June 2023, https://apnews.com/article/jeffrey-epstein-jail-suicide-prison-death-8d194a756f2b429067f009a0c70f96c0 .

title: Full List of Medical Equipment Suppliers in Durban, KwaZulu Natal (2026)

title: Nuteisė už žiaurų elgesį: sumušė šunį Gausą ir gyvą užkasė
meta_description: Moteris sugyventinio augintiniui kastuvu sudavė ne mažiau kaip 12 smūgių į galvą ir kaklą. Atvykę policininkai išgirdo i
headings: Nuteisė už žiaurų elgesį: sumušė šunį Gausą ir gyvą užkasė
Ar pritariate patariamojo referendumo rengimui svarbiais valstybės klausimais?
Jūs jau balsavote. Galite peržiūrėti rezultatus.
body_text: Moteris sugyventinio augintiniui kastuvu sudavė ne mažiau kaip 12 smūgių į galvą ir kaklą. Atvykę policininkai išgirdo iš po žemių krūvos sklindantį cypimą. Šiaulių apylinkės teismas trečiadienį priėmė nuosprendį dviem Raseinių rajono gyventojams plataus atgarsio visuomenėje sulaukusioje byloje dėl sukrečiančio elgesio su šuniu. Kęstučiui Butkui skirta 1 metų laisvės apribojimas be intensyvios priežiūros, o Ilonai Ščerbinskienei - 60 parų arešto bausmė, kurios vykdymas atidėtas 1 metams be intensyvios priežiūros. Bausmės laikotarpiu vyras įpareigotas dalyvauti elgesio pataisos programoje ir uždrausta vartoti psichiką veikiančias medžiagas, o moteris privalo pradėti dirbti arba užsiregistruoti Užimtumo tarnyboje, dalyvauti elgesio pataisos programoje, nevartoti psichiką veikiančių medžiagų. Abu kaltinamieji turi dalyvauti alkoholizmo prevencijos programose. Be to, jiems solidariai skirta sumokėti priteistą 1753 eurų žalą už šuns gydymą gyvūnų globos organizacijai „Nuaras". Kaip rašė „Vakaro žinios", ikiteisminis tyrimas buvo pradėtas 2025 m. birželį, kai į Ariogalos miestelio sodyboje sukrečiantį vaizdą pamačiusi moteris iškvietė policijos pareigūnus. Atvykę jie išgirdo iš po žemių krūvos sklindantį cypimą - rastas stipriai sužalotas šuo. Buvo įtarta, kad žiauriai su gyvūnu elgėsi 68 metų minėtos sodybos šeimininkas ir 49 metų kaimynė. Šunį Gausą gelbėjo gyvūnų prieglauda, vėliau jam buvo surasta globėja. Šių metų vasarį šuo mirė dėl onkologinės ligos. Kaip pranešė teismas, nustatyta, kad nusikaltimo dieną K.Butkus ir I.Ščerbinskienė buvo apsvaigę nuo alkoholio. Vyras laikė kaimynės sugyventiniui priklausantį šunį už pavadėlio. I.Ščerbinskienė kastuvu sudavė Gausui ne mažiau kaip 12 smūgių į galvą ir kaklą. Po to kaltinamieji jį užkasė. Gyvūnui buvo padaryti daugybiniai sužalojimai, pavojingi jo gyvybei. Teismas konstatavo, kad kaltinamoji įvykdė smurtinį nusikaltimą prieš augintinį, o kita vertus, matyti, kad ji jau ne vienerius metus turi sveikatos sutrikimų, alkoholio vartojimo problemą ir tai turėjo įtakos nusikalstamos veikos padarymui. I.Ščerbinskienė ikiteisminio tyrimo metu pirmosios apklausos savo kaltę pripažino. Ji paaiškino, kad mušant šunį iš gretimo namo moteris ėmė kažką jai ir K.Butkui šaukti. Jie išsigando ir nuėjo už ūkinio pastato, kad jų nematytų. Ten I.Ščerbinskienė dar du kartus kastuvu sudavė šuniui per galvą. Ji galvojo, kad gyvūnas yra negyvas, todėl liepė K.Butkui užkasti. Antrosios apklausos metu ir teisme kaltinamoji kaltės nebepripažino ir paaiškino, kad neprisimena, kad būtų mušusi, kankinusi ir sužalojusi šunį. K.Butkus savo kaltę pripažino visiškai. Jis paaiškino, kad I.Ščerbinskienė atėjo pas jį į kiemą ir atsivedė šunį, sakė, kad gyvūnas jai yra nereikalingas ir reikia užmušti. Lietuva Lietuvos politika , Kitos Lietuvos žinios , Verslas , Nusikaltimai ir nelaimės , Žiniasklaida , "Snoro" nacionalizavimo kronika Pasaulis Pasaulio politika , Kitos pasaulio naujienos , Nusikaltimai ir nelaimės Nuomonės ir komentarai „Vakaro žinių" redakcijos skiltis , Išklausyk, Respublika , Skaitytojų laiškai Kultūra Interviu , Kultūros naujienos , Portretai Sportas Krepšinis , Futbolas , Kitos sporto šakos , Olimpinės žaidynės 2024 Pramogos Kinas, muzika, TV , Žvaigždės ir žmonės , Įdomybės , Naudingi patarimai , Grožis ir stilius , Maistas ir kulinarija , Horoskopai

title: 美国考虑在伊朗行动中部署数千名士兵——路透社 提供者 Investing.com
meta_description: 美国考虑在伊朗行动中部署数千名士兵——路透社
headings: 美国考虑在伊朗行动中部署数千名士兵——路透社
body_text: Investing.com- 据路透社周三报道,美国总统唐纳德·特朗普政府正在考虑在中东地区部署数千名士兵,作为其对伊朗行动的可能下一步措施。 路透社援引一名美国官员和三名知情人士的消息称,这些部署可能为特朗普提供更多选择,以便他权衡对伊朗的扩大行动。 一个关键关注点是确保石油运输船只安全通过霍尔木兹海峡。但据路透社报道,保护该海峡也可能涉及在伊朗领土上部署美军。 特朗普还在考虑对伊朗哈尔克岛进行地面入侵,此前上周的一次行动打击了该岛上的军事目标。哈尔克岛是伊朗约90%石油出口的枢纽。 路透社的这一报道发布之际,伊朗战争已进入连续第三周,几乎没有缓和的迹象。美国和以色列的打击据称杀死了许多伊朗高级官员,引发了伊斯兰共和国的激烈报复性打击。 在中东地区使用美国地面部队可能给特朗普带来重大政治风险,因为美国公众对伊朗行动的支持度较低,而且特朗普本人在竞选期间承诺避免让美国卷入更多战争。

Perfect for artists of all skill levels, this collection offers a diverse range of fairy inspirations, from the classic to the contemporary. Learn to capture the enchanting details that make each fairy unique—from delicate wings to intricate attire. Whether you’re looking to add a touch of fantasy to your portfolio or seeking a fun creative challenge, these drawing ideas will ignite your imagination and transport you to a realm of artistic enchantment. All artwork provided is original and can be used as a reference for your own drawings. As you close the chapter on these 25 charming fairy drawing ideas, you’ve not only filled your sketchbook with enchantment but also expanded your artistic skills. Each drawing has invited you into a mystical world, encouraging you to explore your creativity and bring these mythical beings to life. Continue to draw inspiration from the magic of fairies and let your imagination soar. Remember, the world of art is limitless—keep experimenting, keep creating, and keep discovering the magic within your own strokes and colors.

meta_description: Learn the fascinating origin of the Gica surname; its meaning & distribution. Unlock your family history in the largest database of last names.
body_text: Approximately 3,849 people bear this surname The meaning of this surname is not listed. The alternate forms: Gică (118) are calculated separately. Average Salary in: United States United States Political Affiliation in: United States United States View the highest/lowest earning families in The United States The last name Gica is found most frequently in The Philippines. It may also be found in the variant forms: Gică . For other potential spellings of this last name . The surname is the 119,417 th most frequently occurring family name at a global level, borne by around 1 in 1,893,361 people. This last name occurs predominantly in Asia, where 81 percent of Gica live; 80 percent live in Southeast Asia and 79 percent live in Fil-Southeast Asia. Gica is also the 142,627 th most frequently occurring first name on earth. It is borne by 2,649 people. The surname is most common in The Philippines, where it is borne by 3,078 people, or 1 in 32,891. In The Philippines Gica is mostly concentrated in: Central Visayas, where 35 percent live, Northern Mindanao, where 16 percent live and Davao Region, where 14 percent live. Aside from The Philippines this last name is found in 32 countries. It is also common in Romania, where 10 percent live and Brazil, where 4 percent live. In The United States those holding the Gica surname are 18.2% more likely to be registered with the Democratic Party than The US average, with 71.43% registered to vote for the party. Gica earn marginally less than the average income. In United States they earn 3.34% less than the national average, earning $41,708 USD per year. title: Sadhu Kokila Height, Age, Wife, Family, Biography & More » StarsUnfolded

headings: Some Lesser Known Facts About Sadhu Kokila
body_text: Bio/Wiki Real name Sahaya Sheelan Shadrach [1] Deccan Herald Other names Kokila Sadhu, Sadhu Maharaj Profession(s) Actor, Director, Producer, Musician, Composer, Comedian, Screenwriter, and Lyricist Physical Stats & More Height (approx.) in centimeters - 170 cm in meters - 1.70 m in feet & inches - 5’ 7” Eye Colour Black Hair Colour Black Career Debut Film: Sorry (1992) Composer: Shhh! (1993) Director: Raktha Kanneeru (2003) Producer: 90 (2011) Awards, Honours, Achievements • 2004–05, Karnataka State Film Awards in the category of Best Music Director for the Kannada film Rakshasa. • 2007–08, Karnataka State Film Awards in the category of Best Music Director for the Kannada film Inthi Ninna Preethiya. • 2011, South Indian International Movie Awards in the category Best Comedian for the Kannada film Hudugaru • 2012, South Indian International Movie Awards in the category Best Comedian for the Kannada film Yaare Koogadali • 2016: IIFA Utsavam in the category Best Performance In A Comic Role for the Kannada film Mr. and Mrs. Ramachari Personal Life Date of Birth 24 March 1966 (Thursday ) Age (as of 2025) 59 Years Birthplace Bangalore, Mysore State (now Karnataka), India Zodiac sign Aries Nationality Indian Hometown Bengaluru, Karnataka School St. Joseph's Indian High School, Bangalore Food Habit Non-Vegetarian [2] The New Indian Express Relationships & More Marital Status Married Affairs/Girlfriends Not Known Marriage Date Year, 1993 Family Wife/Spouse Celine Children Son - Suraag (Music director) and Srujan (Pianist and trained classical singer) Daughter - None Parents Father - Natesh (musician) Mother - Mangala (playback singer) Siblings Brother - Layendra Laya (Actor) & 1 more (Name Not Known) Sister - 1 (Name Not Known) Does Sadhu Kokila drink alcohol?: Yes [3] The New Indian Express Sadhu Kokila is an Indian actor who has predominantly worked in Kannada cinema. He grew up in a Christian family in Bengaluru. He was inspired by his musician father to follow music as a profession, and at an early age, he started learning the basics of western music. His first music teacher was S J Joseph, the mentor of many legendary musicians including Ilaiyaraaja . Sadhu Kokila is a sports enthusiast, and he played football for his school’s under-13 football team; however, due to his short stature, he could not get selected in the state team of Karnataka. In 1986, he worked as a rhythm player in the 1986 Doordarshan TV series Malgudi Days. His first work as a keyboard player was in the 1987 Kannada film “Ondu Muttina Kathe.” In 1993, he appeared in the Kannada film Shhh! as KungFu Kannan; he also gave the music in the film. After his performance in the film, filmmaker Upendra Rao gave him the stage name ‘Sadhu Kokila.’ In an interview, while talking about Upendra Rao, he said, For several of V Manohar’s compositions, I was the music conductor. Upendra had observed my skills. When he planned ‘Shhh!’, Manohar had his hands full so Upendra roped me in as the music director.” He added, Upendra would crack up by just seeing my face. He felt I should be on the screen. I was hesitant but he forced me to play the kung fu master’s character. My first scene was alongside the great Kashinath. Upendra is my godfather.” For several of V Manohar’s compositions, I was the music conductor. Upendra had observed my skills. When he planned ‘Shhh!’, Manohar had his hands full so Upendra roped me in as the music director.” Upendra would crack up by just seeing my face. He felt I should be on the screen. I was hesitant but he forced me to play the kung fu master’s character. My first scene was alongside the great Kashinath. Upendra is my godfather.” According to Sadhu Kokila, he learned comedy after watching the shows of Johnny Lever and Charlie Chaplin. He also appeared in a Kannada film alongside Johnny Lever, and since then, they are good friends with each other. Sadhu Kokila with Johnny Lever Later, Sadhu Kokila appeared in many Kannada films like Om (1995), O Mallige (1997), Bombat Halva (1999), Kiladi (2000), and Nagarahavu (2002). In 2003, he directed the Kannada film Raktha Kanneeru. Later, he directed many Kannada films including Rakshasa (2005), Suntaragaali (2006), Anatharu (2007), Mr. Theertha (2010), and Police Story 3 (2011). He has also composed the music for many Kannada films including Curfew (1994), Mangalya Sakshi (1995), Ibbara Naduve Muddina Aata (1996), Jackie Chan (1997), and Swalpa Adjust Madkolli (2000). He has also worked with many popular composers and music directors including M Ranga Rao and L Vaidyanathan. In 2009, he worked as a playback singer for the Kannada film Jaaji Mallige. He has also given many stage performances as a singer. In 2013, he played the role of Musthafa in the Kannada film Googly; his performance in the film bagged him the nomination for the Best Comedian at SIIMA Awards. In 2017, he established a film production house, Loop Entertainment, in Bengaluru. In 2020, he appeared as a judge in the singing reality TV show “Haadu Karnataka” alongside Indu Nagaraj, Raghu Dixit, V Harikrishna, Varijashree; the show was hosted by former Bigg Boss contestant Chandana Ananthakrishna. In 2021, he produced the Kannada TV show Gowripurada Gayalligalu; he also composed the title track of the show. Sadhu Kokila is considered one of the fastest keyboard players in India. During the second wave of the COVID-19 in India, his nephew also contracted the virus, and reportedly, Sadhu had to struggle a lot to arrange for the oxygen cylinder for his nephew.

title: Cherche une garde d’enfant à moëze 17780, annonce déposée par Enora
meta_description: Enora, cherche une garde d’enfant à Moëze 17780 ►►► Bonjour, Nous recherchons une baby-sitter de confiance pour garder nos deux enfants lors de notre week-end de mariage. Notre fille a...
headings: Entrez en contact avec des nounous 💬
body_text: Bonjour, Nous recherchons une baby-sitter de confiance pour garder nos deux enfants lors de notre week-end de mariage. Notre fille a 2 ans et notre fils a 3 mois. Notre fils restera sûrement avec nous jusque 19h mais l’idéal serait qu’il rentre à la maison pour dormir tranquillement dans sa chambre. Notre fille restera certainement plus longtemps avec nous et rentrera dormir vers 23h. La salle du mariage étant dans notre village d’habitation nous viendrons déposer les enfants. La baby-sitter serait donc à la maison au calme :) Nous aimerions trouvé quelqu’un de douce et avec un minimum d’expériences. Nous aimerions beaucoup pouvoir compter sur une baby-sitter que nos enfants apprécient pour s’offrir quelques soirées en couple de temps en temps en plus du mariage :) Rejoignez la communauté pour accéder aux profils vérifiés. Expliquez votre besoin, on alerte directement les nounous proches de chez vous. Consultez les profils et avis, discutez par messagerie, puis planifiez une rencontre sereine.

title: WTHR's Anne Marie Tiernon reveals a serious injury has kept her off air for several weeks
meta_description: The anchor revealed in a Facebook post Monday morning that her absence was due to serious injuries sustained during an accident over a month ago.
body_text: Dedicated viewers of WTHR-13 have likely noticed evening 13News anchor Anne Marie Tiernon's absence in recent weeks. Tiernon revealed in a Facebook post Monday morning that her absence was due to serious — but "not life-threatening" — injuries sustained during an accident in the woods over a month ago. She didn't share further details regarding the accident or the nature of her injuries. "My doctors recently determined another surgery is warranted," Tiernon wrote. "I’m now preparing for that and focusing on healing and recovery." Join our new holiday tradition: IndyStar needs your help to map light displays across Indianapolis The Emmy award-winning anchor of the 5 and 6 p.m. weekday newscasts has worked in Indianapolis news for decades, according to her staff bio on the WTHR website. She's a familiar face for anyone who watches the NBC affiliate for coverage of the Olympics — Tiernon traveled to cover Hoosier athletes competing in the 2016 games in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil; the 2014 games in Sochi, Russia; the 2012 summer games in London, England; the 2010 games in Vancouver, Canada; and the 2008 games in Beijing, China. In her post, she thanked viewers, fans and her colleagues at WTHR for their well-wishes and support. "Overall, I trust in God’s plan, am grateful I survived, and know every storm runs out of rain," she wrote. "For now, I’m off to make my health a priority." Contact IndyStar newsroom development director Holly Hays at holly.hays@indystar.com. Follow her on Twitter: @hollyvhays . This article originally appeared on Indianapolis Star: Anne Marie Tiernon shares update on absence from WTHR-13 newscasts

headings: Colton Biss was our sales rep, and he could not have been
The Jeep is in excellent condition excellent milea
body_text: *Estimated payments are calculated by Cars.com and are for informational purposes only. We’ve estimated your taxes based on your provided ZIP code. These two factors can greatly change your monthly payment. Colton Biss was our sales rep, and he could not have been better! So friendly, professional, and competent. He was very knowledgeable about the car I ended up buying, and thorough as far as going thru the car’s features. He even made sure that I had his personal cell phone if I have follow-up questions about the car. I’d highly recommend Colton and Corwin dealership in Nampa! Seems to be a good deal and the mileage is good looking forward to test driving the Jeep the overall car is excellent by far Rob has helped us with two cars this year. He listens to the customer and their needs. When you visit this dealership, ask for Rob! Nick, from the finance department, helped us this time. He was awesome too! However, I can?t say to ask for Nick because both of their finance people at this location are amazing. Prepared, quick working, and doesn?t dwell on paperwork or trying to sell more. Long story short, you?re in good hands. My jeep liberty 2012 is parked. It could only do 25km/hr on the road. Gas pedal not working anymore. Just 2616 miles. I need a help to cruise this car I’ve dealt with Ron S for years. We've bought at least 14 vehicles from Page Honda and Ron S has always been our sales person. I always feel like Ron is working in our best interest to get me the best deal. Thanks Ron and Page Honda. My parents bought this new back in 2009 and after a car accident that totaled my first truck (01 Chevy S10), my mom gave me this and I love every bit of it!! I’ve had it in my possession about 5 years now and it’s such a fun vehicle. The 4WD is amazing and it’s capabilities are really impressive! With the right driving on a trail, lifted truck owners look at me sometimes and say “Ain’t no way a Liberty did that!”. It’s a solid SUV. I use mine as both a daily and a weekend warrior (not too hard because I have to drive it home) and it’s a great vehicle all in all! Gas mileage could be a little better but it’s a V6 Jeep, what do you expect. I highly recommend this vehicle to anyone who wants to get into Overlanding or anyone who just wants a fun Jeep for their buck! Keep up with maintenance (as you should with every vehicle) and it’ll treat you right! The selections only let me choose “New” or “Used” but I’d say both because it was new to my Mom and Dad but used obviously when I got it. Along with “Primary Use”, I use it to both commute to work and have some fun but it would only let me choose one. Go see trey at performance auto they’ll get you right !! Thank you for such a great deal! Just sold my 2008 liberty limited. 162K no problems 12 years in NJ and 5 years in rochester NY. Buyer had friend mechanic check it out told buyer good buy, good condition. Never garage kept. No dirt off road but plenty of round town and 300 mile trips. Daughter loved it as teenager also. Fun for many years will miss it. I had a great experience buying my Tacoma from Immaculate. Thanks for your help Zach I love my Liberty! I was inspired to buy mine (used) after temporarily inheriting my Dad’s 2008 Liberty. I’ll comment on the 2008, then the 2012. 2008: Bought brand new out of the showroom; it was an everyday driver and has been very reliable. No problems with the engine or transmission, 4-wheel drive works great. Some very minor issues with the power window switch, it stopped working, but easy fix. The AC takes a little while to cool the interior down, but it hasn’t been serviced or recharged. This Liberty just turned 300,000 and is still running well! 2012 bought used with 65,000. This Liberty is also a daily driver. The engine and Transmission are fine, no issues. The 4-wheel drive system has been good as well. Used it several times on snowy unplowed Wisconsin roads and it has been solid. Holds skis, snowboards really well. Biggest service item will be new tires. One minor issue with the AM/FM radio, the receiver chip in the radio went bad, so no AM/FM. But the CD and Jack work fine. This Liberty just crossed 125,000 and is running like a champ. Final note: I hated the look and everything about the Liberty when my Dad bought his. Then I drove it. Complete game changer; now I absolutely love it! What an amazing dealership. The staff is so welcoming and make u feel right at home. The vehicles are all great priced and run great. I've bought many cars here over the years and always have been 100% satisfied. Mike the owner is a great man. He makes sure his customers are taken care of. Go in and check the place out for yourself and u will see all I say is true. Im glad to know there is a dealership out there like this one it's makes the car buying experience so much better. It takes the pressure off my family and I to know we can trust them to take care of us all the way through the process. Thank u KC Car Emporium for all u do. May God bless you and keep doing what your doing your making a world of difference to myself and I'm sure to alot of others. Thank you again for all u do it means alot to me and my family. Bought this vehicle new for my daughter. Regular service and maintenance with no major issues. Vehicle currently has 167,000 and still going strong. Hope for least another 100,000. The work was done to my satisfaction, and the shuttle drivers to and from my home were very personable, professional, kind and respectful of my schedule. Bought used in 2015 with 42k miles and as of today 10/27/22 almost 200,000 miles on the beast. I have lucked out with having NO engine/transmission issues. Love the boxy style and there is so much room that I never feel stressed about having enough room for all my stuff. Will keep the beast till it rusts apart!! 2nd jeep for me, 95 wrangler rio grande package bought brand new. Totally recommended! Great customer service and vehicles in great conditions! Thank you so much guys :) I love ❤ mine.. it is great to drive!! Some mechanics issues.. Overall it's Very reliable.. I'm interested in how 🤔 many miles, with good quality upkeep there's are.. Best place to shop if your looking for a new auto! Elmer Pierce and Harold Sigle were amazing and so friendly made my car buying experience a great one!!! They were so friendly and worked hard to find me just what I was looking for in a car!! Thanks guys!! I highly recommend you to everyone!!! Before owning this car I've always made fun of the way it looks until realizing it may not be your ideal Jeep, but it does help with aerodynamics of the car. For a SUV with a V6 engine I expected the gas intake to cost more than it really does but to my surprise the gas cost is no issue for me and I drive it almost every day. As the car is well built it is meant to be a car that is supposed to be taken care of like a car. There's some issues that will occur with the 04 Jeep Liberty but those fixes don't burn holes in your pockets. After 20 years this car is still capable of trail riding and everyday usage with comfort and confidence. The interior can be turned into a quiet a spacious area for the size it appears to be form the exterior. I heavily recommend this as a first car as it was my first. Not to mention it is a pretty hard shell to crack when it comes to wrecks or bumps. To think owning this car Brand New while it runs perfectly fine for its two decades of life. To anyone reading this that is thinking of buying this from a Dealer/FBMP/Family or Friend, I'm sure you won't regret it. This is the last year the Liberty was made by Jeep! The 4wheel drive is the perfect all weather vehicle, And the best Tow vehicle ever made! We love our Jeep Liberty and sorry they quit making the Liberty! Great service! Even the out of state paperwork was easy. Honest and trustworthy owner. I’m satisfied. I only have this not quite a month now and it is a nice jeep, I think a bit of a gas guzzler somewhat but overall so far i'm very happy, its a used car with low miles so i'm not expecting perfection ... we will see but so far i like the look i've done some things to improve its look as well and overall 3.5 stars i guess is best to say. Brought with 76k miles. Great guys . Two brothers run the place . Drive the , left a deposit and picked the car up the next day . Bought this car at 1 year old and sold it in 2019. Fixed very little on it and nothing very expensive. Very reliable. I did not like that it could not be driven on the highway in the snow as it could only be used in 4x4 at low speeds. When not in 4x4, it was not a good snow car. Fine around town. Great on the beach. Everything worked fine when I sold it at 15 years old. Bought a Subaru for the AWD. I purchased a Solstice for my wife, the car was in great shape and had low miles, they didn't have any objections to me going to get it checked out. Very nice and helpful, we definitely recommend them to anyone wanting to purchase a vehicle This is a easy car to drive. Loved it for getting groceries. Slide sacks in ,. No bending over. Plenty of leg room front and back. Slipt back seats that will fold down for extra cargo space. Joe, the salesman was great. Very helpful,informative and obliging. Unfortunately, the vehicle was not as impressive. Our sakes person was Daisy Fuentes, she did an excellent job, The car that I purchased was ready for a test drive when we first arrived. The car looked and performed very well. My trade in was evaluated and after some conversation, a very reasonable offer was tendered which I accepted. When we arrived, I seriously did not think that I would buy the car, but Daisy and the others involved made a fair offer and arranged financing, this all occurred in less than 2 hours. I am most pleased with the way that I was treated, the professionalism of the personnel, and their preparedness for my visit. 2nd owner; made it to 258,000 miles..drove all over country and great in snow. Fast, handle well and survived a rollover (me)! Parking. was a breeze. Loved that Jeep! 56 years in Mt. Penn!!! Personal service! Guaranteed cars and trucks!! As the Owner I will do my best to satisfy customers! My son looked at cars and was not hassled. People answered questions for him. He bought a 2014 Kia FORTE 5 EX hatchback. Blue, ha is happy! My sister in-law gave me this car in 2021 for free because she got a new car. I have no idea how it was maintained before owning it myself. It had around 150k miles on it. I have driven it everyday since and it now has 200k miles. It has a significant amount of rust, so much so that part of the kick plate under the backseat passenger door fell off. I've had to replace brake pads, spark plugs, disc rotor, A/C compressor, muffler, water pump, tie rod, ball joint, thermostat, brake hose, calipers. Check engine light was on for O2 sensor, I had them both replaced and the check engine light is still on for the O2 sensor so it might be a loose wire. I've spent around $4,000 fixing it up in the last 3 years, being a used car and the okay condition it is now I would say it is reliable. I love it, I want to continue maintaining it until it dies. title: Coach believes Sabres prospect Eric Locke has Brett Hull-like shot - Buffalo Hockey Beat
meta_description: Like Brett Like Hull, Sabres prospect Eric Locke’s also listed at an undersized 5-foot-10.
body_text: BUFFALO – Sabres prospect Eric Locke possesses a “rocket” shot so quick and lethal it reminds his coach of a former NHL teammate. Which one? The greatest goal scorer Greg Gilbert ever played with. “Brett Hull, to be honest with you,” Gilbert, Locke’s junior coach with the Saginaw Spirit, said by phone earlier this week. “I mean, Hully was special, there’s no question about it. But Eric’s got that potential. He’s got that quick release. He’s low to the ice. He goes through the shot. He scored goals for us this year from just inside the blue line over the goalie’s glove. The goalies didn’t move.” Said Sabres prospect Justin Kea, Locke’s center much of last season: “He can shoot like I’ve never seen before.” Like Hull, Locke’s also listed at an undersized 5-foot-10. For now, the similarities end there. Hull, Gilbert’s teammate in St. Louis in the mid-1990s, scored 844 total NHL goals, including a notable one 14 years ago in Buffalo. For Locke, the 189 th pick on June 30 and the last of the Sabres’ 11 selections, just making the NHL would be notable. Very few seventh-rounders crack the big leagues. In the past 20 years, only two – Paul Gaustad and Nathan Paetsch – have played regularly in Buffalo. The Sabres couldn’t pass on Locke, whose 44 goals and 97 points in 68 games both ranked fourth in the Ontario Hockey League last season. In addition his scoring prowess, the 19-year-old winger has “got some grit to his game,” amateur scouting director Kevin Devine said. “With those kind of numbers, the coach says, ‘You never know, he might find a way,’” Devine said. “At that point, we hadn’t (drafted) any high offensive players. So we took a shot at him in the seventh round.” Locke, an invitee to the Sabres’ pre-draft combine, never received an opportunity in 2012. Despite compiling 20 goals and 40 points as he battled injuries, teams passed on drafting him. Locke said he was “beat up” and “just never really found a groove” in 2011-12, which he split between the Barrie Colts and Saginaw. “Last year was kind of the complete opposite,” Locke said last week following a session of development camp inside the First Niagara Center. “I had a great year, great teammates, great coaching.” Of course, size could’ve been an issue for NHL clubs, but Gilbert believes no one should worry. “He’s built like a pit bull. He’s built like a boxer,” Gilbert said about the 184-pound Locke. “He’s a strong kid. He’s a powerful kid.” Locke added: “Some people think size matters. But it’s really about how hard you work.” Locke’s work ethic helped elevate him to another level, Gilbert said. He sees similarities between Locke and Ottawa’s Cory Conacher and Montreal’s Brendan Gallagher, two undersized forwards among the NHL’s top rookies last season. “They’re not big players, but they compete their backsides off,” Gilbert said. “That’s what Eric does. He just competes, and he’s got skill. He’s not afraid to go in the tough areas to score the goals and finish checks. (It) comes into one package. It might not be a 6-foot-5 package, but it’s in a package, and that’s what people want.” “Not only am I scoring from shots and whatnot, but I’m getting in the crease and getting those gritty goals as well,” he said. Locke wasn’t the only gritty presence scoring for Saginaw, though. Kea, the 73 rd pick in 2012, compiled 22 goals and 48 points, a huge 19-score, 34-point improvement. What happened? The 19-year-old began effectively utilizing his size. Before he moved up to the OHL, Kea could get away with things on the ice because he was bigger than the competition. “You start playing against bigger, stronger, older guys, you’re not able to do that,” Gilbert said. “So you got to sort things out and how you have to play and what’s the best style for him to play.” Gilbert said Kea’s “not going to be a Mario Lemieux.” “He’s going to be a straight-ahead power centerman,” he said. “He’s got the ability to score goals. I think that’s going to come. He’s got to keep working on skills.” Kea also stopped fretting about his offense. “I think my first couple years I was just worrying too much about points, and then my third year last year, I just kind of started playing, having fun and enjoying the game, working a little bit harder and trying to play more of a power forward role,” Kea said. “The points just came along fast. I felt something work for me. I just kept doing it.” Kea will return to Saginaw next season. Right now, the Sabres plan to sign Locke and put him in Rochester. He feels he’s ready for the AHL. “I showed I could dominate the OHL level,” Locke said. “I’m just looking forward to the opportunity.”
